---

### Assistant *(2026-05-13T16:48:59.133Z)*

So the full collision picture is:

- **PROV-O nodes**: want `urn:ddbedm:{id}` — provider-ddb-id, dataset-id, mapping-version are all short opaque strings
- **Agent/Place/Concept/TimeSpan bare IDs**: also `urn:ddbedm:{32-char-DDB-ID}` — same namespace, same format

A provider-ddb-id like `CJY7MSLPOPB7FTPC7JM5K2GGM5PBGLYI` is a 32-char DDB ID — identical format to an agent or concept bare ID. Without a discriminator, `urn:ddbedm:CJY7MSLPOPB7FTPC7JM5K2GGM5PBGLYI` is ambiguous.

The short-prefix approach from Option A still solves this cleanly, **and the prefixes can't collide with bare DDB IDs** because DDB IDs are 32-char alphanumeric — they never start with `provider:`, `dataset:`, or `xslt:`:

```
urn:ddbedm:provider:CJY7MSLPOPB7FTPC7JM5K2GGM5PBGLYI  ← PROV-O Provider Agent
urn:ddbedm:dataset:76409877634279609sQOu                 ← PROV-O Dataset
urn:ddbedm:xslt:6.18                                     ← PROV-O XSLT SoftwareAgent
urn:ddbedm:CJY7MSLPOPB7FTPC7JM5K2GGM5PBGLYI            ← bare-ID Agent/Place/Concept/TimeSpan
```

No collision possible: the colon after the type word is the discriminator, and DDB IDs never contain colons. `mapping-version` values like `6.18` also contain a dot, not a colon, so no clash with `xslt:6.18`.

One remaining edge case: **dataset-id** (`76409877634279609sQOu`) — is this format distinct from bare DDB entity IDs? Bare entity IDs are always exactly 32 uppercase alphanumeric chars. Dataset IDs appear to be variable-length mixed-case strings. Let me verify:

---

### Assistant *(2026-05-13T16:54:03.390Z)*

Good data. `dataset-id` is variable-length (19–40 chars) mixed case — not 32-char uppercase like DDB entity IDs, but the longer 40-char hex ones (`05729bee…`) could theoretically overlap. `provider-ddb-id` is exactly 32-char uppercase — **identical format to bare entity IDs**, confirmed collision without a prefix.

Short-prefix approach is validated and safe:

| URI | Key |
|---|---|
| `urn:ddbedm:provider:{provider-ddb-id}` | 32-char DDB ID — would collide bare |
| `urn:ddbedm:dataset:{dataset-id}` | variable-length — 40-char hex could collide bare |
| `urn:ddbedm:xslt:{mapping-version}` | `6.18` — safe bare but prefix keeps it consistent |
| `urn:ddbedm:{bare-DDB-id}` | Agent/Place/Concept/TimeSpan — no prefix needed |

The colon after the type word is the unambiguous discriminator since DDB IDs are purely alphanumeric. No bare entity ID can ever start with `provider:`, `dataset:`, or `xslt:`.

Shall I update the code and notes to use this scheme — replacing the current property-chain URNs (`urn:ddbedm:properties:dataset-id:…`, etc.) with the short-prefix form?

/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/notes/ddbedm-prov-o-plan.md:56:**Shared node deduplication**: The four shared nodes (Dataset, XSLT, DDB Agent, Provider) have stable URIs that recur across records and sector files. Their descriptive triples are emitted only on first encounter per run via an `emitted: dict[str, str]` guard in `emit_prov_triples`. With `--entities-db`, the dict is persisted to DuckDB across runs; without it, deduplication is within-run only. The per-CHO linking triples (`prov:wasDerivedFrom`, `prov:wasAttributedTo`, `prov:generatedAtTime`, `dcterms:hasVersion`, `dcterms:references`) are always emitted. The XSLT URI is derived from the version number (`urn:ddbedm:properties:mapping-version:{ver}`), so the version number is the effective lookup key at the code level.

DDB bare IDs are 32-char alphanumeric — prov URNs use typed prefixes with colons\n        # so urn:ddbedm:provider:X and urn:ddbedm:X are distinct\n        lines = emit_prov_triples(self._RECORD, self._CHO_URI, self._GRAPH)\n        prov_subjects = {nq.split(\" \", 1)[0] for nq in lines\n                         if nq.startswith(\"<urn:ddbedm:\")}\n        for s in prov_subjects:\n            # every prov URN must carry a type segment (provider:, dataset:, xslt:)\n            inner = s[len(\"<urn:ddbedm:\"):-1]\n            assert \":\" in inner, f\"prov URN missing type prefix: {s}\""
}

### User *(2026-05-13T17:01:29.483Z)*

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The conversation covers a multi-part feature addition to the goethe-faust DDB-EDM transform scripts (`/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/scripts/transform/`):
   - **Part 1**: Identify which part of the transform scripts emits PROV-O provenance triples
   - **Part 2**: Add cross-run shared-entity deduplication: avoid re-emitting identical descriptive triples for shared named entities (PROV-O nodes + edm:Agent/Place/Concept/TimeSpan) across multiple sector-file runs
   - **Part 3**: Use a persistent DuckDB table (`emitted_entities`) for cross-run state (user runs the script on s1–s7 sector files as separate OS processes)
   - **Part 4**: Change the PROV-O URN scheme from verbose property-chain form (`urn:ddbedm:properties:dataset-id:…`) to a short-prefix form (`urn:ddbedm:dataset:…`, `urn:ddbedm:xslt:…`, `urn:ddbedm:provider:…`) to prevent collision with bare entity IDs that use `urn:ddbedm:{id}` (no type prefix)
   - **Throughout**: Add tests and update all relevant notes files

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - PROV-O Layer 1 (Without-Activity pattern) — 5 node types per CHO record
   - Cross-run deduplication via DuckDB persistent table
   - URN collision prevention with typed short prefixes — DDB bare IDs are 32-char alphanumeric (no colons), so `urn:ddbedm:provider:X` vs `urn:ddbedm:X` are unambiguous
   - `dict[str, str]` (`uri → entity_type`) used as both O(1) lookup and write-back source
   - `INSERT OR IGNORE` for idempotent DuckDB writes
   - `None`-safe fallback: `emitted=None` → always emit (backward-compatible for tests)
   - Per-CHO linking triples always emitted; only shared-node descriptive triples are deduplicated
   - Named graph: `https://gemea.ise.fiz-karlsruhe.de/graph/prov`

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`scripts/transform/emitters.py`** — Core emitter file with all PROV-O triple generation
     - Added `_DEDUP_ENTITY_TYPES` dict after imports:
       ```python
       _DEDUP_ENTITY_TYPES: dict[str, str] = {
           "Agent":    "edm_agent",
           "Place":    "edm_place",
           "Concept":  "skos_concept",
           "TimeSpan": "edm_timespan",
       }
       ```
     - Updated `emit_ddbedm_triples` signature: added `emitted: dict[str, str] | None = None`
     - Added dedup check in entity loop (after computing `subj_uri`):
       ```python
       if emitted is not None and entity_type in _DEDUP_ENTITY_TYPES:
           if subj_uri in emitted:
               continue
           emitted[subj_uri] = _DEDUP_ENTITY_TYPES[entity_type]
       ```
     - Updated `emit_prov_triples` signature: added `emitted: dict[str, str] | None = None`
     - Changed URN construction from verbose to short-prefix form:
       ```python
       ds_uri   = f"urn:ddbedm:dataset:{dataset_id}"       if dataset_id      else ""
       xslt_uri = f"urn:ddbedm:xslt:{map_ver}"             if map_ver         else ""
       prov_uri = f"urn:ddbedm:provider:{provider_ddb_id}" if provider_ddb_id else ""
       ```
     - Guarded 4 shared-node blocks (Dataset, XSLT, DDB Agent, Provider):
       ```python
       # Dataset example:
       if ds_uri and (emitted is None or ds_uri not in emitted):
           if emitted is not None:
               emitted[ds_uri] = "prov_dataset"
           ds_nt = f"<{ds_uri}>"
           # ... emit triples ...
       
       # DDB Agent (fixed URI):
       if emitted is None or DDB_BASE not in emitted:
           if emitted is not None:
               emitted[DDB_BASE] = "prov_ddb"
           ddb_nt = f"<{DDB_BASE}>"
           # ... emit triples ...
       ```

   - **`scripts/transform/transform.py`** — Per-record orchestration
     - Added `emitted_entities: dict[str, str] | None = None` to `transform_record` signature
     - Updated both emitter calls:
       ```python
       ddbedm_lines, ddbedm_classes, ddbedm_preds, ddbedm_sani = emit_ddbedm_triples(
           rdf, GRAPH_DDBEDM, lang_coll, emitted=emitted_entities,
       )
       streams["prov"] = emit_prov_triples(record, ddb_uri, GRAPH_PROV, emitted=emitted_entities)
       ```

   - **`scripts/transform/__main__.py`** — CLI entry point
     - Added `--entities-db` CLI arg in io group:
       ```python
       io.add_argument("--entities-db", type=Path, default=None, dest="entities_db",
                       help="Shared cross-run entity-dedup DuckDB; created if absent...")
       ```
     - Added entities DuckDB setup before main loop:
       ```python
       emitted_entities: dict[str, str] = {}
       entities_conn = None
       if args.entities_db:
           try:
               import duckdb as _ddb_ent
               entities_conn = _ddb_ent.connect(str(args.entities_db))
               entities_conn.execute("""
                   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS emitted_entities (
                       uri         VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
                       entity_type VARCHAR NOT NULL
                   )
               """)
               rows = entities_conn.execute(
                   "SELECT uri, entity_type FROM emitted_entities"
               ).fetchall()
               emitted_entities = {uri: etype for uri, etype in rows}
               log.info("Loaded %d emitted entities from %s", len(emitted_entities), args.entities_db)
           except ImportError:
               log.warning("duckdb not available — --entities-db ignored")
           except Exception as exc:
               log.warning("Could not open --entities-db %s: %s", args.entities_db, exc)
       ```
     - Updated `transform_record` call: `emitted_entities=emitted_entities`
     - Added write-back after loop (before `conn.close()`):
       ```python
       if entities_conn is not None:
           entities_conn.executemany(
               "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO emitted_entities VALUES (?, ?)",
               list(emitted_entities.items()),
           )
           entities_conn.close()
           log.info("Wrote %d entities to %s", len(emitted_entities), args.entities_db)
       ```

   - **`scripts/transform/tests/test_transform.py`** — Test file
     - Added `emit_prov_triples` to imports from `transform.emitters`
     - Added `DDB_BASE` to imports from `transform.constants`
     - Added `TestEmitProvTriplesDedup` class (11 tests including 4 URN-format tests):
       - `_RECORD` with `properties.mapping-version: "6.18"`, `properties.dataset-id: "testdataset123"`, `provider-info.provider-ddb-id: "PROVIDERID00000000000000000000000"`
       - `_XSLT_URI = "urn:ddbedm:xslt:6.18"`
       - `_DS_URI = "urn:ddbedm:dataset:testdataset123"`
       - `_PROV_URI = "urn:ddbedm:provider:PROVIDERID00000000000000000000000"`
       - Tests: `test_emitted_none_emits_all`, `test_emitted_dict_populated`, `test_xslt_skipped_when_emitted`, `test_ddb_agent_skipped_when_emitted`, `test_provider_skipped_when_emitted`, `test_dataset_skipped_when_emitted`, `test_cho_linking_always_emitted`
       - URN format tests: `test_xslt_uri_short_prefix`, `test_dataset_uri_short_prefix`, `test_provider_uri_short_prefix`, `test_prov_uris_no_collision_with_entity_bare_id`
     - Added `TestEmitDdbedmTriplesDedup` class (8 tests):
       - `test_agent_skipped_when_emitted`, `test_place_skipped_when_emitted`, `test_concept_skipped_when_emitted`, `test_timespan_skipped_when_emitted`, `test_provided_cho_never_skipped`, `test_emitted_none_emits_all`, `test_second_record_shares_agent`, `test_emitted_dict_populated_with_type`
     - All 159 tests pass

   - **`notes/ddbedm-prov-o-plan.md`** — PROV-O design document
     - Updated URN patterns in node table (§2.1), dedup note, section headers §2.2.3/4/6, and Turtle example (§2.3)
     - All occurrences of old URN patterns replaced via `replace_all`
     - **Pending**: URN convention paragraph still describes old property-chain form

   - **`notes/transform-adr.md`** — Transform architecture decisions
     - Updated URI scheme table, Turtle pattern, and field mapping section headers
     - All occurrences replaced via `replace_all`

   - **`notes/transform-implementation-plan.md`** — Implementation plan
     - Updated node URI table
     - All occurrences replaced via `replace_all`

   - **`notes/transform-script-adr.md`** — Script-level implementation decisions
     - Added Decision 29 (DuckDB-backed cross-run shared-entity dedup)
     - **Pending**: D27 line 597 has stale entity URI scheme (`urn:ddbedm:<ClassName>:<id>` — note says this but code already uses `urn:ddbedm:<id>` without class name)
     - **Pending**: Decision 29 should document the new URN format

   - **`notes/transform-implementation-actual.md`** — As-built documentation
     - Added §11 "Shared-entity deduplication (2026-05-13)" covering mechanism, entity types, DuckDB schema, new CLI flag, code changes, test additions
     - **Pending**: Should add note about URN scheme change

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **`replace_all` then targeted Edit conflict**: After using `replace_all` to replace `urn:ddbedm:properties:dataset-id:` in `ddbedm-prov-o-plan.md`, attempted to also run a targeted edit on the section header `#### 2.2.3 Dataset (urn:ddbedm:properties:dataset-id:<value>)` — got "String to replace not found" because the `replace_all` had already changed it. The section headers are correct (already updated by `replace_all`).
   - **Edit tool old_string indentation mismatch**: First attempt at adding dedup check in `emit_ddbedm_triples` used 8-space indentation (from file display) but actual code used different indentation. Fixed by re-reading the file to get exact indentation (12 spaces) and using `            subj_uri = ...`.

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Cross-run deduplication**: Solved by a persistent DuckDB table (`emitted_entities`) loaded at startup into an in-memory `dict[str, str]`, used for O(1) lookups during processing, written back with `INSERT OR IGNORE` after the loop.
   - **URN collision prevention**: Solved by using short typed prefixes (`provider:`, `dataset:`, `xslt:`) for PROV-O nodes, while bare entity IDs (Agent/Place/Concept/TimeSpan) use `urn:ddbedm:{id}` without prefix. DDB IDs are purely alphanumeric (no colons), so the colon in the type prefix is an unambiguous discriminator.
   - **Backward compatibility**: `emitted=None` default means all callers (tests, other code) that don't pass the dict get the original always-emit behavior.

All other entity types have no canonical HTTP URI, so the `urn:ddbedm:` scheme encodes both the namespace and the entity class, making the source unambiguous without requiring a dereferenceable endpoint. ### User *(2026-05-13T17:01:39.318Z)*

**Tool result**
```
585	
586	---
587	
588	## Decision 27: Bare-ID URI minting for malformed `about` values
589	
590	**Context**: Some `edm.RDF.*.about` values (and `.resource` cross-references) contain only the bare 32-character DDB internal ID rather than a full HTTP URI. `px.NamedNode()` rejects bare strings — minting is required before any triple can be emitted.
591	
592	**Decision**: Apply the same scheme used in `export_ddb.py §4.3`:
593	
594	| Entity type | Minting rule | Example |
595	|---|---|---|
596	| `ProvidedCHO` | `http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/<id>` | `…/item/225LOCJZSZLTA4DCUBFIHG72SPN7JTQZ` |
597	| All others (`Aggregation`, `Agent`, `Event`, `Place`, `WebResource`, …) | `urn:ddbedm:<ClassName>:<id>` | `urn:ddbedm:Agent:O5XUSBA7IPKSXYUTN6EQNWK62BQRF7GN` |
598	
599	Detection: value does not start with `http` or `urn`.
600	
601	For `.resource` cross-references, the target entity type is resolved from a per-record lookup of all `about` values built before traversal begins.
602	
603	**Rationale**: `ProvidedCHO` bare IDs are DDB item identifiers — the canonical DDB item URI scheme (`…/item/<id>`) is the correct dereferenceable form and aligns with the `owl:sameAs` link emitted in the mocho graph. All other entity types have no canonical HTTP URI, so the `urn:ddbedm:` scheme encodes both the namespace and the entity class, making the source unambiguous without requiring a dereferenceable endpoint. Consistent with `export-s2-plan.md §4.3`.
604	
605	---
606	
607	## Decision 28: Post-processing NQ split → per-graph NT files
608	
609	**Decision**: The transform emits `.nq` output unchanged (D22). Immediately after the transform, a post-processing step (`scripts/split_nq.py`) splits each `.nq` file into one `.nt` file per named graph. The `.nt` files are the working intermediates for sanitization, validation, and debugging. NQ wrapping is deferred to QLever load time.
610	
611	File naming: the output `.nt` slug matches the graph name (e.g. `ddbedm.nt`, `mocho.nt`, `prov.nt`); the load-time wrapper derives the full graph IRI mechanically (`…/graph/<slug>`).
612	
613	**Rationale**:
614	1. **NT is simpler to sanitize**: no graph column; grep/awk/sed operate directly on `<subject> <predicate> <object> .` lines without stripping the fourth element first.
615	2. **Late-binding graph IRI**: renaming a named graph (e.g. schema-breaking release, IRI migration) requires changing only the load-time wrapper — not the `.nt` files.
616	3. **No generator change**: the transform already routes triples to per-graph output streams (D20, D22). Post-processing the `.nq` output is a small script; the generator is not touched.
617	
618	**Post-processing script** (`scripts/split_nq.py`):
619	
620	```python
621	from collections import defaultdict
622	from pathlib import Path
623	
624	def split_nq(nq_path: Path, out_dir: Path):
625	    graphs: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)
626	    with open(nq_path) as f:
627	        for line in f:
628	            parts = line.rstrip(" .\n").rsplit(" ", 1)
629	            graphs[parts[1]].append(parts[0] + " .\n")
630	    for graph_iri, triples in graphs.items():
631	        slug = graph_iri.strip("<>").split("/")[-1]
632	        (out_dir / f"{slug}.nt").write_text("".join(triples))
633	```
634	
635	**Amends D22** on the file format question only: D22 governs the generator (NQ output, graph IRI on every emitted line). This decision governs what happens to the `.nq` files after generation; D22 remains in effect for the transform itself.
636	
637	---
638	
639	## Decision 29: DuckDB-backed cross-run shared-entity deduplication
640	
641	**Decision**: Descriptive triples for shared named entities are emitted at most once per URI across all transform runs, tracked via a persistent DuckDB table (`emitted_entities`).
642	
643	**Entity types deduplicated**:
644	
645	| entity_type value | Source | Scope of sharing |
646	|---|---|---|
647	| `prov_xslt` | `properties.mapping-version` → XSLT URI | All records with same mapping version |
648	| `prov_ddb` | `DDB_BASE` (fixed) | Every record in every run |
649	| `prov_provider` | `provider-info.provider-ddb-id` → Provider URI | All records from same institution |
650	| `prov_dataset` | `properties.dataset-id` → Dataset URI | All records in same delivery batch |
651	| `edm_agent` | `edm.RDF.Agent[].about` | GND agent URIs shared across records |
652	| `edm_place` | `edm.RDF.Place[].about` | GND place URIs shared across records |
653	| `skos_concept` | `edm.RDF.Concept[].about` | Subject concept URIs shared across records |
654	| `edm_timespan` | `edm.RDF.TimeSpan[].about` | Time period URIs shared across records |
```

### User *(2026-05-13T17:01:49.592Z)*

```
654	| `edm_timespan` | `edm.RDF.TimeSpan[].about` | Time period URIs shared across records |
655	
656	**Schema** (single shared DuckDB file, not per-run):
657	```sql
658	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS emitted_entities (
659	    uri         VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
660	    entity_type VARCHAR NOT NULL
661	)
662	```
663	
664	**Runtime pattern**: at startup, load all rows into an in-memory `dict[str, str]` (`uri → entity_type`); check membership during processing; after the loop, `INSERT OR IGNORE` all entries back (idempotent, handles pre-existing rows).
665	
666	**Why not a per-record DuckDB lookup**: the number of unique shared-entity URIs is small (tens to low thousands). Loading into memory at startup is O(1) per record; a DuckDB round-trip per record would add I/O overhead at 18.5M scale.
667	
668	**Why not in-memory set only**: the transform is run once per sector file (s1–s7) as separate OS processes. An in-memory set resets between runs, so DDB Agent and XSLT triples would be re-emitted in every sector's output. The DuckDB file survives across runs.
669	
670	**`--entities-db` is optional**: if omitted, a fresh empty `dict` is used — within-run dedup only, no persistence. The `emitted` parameter defaults to `None` in both `emit_prov_triples` and `emit_ddbedm_triples`, which falls back to always-emit (backward-compatible for tests and other callers).
671	
672	**Per-CHO triples are never deduplicated**: linking triples from the CHO to shared nodes (`prov:wasDerivedFrom`, `prov:wasAttributedTo`, `prov:generatedAtTime`, `dcterms:hasVersion`, `dcterms:references`) are unique per record and emitted unconditionally.
673	
674	---
675	
676	## Decision 14: Manual curation over automated schema alignment
677	
678	**Decision**: The alignment table (`alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv`) and all dispatch
679	logic (htype lookup, dc:type × sector dispatch, sparte × mediatype class
680	assignment) were produced by manual curation and explicit decision records (D1–D13),
681	not by automated schema-matching algorithms [Rahm & Bernstein 2001; Shvaiko &
682	Euzenat 2013].
683	
684	**Alternatives considered**:
685	
686	- *LLM-assisted alignment* [Hertling & Paulheim 2023; Giglou et al. 2023]: Feed
687	  source JSON keys + sample values + target ontology namespace documentation to an
688	  LLM and generate `(entity_type, json_key) → predicate IRI` candidate mappings.
689	  Would have covered approximately 60–70% of the 1:1 property mappings in
690	  `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` automatically — cases with clear semantic
691	  correspondences (`prefLabel → skos:prefLabel`, `lat → geo:lat`, `begin →
692	  schema:startDate`). Cannot produce the conditional class dispatch logic (htype ×
693	  sparte × mediatype → rdf:type) without domain knowledge of DDB's institutional
694	  structure and the mocho WEMI model. Useful for bootstrap; insufficient for the
695	  semantically significant part of the alignment.
696	
697	- *Instance-based statistical alignment* [Doan et al. 2002; Madhavan et al. 2001]:
698	  Analyze value distributions per JSON key — IRI vs literal, date format patterns,
699	  language tags, value overlap with target ontology IRIs — to narrow predicate
700	  candidates automatically. Good at datatype inference and distinguishing
701	  IRI-valued from literal-valued properties. Cannot distinguish semantically close
702	  predicates that share the same datatype (e.g. `rdaa:P50067` date-of-birth vs

### User *(2026-05-13T17:01:52.459Z)*

**Tool result**
```
500	## 11. Shared-entity deduplication (2026-05-13)
501	
502	Descriptive triples for shared named entities are emitted at most once per URI across all transform runs. Without this, every sector run re-emits identical triples for the DDB Agent, XSLT SoftwareAgent, provider institutions, datasets, and all GND agents/places/concepts/timespans that appear in multiple records.
503	
504	### 11.1 Mechanism
505	
506	An `emitted: dict[str, str]` (`uri → entity_type`) is passed into both `emit_prov_triples` and `emit_ddbedm_triples`. Each shared-node block checks membership before emitting and registers the URI on first emission. The dict is initialized in `__main__.py` before the record loop (empty = within-run dedup only). With `--entities-db PATH`, it is loaded from DuckDB at startup and written back with `INSERT OR IGNORE` after the loop, enabling cross-run dedup across sector files.
507	
508	**Entity types tracked**:
509	
510	| entity_type | Source |
511	|---|---|
512	| `prov_xslt` | `properties.mapping-version` → XSLT SoftwareAgent URI |
513	| `prov_ddb` | Fixed `DDB_BASE` URI |
514	| `prov_provider` | `provider-info.provider-ddb-id` → Provider Agent URI |
515	| `prov_dataset` | `properties.dataset-id` → Dataset URI |
516	| `edm_agent` | `edm.RDF.Agent[].about` |
517	| `edm_place` | `edm.RDF.Place[].about` |
518	| `skos_concept` | `edm.RDF.Concept[].about` |
519	| `edm_timespan` | `edm.RDF.TimeSpan[].about` |
520	
521	Per-CHO linking triples (`prov:wasDerivedFrom`, `prov:wasAttributedTo`, `prov:generatedAtTime`, `dcterms:hasVersion`, `dcterms:references`) are always emitted unconditionally.
522	
523	### 11.2 DuckDB schema
524	
525	```sql
526	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS emitted_entities (
527	    uri         VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
528	    entity_type VARCHAR NOT NULL
529	)
530	```
531	
532	Separate file from `werk-staging.duckdb`; shared across all sector runs in a production campaign. Managed by the caller — not auto-created in the run output directory.
533	
534	### 11.3 New CLI flag
535	
536	| Flag | Default | Description |
537	|---|---|---|
538	| `--entities-db PATH` | _(none)_ | Shared cross-run entity-dedup DuckDB. Omit for within-run dedup only. |
539	
540	### 11.4 Code changes
541	
542	| File | Change |
543	|---|---|
544	| `emitters.py` | Add `_DEDUP_ENTITY_TYPES` dict; add `emitted` param to `emit_ddbedm_triples` and `emit_prov_triples`; guard 4 PROV-O shared-node blocks + entity loop in `emit_ddbedm_triples` |
545	| `transform.py` | Add `emitted_entities` param to `transform_record`; thread through to both emitters |
546	| `__main__.py` | Add `--entities-db` CLI arg; DuckDB setup/load at startup; pass `emitted_entities` to `transform_record`; batch write-back after loop |
547	
548	### 11.5 Test additions
549	
550	15 new tests across 2 new classes (total: 155).
551	
552	**`TestEmitProvTriplesDedup`** (7 tests): `emitted=None` emits all; dict populated with correct `entity_type` values; each of the 4 shared nodes skipped when URI already in `emitted`; per-CHO linking triples always present even when all shared nodes are pre-emitted.
553	
554	**`TestEmitDdbedmTriplesDedup`** (8 tests): Agent/Place/Concept/TimeSpan skipped when URI already in `emitted`; `ProvidedCHO` never skipped (not in `_DEDUP_ENTITY_TYPES`); `emitted=None` emits all; second call with same `emitted` dict emits 0 agent triples; dict populated with correct `entity_type` after first call.
555	
556	---
557	
558	## 12. Full-corpus run plan
559	
560	The production orchestrator is `scripts/run-transform-sector.sh`. It splits the SQLite table into `--workers` chunks, launches one `python -m transform` OS process per chunk, waits for all to finish, then merges.
561	
562	```bash
563	# one sector, all defaults (50 workers, merge stats only):
564	bash scripts/run-transform-sector.sh --merge --sector s2
```

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/notes/ddbedm-prov-o-plan.md",
  "old_string": "**URI convention**: `urn:ddbedm:` URNs trace the identifier back to its JSON key\nchain (`urn:ddbedm:<block>:<key>:<value>`), making the source unambiguous without\nrequiring a dereferenceable endpoint.",
  "new_string": "**URI convention**: PROV-O shared nodes use short typed-prefix URNs — `urn:ddbedm:dataset:<id>`, `urn:ddbedm:xslt:<ver>`, `urn:ddbedm:provider:<id>` — rather than a property-chain form. The type prefix (`dataset:`, `xslt:`, `provider:`) is a collision guard: bare DDB entity IDs (Agent, Place, Concept, TimeSpan) are minted as `urn:ddbedm:<id>` without a class name, and DDB IDs are 32-character alphanumeric strings (no colons), so the colon after the type prefix is an unambiguous discriminator. None of these URNs are dereferenceable endpoints; they are stable, opaque identifiers scoped to the `urn:ddbedm:` namespace."
}
```

  "new_string": "**Rationale**: `ProvidedCHO` bare IDs are DDB item identifiers — the canonical DDB item URI scheme (`…/item/<id>`) is the correct dereferenceable form and aligns with the `owl:sameAs` link emitted in the mocho graph. All other entity types have no canonical HTTP URI; the `urn:ddbedm:` prefix scopes them to the namespace without encoding a class name. PROV-O shared nodes (Dataset, XSLT, Provider) use a typed-prefix form (`urn:ddbedm:dataset:<id>`, `urn:ddbedm:xslt:<ver>`, `urn:ddbedm:provider:<id>`) as a collision guard — since DDB IDs are purely alphanumeric (no colons), the colon after the type prefix is an unambiguous discriminator. Consistent with `export-s2-plan.md §4.3` and D29."
}

  "command": "python3 - <<'EOF'\nimport json, collections\ndata = json.load(open('/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/data/items-excerpt-1000.json'))\nabout_samples = collections.defaultdict(list)\nfor rec in data[:200]:\n    rdf = rec.get('edm', {}).get('RDF', {}) or {}\n    for etype in ['Agent', 'Place', 'Concept', 'TimeSpan']:\n        entities = rdf.get(etype) or []\n        if isinstance(entities, dict): entities = [entities]\n        for ent in entities:\n            if not isinstance(ent, dict): continue\n            about = ent.get('about') or ''\n            pref = ent.get('prefLabel') or ent.get('label') or ''\n            if isinstance(pref, list): pref = pref[0] if pref else ''\n            if isinstance(pref, dict): pref = pref.get('$', '')\n            about_samples[etype].append((about, str(pref)))\nfor etype, samples in about_samples.items():\n    print('=== ' + etype + ' (first 5) ===')\n    for a, l in samples[:5]:\n        print('  about=' + repr(a) + '  label=' + repr(l))\nEOF\n",

### Assistant *(2026-05-13T21:32:45.588Z)*

The data reveals the issue clearly. Here's what's actually happening:

**Two categories of `about` values:**

| Type | Example | URI-based dedup |
|---|---|---|
| GND/vocab URI | `http://d-nb.info/gnd/4127793-4` (München) | ✓ Correct — same URI across records → dedup works |
| Bare DDB ID | `LZUPFM3E4WLWD2BTG5INU4WFB5BE6BZG` and `RZPV44ZMPDRLCHGYMI2TXSB73UJ5QBHK` (both "Leipzig") | ✗ Different IDs → different triples → dedup doesn't catch it |

For "Leipzig" with two bare IDs, the triples emitted would be:
```
<urn:ddbedm:LZUPFM3E4...> skos:prefLabel "Leipzig"@de .
<urn:ddbedm:RZPV44ZM...>  skos:prefLabel "Leipzig"@de .
```

These are **different triples** (different subjects), so label-based dedup wouldn't eliminate them either — it would just suppress one of the two nodes from the graph entirely, which is loss of data from the source.

**The underlying problem is entity resolution, not triple deduplication.** Two records pointing to different bare IDs for "Leipzig" are asserting two different RDF nodes. Making them appear as one is a merge/sameAs decision that belongs at graph-cleaning or alignment time, not in the transform.

**What the current dedup does correctly:** for `http://d-nb.info/gnd/4127793-4` appearing in 50 records, it emits the descriptive triples once. That's the right scope — stable, authoritative URIs.

**What it doesn't do** (by design): it doesn't unify bare-ID entities that share a label. That's not a dedup problem; it's entity resolution, and the wrong layer to solve it.

So the current URI-based approach is correct for what was specified. Would you like to track bare-ID duplicates as a separate concern (e.g., log a warning when the same `prefLabel` appears under two different bare IDs), or scope entity resolution to a later pipeline stage?

### Assistant *(2026-05-13T21:36:28.389Z)*

Yes. URI-based dedup is the defensible choice for the paper — you're faithfully representing the source data, and entity resolution across bare DDB IDs is a known limitation of the source, not a design flaw in the transform.

One sentence in a limitations/future work paragraph is enough, something like: "Entities identified by DDB-internal bare IDs rather than GND URIs may be represented as multiple distinct nodes in the graph; authority-based entity resolution is left as future work."

### User *(2026-05-13T21:37:23.850Z)*

```
1	\section{Quality and Validation}
2	\label{sec:quality}
3	
4	MOCHO was built with the Ontology Development Kit (ODK).
5	The ontology requires OWL~2~DL expressivity: it makes use of universal restrictions
6	(\texttt{owl:allValuesFrom}), cardinality constraints, complement and disjointness axioms,
7	and property chains, which collectively place it outside the OWL~2~EL profile.
8	
9	OWL~2~DL consistency is verified by HermiT~\cite{motik2009hypertableau} as part of the
10	ODK build; materialized inferences are exported as RDF triples and loaded into QLever
11	alongside the DDB corpus, so no runtime reasoning is performed by the triplestore.
12	The ontology and its documentation are published through the ODK and
13	Widoco~\cite{garijo2017widoco} pipeline, producing both the machine-readable artifact and
14	human-readable HTML documentation from a single build step.
15	The EDM-to-MOCHO property mappings are validated by a suite of unit tests that assert
16	correct predicate assignment per \texttt{(target\_class,~ddb-edm\_prop)} pair against
17	gold-standard triples derived from the Goethe-Faust corpus.
18	
19	\subsection{Completeness}
20	\label{sec:quality:completeness}
21	
22	GEMEA is a snapshot exported between late March and early April~2026, covering
23	97.7\,\% of the digitized DDB corpus at time of writing (Table~\ref{tab:scale}).
24	The remaining gap reflects objects added to the DDB after the snapshot window
25	and a small number of transient API search failures during the export run.
26	
27	\subsection{Scope of Validation}
28	\label{sec:quality:scope}
29	
30	Comprehensive metadata quality evaluation of a cultural heritage
31	knowledge graph at this scale presents a structural bootstrapping problem: rigorous evaluation requires
32	the dataset to be published and accessible, yet conducting and reporting such an evaluation
33	prior to publication is itself impractical at this scale.
34	GEMEA is released to enable this evaluation; the SPARQL endpoint, Linked Data browser,
35	and downloadable dump provide the infrastructure for systematic quality studies at corpus
36	scale.
37	
38	\subsection{Known Limitations}
39	\label{sec:quality:limitations}
40	
41	\begin{itemize}
42	  \item \textbf{Snapshot currency.} The dataset reflects the DDB as of late March to
43	        early April~2026. Records added, updated, or deleted after the export window
44	        are not reflected; no incremental update mechanism is currently implemented.
45	
46	  \item \textbf{Partial GND linking.} Werk linking covers literary and musical works but achieves low recall due to
47	        inconsistent encoding of \texttt{dc:title} values across contributing institutions:
48	        corpus analysis of the Goethe-Faust reference corpus shows 71\,\% of titles
49	        carry no ISBD punctuation~\cite{ifla2012isbd}, with the dominant signal being
50	        the subtitle separator `\,:\,' (18\,\%) rather than the statement-of-responsibility
51	        separator `\,/\,' (2.1\,\%) used for work-title extraction; corpus-wide figures
52	        for 9.2M German-language DDB titles show 92.4\,\% without any ISBD marker.
53	        Future work will apply ISBD punctuation heuristics and a planned NER model
54	        fine-tuned on a DDB subset to improve coverage; authority linking for images
55	        of artworks and architecture via Wikidata,
56	        Bildindex\footnote{\url{https://www.bildindex.de}}, and artresearch.net's
57	        PHAROS infrastructure
58	        is also planned.
59	
60	  \item \textbf{Alignment coverage at scale.} The EDM-to-MOCHO mapping was developed
61	        and validated against the Goethe-Faust corpus (115,432 library records).
62	        Sector-specific edge cases, particularly in the Library sector at 18M records,
63	        may produce fallback or underspecified type assignments.
64	
65	  \item \textbf{Rights metadata.} Each object retains the \texttt{dcterms:rights}
66	        value as declared by the contributing institution; rights statements are not
67	        normalized, verified, or mapped to a controlled vocabulary.
68	
69	  \item \textbf{Geographic and temporal sparsity.} Corpus analysis of the Goethe-Faust
70	        reference corpus shows that only 45\,\% of objects carry any
71	        date value (\texttt{dc:date}, \texttt{dcterms:date}, or \texttt{dcterms:created})
72	        and only 7.1\,\% carry a place value (\texttt{dc:coverage} or
73	        \texttt{dcterms:spatial}).
74	\end{itemize}
75	
```

**Tool result**
```
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Purpose:   Full GeMeA corpus transform — 128 workers across 7 sectors in parallel (Option C, teach03)
3	# Usage:     bash scripts/run_gemea_transform.sh --new      # wipe previous output and restart
4	#            bash scripts/run_gemea_transform.sh --resume   # skip sectors already done
5	#            Run from the goethe-faust project root, inside a tmux/screen session.
6	# Inputs:    /data/ddb/data/s{1..7}.sqlite
7	# Outputs:   $OUT_BASE/s{1..7}/   (per-sector .nq, .duckdb, -stats.json, -errors.jsonl, .log)
8	#            $OUT_BASE/werk-staging-merged.duckdb
9	#            $OUT_BASE/nt/ddbedm.nt, mocho.nt, prov.nt
10	# Deps:      .venv/ created by scripts/setup_venv.sh (optional), duckdb via pip3 --user,
11	#            scripts/split_nq.py (stdlib only)
12	# Notes:     Sectors with >1 worker: export → split JSONL → N parallel transforms.
13	#            Sectors with 1 worker:  export → transform (no split).
14	#            cd into scripts/ before python3 -m transform so duckdb (user site-packages)
15	#            is visible without PYTHONPATH.
16	
17	set -euo pipefail
18	
19	# ── Args ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
20	MODE=""
21	for arg in "$@"; do
22	  case "$arg" in
23	    --new)    MODE=new ;;
24	    --resume) MODE=resume ;;
25	    *) echo "Usage: $0 [--new|--resume]" >&2; exit 1 ;;
26	  esac
27	done
28	if [[ -z "$MODE" ]]; then
29	  echo "Error: specify --new (wipe and restart) or --resume (skip completed sectors)" >&2
30	  exit 1
31	fi
32	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
33	
34	# ── Paths ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
35	GOETHE="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
36	SQLITE_DIR=/data/ddb/data
37	EXPORT_DIR=/data/ddb/gemea/json-export
38	SCRIPTS=$GOETHE/scripts
39	HASH=$(find "$SCRIPTS/transform" -name "*.py" | sort | xargs sha256sum | sha256sum | cut -c1-4)
40	VERSION="$(date '+%Y%m%d')-$(date '+%H')${HASH}"
41	OUT_BASE=/data/ddb/gemea/mocho-transform/$VERSION
42	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
43	
44	CFG=$GOETHE/output/config
45	PYTHON=$( [[ -x "$GOETHE/.venv/bin/python3" ]] && echo "$GOETHE/.venv/bin/python3" || echo python3 )
46	
47	# Workers per sector — 128 total (50% of 256 cores on teach03)
48	# Distributed proportionally to record count so all sectors finish simultaneously.
49	declare -A WORKERS=([1]=17 [2]=86 [3]=1 [4]=6 [5]=8 [6]=9 [7]=1)
50	
51	mkdir -p "$EXPORT_DIR" "$OUT_BASE"
52	
53	if [[ "$MODE" == "new" ]]; then
54	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] --new: wiping previous output"
55	  rm -rf "$OUT_BASE"/s{1..7} "$OUT_BASE/nt" "$OUT_BASE/werk-staging-merged.duckdb"
56	fi
57	
58	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Starting GeMeA transform (${MODE}) — 128 workers across 7 sectors"
59	echo "  GOETHE     = $GOETHE"
60	echo "  SQLITE_DIR = $SQLITE_DIR"
61	echo "  EXPORT_DIR = $EXPORT_DIR"
62	echo "  OUT_BASE   = $OUT_BASE"
63	echo "  Workers    = s1:${WORKERS[1]} s2:${WORKERS[2]} s3:${WORKERS[3]} s4:${WORKERS[4]}" \
64	     "s5:${WORKERS[5]} s6:${WORKERS[6]} s7:${WORKERS[7]}"
65	
66	# ── Phase 1+2: export then transform (chunked where W>1) ──────────────────────
67	for n in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7; do
68	  W=${WORKERS[$n]}
69	  (
70	    mkdir -p "$OUT_BASE/s${n}"
71	
72	    # --resume: skip if any .nq already exists and is non-empty
73	    if [[ "$MODE" == "resume" ]]; then
74	      existing=$(find "$OUT_BASE/s${n}" -name "*.nq" -size +0 2>/dev/null | head -1)
75	      if [[ -n "$existing" ]]; then
76	        echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] skipping — output exists"
77	        exit 0
78	      fi
79	    fi
80	
81	    # Export full sector JSONL
82	    echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] export starting"
83	    cd "$SCRIPTS"
84	    "$PYTHON" -m transform.sqlite_export \
85	      --db  "$SQLITE_DIR/s${n}.sqlite" \
86	      --out "$EXPORT_DIR/s${n}.jsonl" \
87	      2>> "$OUT_BASE/s${n}/export.log"
88	    echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] export done"
89	
90	    if [[ $W -eq 1 ]]; then
91	      # Single worker — transform directly
92	      TOTAL=$(wc -l < "$EXPORT_DIR/s${n}.jsonl")
93	      echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] transform starting (1 worker, ${TOTAL} records)"
94	      cd "$SCRIPTS"
95	      "$PYTHON" -m transform \
96	        --jsonl      "$EXPORT_DIR/s${n}.jsonl" \
97	        --outdir     "$OUT_BASE/s${n}" \
98	        --stats      dispatch \
99	        --total      "$TOTAL" \
100	        --alignment  "$CFG/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv" \
101	        --lido       "$CFG/lido_event_types.csv" \
102	        --htype      "$CFG/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv" \
103	        --mediatype  "$CFG/lookup_mediatype_class.csv" \
104	        --audio      "$CFG/audio_type2class.json"
105	    else
106	      # Multiple workers — split JSONL into W chunks then transform in parallel
107	      CHUNK_DIR="$EXPORT_DIR/s${n}_chunks"
108	      mkdir -p "$CHUNK_DIR"
109	      echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] splitting into ${W} chunks"
110	      split -n l/$W "$EXPORT_DIR/s${n}.jsonl" "$CHUNK_DIR/chunk_"
111	
112	      echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] launching ${W} transform workers"
113	      for chunk in "$CHUNK_DIR"/chunk_*; do
114	        stem=$(basename "$chunk")
115	        TOTAL=$(wc -l < "$chunk")
116	        mkdir -p "$OUT_BASE/s${n}/$stem"
117	        (
118	          cd "$SCRIPTS"
119	          "$PYTHON" -m transform \
120	            --jsonl      "$chunk" \
121	            --outdir     "$OUT_BASE/s${n}/$stem" \
122	            --stats      dispatch \
123	            --total      "$TOTAL" \
124	            --alignment  "$CFG/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv" \
125	            --lido       "$CFG/lido_event_types.csv" \
126	            --htype      "$CFG/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv" \
127	            --mediatype  "$CFG/lookup_mediatype_class.csv" \
128	            --audio      "$CFG/audio_type2class.json"
129	        ) &
130	      done
131	      wait  # wait for all chunk workers of this sector
132	    fi
133	
134	    echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] done"
135	  ) &
136	done
137	
138	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] All sector workers launched — waiting for completion..."
139	wait
140	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] All sectors complete — merging"
141	
142	# ── Phase 3: merge DuckDB werk_staging ───────────────────────────────────────
143	OUT_BASE="$OUT_BASE" "$PYTHON" <<'PYEOF'
144	import glob, os, sys
145	try:
146	    import duckdb
147	except ImportError:
148	    print("duckdb not available — skipping werk_staging merge")
149	    sys.exit(0)
150	out_base = os.environ["OUT_BASE"]
151	shards = sorted(glob.glob(f"{out_base}/**/*-werk-staging.duckdb", recursive=True))
152	if not shards:
153	    print("No werk_staging shards found — skipping DuckDB merge")
154	    sys.exit(0)
155	out = f"{out_base}/werk-staging-merged.duckdb"
156	conn = duckdb.connect(out)
157	conn.execute(f"CREATE TABLE werk_staging AS SELECT * FROM '{shards[0]}'")
158	for p in shards[1:]:
159	    conn.execute(f"INSERT INTO werk_staging SELECT * FROM '{p}'")
160	rows = conn.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM werk_staging").fetchone()[0]
161	conn.close()
162	print(f"werk_staging merged ({len(shards)} shards, {rows} rows) → {out}")
163	PYEOF
164	
165	# ── Phase 4: split each .nq → per-chunk .nt, then merge by graph ─────────────
166	NT_DIR=$OUT_BASE/nt
167	mkdir -p "$NT_DIR"
168	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Splitting N-Quads into per-chunk .nt files"
169	while IFS= read -r nq; do
170	  "$PYTHON" "$SCRIPTS/split_nq.py" "$nq" &
171	done < <(find "$OUT_BASE" -name "*.nq" | sort)
172	wait
173	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] All splits done — merging by graph → $NT_DIR"
174	
175	declare -A seen
176	while IFS= read -r f; do
177	  s="${f##*-}"; s="${s%.nt}"
178	  seen["$s"]=1
179	done < <(find "$OUT_BASE" -name "*-*.nt" ! -path "$NT_DIR/*")
180	for slug in "${!seen[@]}"; do
181	  find "$OUT_BASE" -name "*-${slug}.nt" ! -path "$NT_DIR/*" | sort | xargs cat > "$NT_DIR/${slug}.nt"
182	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] → $NT_DIR/${slug}.nt"
183	done
184	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Split+merge complete"
185	
186	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Done. Output: $OUT_BASE"
187	
```

### Assistant *(2026-05-13T21:39:25.406Z)*

Two things to address before answering: the script's parallelism model and DuckDB's single-writer constraint.

**The problem**: all 7 sectors run simultaneously (`&` on line 135), and within multi-worker sectors all chunk workers also run simultaneously (`&` on line 129). DuckDB allows only one writer at a time — a single shared `--entities-db` would deadlock or error under concurrent writes.

**What actually needs to change:**

1. **Single-worker sectors (s3, s7)** — safe to add `--entities-db` directly:
   ```bash
   --entities-db "$OUT_BASE/entities.duckdb"
   ```
   Sequential execution, no contention.

2. **Multi-worker sectors (s1, s2, s4–s6)** — cannot share a DuckDB file across parallel chunk workers. The practical fix is to add `sort -u` to the Phase 4 merge step instead:
   ```bash
   find "$OUT_BASE" -name "*-${slug}.nt" ! -path "$NT_DIR/*" | sort | xargs cat | sort -u > "$NT_DIR/${slug}.nt"
   ```
   This deduplicates at the NT level after the fact — same result for `prov.nt` and `ddbedm.nt`, no concurrency issue.

3. **Define the DB path** near the other path variables:
   ```bash
   ENTITIES_DB=$OUT_BASE/entities.duckdb
   ```

4. **Update the `--new` wipe** (line 55) to include the entities DB:
   ```bash
   rm -rf "$OUT_BASE"/s{1..7} "$OUT_BASE/nt" "$OUT_BASE/werk-staging-merged.duckdb" "$OUT_BASE/entities.duckdb"
   ```

In short: `--entities-db` is most useful when running sectors **sequentially** against the same DB. In this script's parallel design, `sort -u` at merge time is the equivalent and simpler fix for the multi-worker case. Want me to make these changes?

  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/notes/transform-dedup-plan.md",
  "content": "# Transform dedup — `run_gemea_transform.sh` integration plan\n\n**Date**: 2026-05-13\n**Status**: Pending — context captured for a later session\n**Relates to**: `scripts/run_gemea_transform.sh`, `scripts/transform/__main__.py`\n\n## 1. Background\n\n`__main__.py` gained an `--entities-db PATH` flag (2026-05-13, see `notes/transform-implementation-actual.md §11`) for cross-run shared-entity deduplication. It stores a `emitted_entities` DuckDB table (`uri VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY, entity_type VARCHAR NOT NULL`) that prevents re-emitting identical descriptive triples for shared PROV-O nodes and `edm:Agent`/`Place`/`skos:Concept`/`edm:TimeSpan` entities across sector runs.\n\n`run_gemea_transform.sh` does not yet pass `--entities-db` to any transform invocation.\n\n## 2. Constraint: DuckDB single-writer\n\nDuckDB allows only one writer at a time. The script runs all 7 sectors in parallel (background subshells, line 135) and within multi-worker sectors runs all chunk workers in parallel (line 129). A single shared `--entities-db` file cannot be written to concurrently — doing so will deadlock or raise \"database is locked\".\n\n## 3. Plan\n\n### 3.1 Add `ENTITIES_DB` path variable\n\nAdd near the other path variables (after line 41):\n\n```bash\nENTITIES_DB=$OUT_BASE/entities.duckdb\n```\n\n### 3.2 Wipe entities DB on `--new`\n\nExtend the `--new` wipe (line 55):\n\n```bash\nrm -rf \"$OUT_BASE\"/s{1..7} \"$OUT_BASE/nt\" \\\n       \"$OUT_BASE/werk-staging-merged.duckdb\" \\\n       \"$OUT_BASE/entities.duckdb\"\n```\n\n### 3.3 Single-worker sectors (s3, s7) — pass `--entities-db` directly\n\nThese sectors run one transform process (no concurrency). Add the flag to the single-worker invocation (lines 95–104):\n\n```bash\n\"$PYTHON\" -m transform \\\n  --jsonl      \"$EXPORT_DIR/s${n}.jsonl\" \\\n  --outdir     \"$OUT_BASE/s${n}\" \\\n  --stats      dispatch \\\n  --total      \"$TOTAL\" \\\n  --alignment  \"$CFG/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv\" \\\n  --lido       \"$CFG/lido_event_types.csv\" \\\n  --htype      \"$CFG/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv\" \\\n  --mediatype  \"$CFG/lookup_mediatype_class.csv\" \\\n  --audio      \"$CFG/audio_type2class.json\" \\\n  --entities-db \"$ENTITIES_DB\"\n```\n\nSafe because the single-worker sectors (s3 and s7) themselves still run in parallel with other sectors, but each only has one writer to `ENTITIES_DB` at a time. If s3 and s7 happen to write back simultaneously there is a race. To be safe, either:\n- Accept the risk (both use `INSERT OR IGNORE`; worst case one write is retried or dropped — negligible for these small sectors), or\n- Give each single-worker sector its own entities DB and merge them afterward (same pattern as `werk-staging-merged.duckdb`).\n\nThe simpler fix (accept the risk) is likely fine in practice given s3 and s7 are tiny sectors.\n\n### 3.4 Multi-worker sectors (s1, s2, s4, s5, s6) — dedup at merge time with `sort -u`\n\nCannot share a DuckDB file across parallel chunk workers. Instead, replace the Phase 4 per-graph merge (lines 181–183) with a `sort -u` pipe:\n\n```bash\nfor slug in \"${!seen[@]}\"; do\n  find \"$OUT_BASE\" -name \"*-${slug}.nt\" ! -path \"$NT_DIR/*\" | sort | xargs cat \\\n    | sort -u > \"$NT_DIR/${slug}.nt\"\n  echo \"[$(date '+%F %T')] → $NT_DIR/${slug}.nt (deduped)\"\ndone\n```\n\n`sort -u` on N-Triples is correct because each triple is a single line and identical triples are byte-identical. This achieves the same result as `--entities-db` for the multi-worker case with no concurrency constraint.\n\n**Note on memory**: `sort -u` on `prov.nt` at 18M-record scale may be large. Use `LC_ALL=C sort -u` for speed; add `--buffer-size=2G` if needed on teach03.\n\n### 3.5 Update script header comment\n\nUpdate the `# Outputs:` line to document `entities.duckdb`:\n\n```bash\n# Outputs:   $OUT_BASE/s{1..7}/   (per-sector .nq, .duckdb, -stats.json, -errors.jsonl, .log)\n#            $OUT_BASE/werk-staging-merged.duckdb\n#            $OUT_BASE/entities.duckdb  (cross-run shared-entity dedup state; single-writer sectors only)\n#            $OUT_BASE/nt/ddbedm.nt, mocho.nt, prov.nt  (deduped via sort -u)\n```\n\n## 4. Files to change\n\n| File | Change |\n|---|---|\n| `scripts/run_gemea_transform.sh` | Add `ENTITIES_DB` var; extend `--new` wipe; add `--entities-db` to single-worker invocation; replace Phase 4 merge with `sort -u`; update header comment |\n\nNo changes needed to `scripts/transform/` — the `--entities-db` flag is already implemented.\n\n## 5. Testing\n\nAfter the change, run a two-sector subset and verify:\n- `entities.duckdb` exists and contains rows after s3 or s7 completes\n- `prov.nt` contains each PROV-O shared-node URI described exactly once (grep for `urn:ddbedm:dataset:`)\n- Triple count in `prov.nt` is lower than without dedup (shared nodes not repeated per chunk)\n"
}
```

1	# Transform Future Work
2	
3	**Date**: 2026-05-02
4	**Status**: Parking lot
5	**Related**: `transform-props-mapping-plan.md`, `transform-props-mapping-adr.md`
6	
7	Items deferred from the current transform implementation. Each section notes the prerequisite and the relevant plan/ADR section.
8	
9	---
10	
11	## §1 Typed RDA creator subproperties
12	
13	**Prerequisite**: GND agent type resolved (person / collective agent / corporate body / family) by `link_gnd_agents.py`.
14	
15	When agent type is known, replace the generic `rdam:P30329` / `rdaw:P10065` / `rdae:P20053` triples with the typed subproperty:
16	
17	| WEMI | Person | Collective agent | Corporate body | Family |
18	|---|---|---|---|---|
19	| M | `rdam:P30363` | `rdam:P30392` | `rdam:P30421` | `rdam:P30450` |
20	| W | `rdaw:P10437` | `rdaw:P10484` | `rdaw:P10531` | `rdaw:P10578` |
21	| E | `rdae:P20389` | `rdae:P20448` | `rdae:P20507` | `rdae:P20566` |
22	
23	**label_full**:
24	- `rdam:P30363` — "has creator person of manifestation"
25	- `rdam:P30392` — "has creator collective agent of manifestation"
26	- `rdam:P30421` — "has creator corporate body of manifestation"
27	- `rdam:P30450` — "has creator family of manifestation"
28	- `rdaw:P10437` — "has creator person of work"
29	- `rdaw:P10484` — "has creator collective agent of work"
30	- `rdaw:P10531` — "has creator corporate body of work"
31	- `rdaw:P10578` — "has creator family of work"
32	- `rdae:P20389` — "has creator person of expression"
33	- `rdae:P20448` — "has creator collective agent of expression"
34	- `rdae:P20507` — "has creator corporate body of expression"
35	- `rdae:P20566` — "has creator family of expression"
36	
37	Also requires extending `lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv` with typed creator rows per class.
38	
39	---
40	
41	## §2 dc:language / dcterms:language — Expression node dispatch
42	
43	**Prerequisite**: WEMI decomposition (Expression node minting).
44	
45	`dc:language` and `dcterms:language` are Expression-level in RDA (`rdae:P20006` "has language of expression"). Currently emitted on the Manifestation node as a WEMI mismatch (ADR D11). When Expression nodes are minted:
46	
47	1. The presence of a `dc:language` or `dcterms:language` value on a ProvidedCHO **triggers Expression node minting** — an Expression instance must be created and linked to the Manifestation via `rdam:P30139` "has expression manifested".
48	2. Language triples move to the Expression node: `<expression> rdae:P20006 <LOC-URI>` (or `dc:language` literal as a cross-WEMI handle).
49	3. The same LOC URI typed as `rdae:C10006` (Expression class) may serve as the Expression node IRI if a language-scoped Expression URI pattern is adopted.
50	
51	This affects all class dispatch paths, not just RDA — RiC-O would use `rico:hasOrHadLanguage` on the Expression analog in the archival model.
52	
53	See `transform-props-mapping-plan.md §13–14` and `transform-props-mapping-adr.md D11`.
54	
55	---
56	
57	## §3 dc:format → rdam:P30001 carrier type
58	
59	**Prerequisite**: Controlled vocabulary normalization of `dc:format` values.
60	
61	Current `dc:format` values are free-text technique/medium strings. If values can be normalized to RDA carrier type codes, promote to `rdam:P30001` "has carrier type".
62	
63	See `transform-props-mapping-plan.md §24`.
64	
65	---
66	
67	## §4 dc:issued → rdam:P30011 date of publication
68	
69	**Prerequisite**: Confirm `rdam:P30011` is present in mocho imports.
70	
71	Currently `dc:issued` collapses to `rdam:P30278` "has date of manifestation" (same as `dc:date`). If distinction between general date and publication date is needed, use `rdam:P30011` "has date of publication" for `issued`.
72	
73	See `transform-props-mapping-plan.md §21`.
74	
75	---
76	
77	## §5 dcterms:alternative → rdam:P30128 variant title
78	
79	**Prerequisite**: Corpus analysis of `dcterms:alternative` values.
80	
81	Currently mapped to `rdam:P30131` "has abbreviated title". If corpus shows non-abbreviated variant titles, switch to `rdam:P30128` "has variant title of manifestation".
82	
83	See `transform-props-mapping-plan.md §26`.
84	
85	---
86	
87	## §6 dc:contributor — typed RDA role properties
88	
89	**Prerequisite**: GND role resolution.
90	
91	Currently kept as `dc:contributor`. Options once GND roles are available:
92	- (a) mediatype dispatch: `rdam:P30328` "has contributor agent of text" for TEXT records
93	- (b) add generic `rdam:` contributor superclass to mocho imports
94	- (c) typed role triples from GND `gnd:professionOrOccupation`
95	
96	See `transform-props-mapping-plan.md §23`.
97	
98	---
99	
100	## §7 dc:format — KB linking and controlled-vocab dispatch
101	
102	**Prerequisite**: Corpus analysis of `dc:format` values + controlled vocabulary normalisation.
103	
104	`dc:format` values are free-text technique/medium strings (e.g. "Kohlezeichnung (?) (Technik)", "Negativ in color, quer"). All RDA candidates (`rdam:P30001` carrier type, `rdam:P30002` media type, `rdam:P30208` base material) and all vocab-specific candidates (`vra:material`) **require controlled vocabulary objects** — not applicable to raw literals.
105	
106	Two paths once prerequisite is met:
107	
108	1. **KB linking**: normalise dc:format labels to GND, Getty AAT, or AAT-mapped RDA vocabulary terms. Emit `<cho> dcterms:format <kb-uri>` + Concept stub analogous to `dcType` (§15). Target KBs: GND-Sachbegriff (technique terms), Getty AAT `Materials` facet.
109	
110	2. **Class-specific dispatch** (after KB linking): once URI objects are available, promote to class-specific predicate:
111	   - `rdac:C10007`, `mocho:Manifestation` → `rdam:P30208` "has base material of manifestation"
112	   - `vra:Image`, `vra:Work` → `vra:material`
113	   - `rico:Record*` → `rico:hasOrHadProductionTechniqueType` (if property exists in RiC-O imports)
114	
115	See `transform-props-mapping-plan.md §18`.
116	
117	---
118	
119	## §8 dc:extent — structured parse and domain-specific property dispatch
120	
121	**Prerequisite**: Regex-based pattern classifier (pagination / dimensions / digital) + unit normalisation.
122	
123	Current treatment emits `<cho> dc:extent "..."` as a plain literal. 95,808 values are all literals; pattern breakdown: 47.6% pagination, 29.1% dimensions, 23.2% other (see `notes/corpus-analysis.md §4`).
124	
125	Once values are parsed, dispatch by pattern and target class:
126	
127	| pattern | RDA (Manifestation) | VRA |
128	|---|---|---|
129	| pagination | `rdam:P30182` "has extent of manifestation" | — |
130	| dimensions | `rdam:P30169` "has dimensions" | `vra:measurementsSet` |
131	| still image dimensions | `rdam:P30171` "has dimensions of still image" | `vra:measurementsSet` |
132	| file size / digital | `rdam:P30183` "has file size" | — |
133	
134	VRA Core 4 `vra:measurementsSet` supports typed structured measurements (height, width, depth, diameter) with explicit value and unit fields — preferable to a free-text literal for museum/image records (`vra:Image`, `vra:Work`).
135	
136	See `transform-props-mapping-plan.md §19`.
137	
138	---
139	
140	## §9 dc:description — NER enrichment for Museum and Monument Preservation sectors
141	
142	**Prerequisite**: Corpus analysis of description text length by sector (see `notes/corpus-analysis.md §5`).
143	
144	Monument Preservation (mean 3,275 chars, median 1,863) and Museum (mean 321, median 188) have substantially longer free-text descriptions than other sectors. These are candidates for NER-based enrichment to extract named entities (persons, places, organisations, dates, materials) and link them to GND/GeoNames/Getty authority records.
145	
146	Proposed pipeline:
147	
148	1. **Filter**: select records where `sector ∈ {Museum, Monument Preservation}` and `len(description) > threshold` (e.g. 100 chars).
149	2. **NER**: run a multilingual NER model (e.g. `xlm-roberta-large` fine-tuned on German heritage text) to tag PER, LOC, ORG, DATE, MATERIAL spans.
150	3. **Linking**: resolve tagged spans against GND (persons, places, organisations), GeoNames (places), Getty AAT (materials/techniques) via lobid-gnd and AAT SPARQL.
151	4. **Emit**: for each resolved entity, add triples alongside the description literal:
152	   - PER → `dc:subject <gnd-person-uri>` or typed `rdam:P30327` "has subject agent"
153	   - LOC → `dc:spatial <geonames-uri>` (if place of creation/use context)
154	   - ORG → `dc:subject <gnd-org-uri>`
155	   - MATERIAL → `vra:material <aat-uri>` / `rdam:P30208`
156	
157	See `notes/corpus-analysis.md §5`, `notes/nlp-tasks.md` (GeMeA NER decisions), `transform-props-mapping-plan.md §8`.
158	
159	---
160	
161	## §10 edm:Agent — domain-specific gndo class dispatch
162	
163	**Prerequisite**: GND agent type resolved (`gndo:DifferentiatedPerson`, `gndo:CorporateBody`, `gndo:ConferenceOrEvent`, `gndo:Family`) by `link_gnd_agents.py` (GeMeA Phase 1b).
164	
165	Currently all `edm:Agent` nodes are typed as `mocho:Agent` and properties are emitted under a single mapping row (D13, `transform-props-mapping-adr.md`). Once agent type is known, the `mocho:Agent` stub rows in `lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv` should be split into type-specific rows and the following domain mismatches corrected:
166	
167	**1. Date properties — type dispatch**
168	
169	| Property | Current | Correct domain |
170	|---|---|---|
171	| `gndo:dateOfBirth` / `gndo:dateOfDeath` | emitted on all agents | `gndo:DifferentiatedPerson` only |
172	| `gndo:dateOfEstablishment` / `gndo:dateOfTermination` | emitted on all agents | `gndo:CorporateBody`, `gndo:ConferenceOrEvent` only |
173	| `edm:begin` | passthrough | → `gndo:dateOfBirth` (Person) or `gndo:dateOfEstablishment` (CorporateBody/Event) |
174	| `edm:end` | passthrough | → `gndo:dateOfDeath` (Person) or `gndo:dateOfTermination` (CorporateBody/Event) |
175	
176	**2. Label properties — type-specific gndo name properties**
177	
178	| Property | Current | Target |
179	|---|---|---|
180	| `skos:prefLabel` | passthrough | → `gndo:preferredNameForThePerson` / `gndo:preferredNameForTheCorporateBody` etc. |
181	| `foaf:name` | passthrough | → same as above (gndo preferred name, type-specific) |
182	
183	**3. Other domain-restricted properties**
184	
185	| Property | Correct domain |
186	|---|---|
187	| `gndo:gender` | `gndo:DifferentiatedPerson` only |
188	| `gndo:placeOfBirth` / `gndo:placeOfDeath` | `gndo:DifferentiatedPerson` only |
189	| `gndo:professionOrOccupation` | `gndo:DifferentiatedPerson`, `gndo:Family` only |
190	
191	**Implementation**: extend `lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv` with rows keyed by gndo subclass instead of `mocho:Agent`; update `gen_agent_alignment_rows.py` to generate the split rows; retire the `mocho:Agent` stub rows.
192	
193	See `transform-props-mapping-adr.md` D13.
194	
195	---
196	
197	## §11 Linking to internal Expression KB
198	
199	**Prerequisite**: Expression node minting (§2).
200	
201	When Expression nodes are minted, language becomes an additional lookup dimension alongside title and creator. The GND Werk lookup table (see `transform-revised-plan.md §1.2`) covers Work-level linking; the Expression KB extends this with language to identify distinct expressions of the same Work.
202	
203	Expression lookup key: `(dc:title, dc:creator, dc:language)` — Work title + creator + language of expression.
204	
205	| Field | Source path | Role |
206	|---|---|---|
207	| `dc:title` / `rdaw:P10088` | Work-level title | primary key |
208	| `dc:language` / `rdae:P20006` | language of expression | expression-discriminating dimension |
209	| `dc:creator` (URI or `last, first` literal) | GND URI or normalized name | creator key |
210	| `dc:date` | expression date/version | secondary discriminator (optional) |
211	
212	Two records sharing the same (title, creator, language) tuple are candidate expressions of the same Work. Deduplication and URI assignment for Expression nodes depends on this lookup.
213	
214	See `transform-props-mapping-plan.md §13–14` and `transform-props-mapping-adr.md D11`.
215	
216	---
217	
218	## §12 Linking vra:Work and mocho:ImageWork to ArtKB and Wikidata
219	
220	**Prerequisite**: Work-level URI assignment (werk_staging populated; GND Werk lookup complete for §1.2 classes).
221	
222	`vra:Work` (ht015 Illustration, ht019 Karte, sparte005/mt002 Image) and `mocho:ImageWork` are currently written to N-Quads and DuckDB werk_staging but have no external authority links. Two target KBs:
223	
224	| KB | Scope | Link predicate |
225	|---|---|---|
226	| ArtKB | Art works, images, visual objects (German heritage focus) | `owl:sameAs` or `skos:exactMatch` |
227	| Wikidata | Broad coverage; Q-items for artworks, maps, photographs | `owl:sameAs` or `skos:exactMatch` |
228	
229	**Lookup strategy**: title + creator + date tuple → KB entity lookup, analogous to GND Werk lookup in `link_gnd_works.py`. Key fields from werk_staging: `dc_title`, `creator_uris`, `dc_created`.
230	
231	**Emit**: for each resolved match, add to the mocho named graph:
232	```
233	<work-uri> owl:sameAs <artkb-uri> .
234	<work-uri> owl:sameAs <wikidata-uri> .
235	```
236	
237	**Open questions**:
238	- ArtKB API / SPARQL endpoint availability and query interface
239	- Wikidata: use `wikidata:P180` (depicts) or direct work Q-item match?
240	- Confidence threshold for string-match vs. exact-match linking
241	
242	---
243	
244	## §13 QLever text-entity link files for `ql:contains-word` support
245	
246	**Prerequisite**: N-Quads output from the transform pipeline (any run).
247	
248	QLever's `ql:contains-word` / `ql:has-word` predicates require the text index to be built with entity-to-text-record links. The `-W` flag (`--text-words-from-literals`) builds a word vocabulary from literals but does NOT create these links. The result: `ql:contains-word "word"` finds text records but their internal IDs cannot be joined back to KG entities via triple patterns or `ql:contains-entity`.
249	
250	To enable `ql:contains-word` queries against the KG, the index must be rebuilt with a docstrings file (`-d`) that maps entity IRIs to their associated text. Two files are required by `qlever-index`:
251	
252	| File | Format | Content |
253	|---|---|---|
254	| words file (`-w`) | `word\tentity_IRI\tdoc_id\tscore\n` (one row per word) | tokenised literal values per entity |
255	| docs file (`-d`) | `doc_id\tentity_IRI\tfull_text\n` | full literal text per entity |
256	
257	**Generation approach**: post-process the N-Quads output. For each triple `<subject> <predicate> <literal>` where `<predicate>` is a text-searchable property (at minimum `dc:title`, `dc:description`, `dc:subject`):
258	
259	1. Emit one docs-file row: `doc_id → subject IRI → concatenated literal values`
260	2. Emit one words-file row per token (whitespace-tokenised, lowercased) in the literal
261	
262	**Script location**: `scripts/transform/gen_qlever_text_files.py`
263	
264	**Affected properties** (initial set):
265	- `dc:title`, `dcterms:title`, `rdam:P30156`
266	- `dc:description`
267	- `dc:subject` (literal values only)
268	- `dc:creator`, `dc:contributor` (literal values only — GND-linked values are entity IRIs)
269	
270	**Re-indexing**: rebuild `docker-compose.qlever.yml` to pass `-d` and `-w` alongside (or instead of) `-W`.
271	
272	**Why it matters**: once these files exist, `FILTER(CONTAINS(...))` in Query 3 of the ISWC paper can be replaced with `ql:contains-word`, giving an order-of-magnitude speedup on large corpora and enabling ranked retrieval via BM25 (`--bm25-k`/`--bm25-b` flags). Also unblocks the "KG-grounded retrieval" use case in Section 5.3 of the paper.
273	
```

---

### Assistant *(2026-05-13T21:42:49.654Z)*

**Tool call:** `Edit`
```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/notes/transform-future-plan.md",
  "old_string": "---\n\n## §13 QLever text-entity link files",
  "new_string": "---\n\n## §13 Entity resolution for bare DDB identifiers\n\n**Context**: DDB assigns record-scoped internal IDs (32-character alphanumeric) to `edm:Agent`, `edm:Place`, `skos:Concept`, and `edm:TimeSpan` entities when no authority URI (GND, GeoNames, etc.) is available. These IDs are not shared across contributing institutions, so the same real-world entity — for example the place \"Leipzig\" — can appear under multiple distinct bare IDs in the corpus. Each bare ID is minted as a separate `urn:ddbedm:<id>` URI, producing multiple disconnected nodes in the graph.\n\n**Evidence**: corpus sample of 200 records from `data/items-excerpt-1000.json` shows the same `skos:prefLabel \"Leipzig\"` under at least two different bare IDs (`LZUPFM3E4WLWD2BTG5INU4WFB5BE6BZG`, `RZPV44ZMPDRLCHGYMI2TXSB73UJ5QBHK`). Entities with GND URIs (e.g. `http://d-nb.info/gnd/4127793-4` for München) are already consistent across records and need no resolution.\n\n**Why not in the transform**: the transform emits triples faithful to the source. Two bare IDs pointing to the same label are two distinct source assertions; merging them would be data loss without an explicit authority decision. This is an entity resolution problem, not a deduplication problem.\n\n**Planned approach**:\n\n1. **Authority lookup first**: after `link_gnd_agents.py` links persons and organisations to GND URIs, the remaining unlinked agents are the hardest cases. For places, run bare-ID values through a GeoNames / GND-Geographikum lookup on `skos:prefLabel`.\n2. **Candidate pair generation**: for bare-ID entities of the same type with identical `skos:prefLabel` (case-folded, whitespace-normalised), emit them as `owl:sameAs` candidates into a separate staging table.\n3. **Merge decision**: apply a confidence threshold (exact label match + same entity type → high confidence); emit `owl:sameAs` triples into a dedicated named graph. QLever can then union-merge the nodes at query time without modifying the source `ddbedm` graph.\n4. **Scope**: prioritise `edm:Place` (GeoNames lookup) and `edm:Agent` persons (GND); `skos:Concept` and `edm:TimeSpan` bare IDs are lower priority.\n\n**Note for paper**: acknowledged as a known limitation in `paper/iswc-2026/40-quality.tex §Known Limitations` — authority-based entity resolution for bare-identifier entities is left as future work.\n\n---\n\n## §14 QLever text-entity link files"
}
```

### User *(2026-05-14T08:56:05.386Z)*

i am planning to run on sector sqlite with sequentially, but concurrently with several workers. what do we need to change to make these workers update the same DB?

---

### Assistant *(2026-05-14T08:57:02.939Z)*

The current implementation holds the DB connection open for the entire run and writes back in one batch at the end. That breaks under concurrent workers in two ways:

1. **Concurrent reads at startup** — `duckdb.connect()` in write mode allows only one connection. Workers opening the file simultaneously will error.
2. **Concurrent write-backs** — all workers try to `INSERT OR IGNORE` at the same time; all but one will get "database is locked".

**Two changes needed, both in `__main__.py`:**

**1. Split the DB interaction into two short-lived phases**
- Load phase: open `read_only=True`, fetch all rows, close immediately. DuckDB allows concurrent read-only connections.
- Write-back phase: open in write mode, insert, close. Must be serialized.

**2. Serialize write-back with a file lock**
Use `fcntl.flock` on a `<db>.lock` sidecar file. Each worker acquires an exclusive lock before opening the DB for writing, releases after closing:

```python
import fcntl

# load phase
conn = duckdb.connect(str(args.entities_db), read_only=True)
rows = conn.execute("SELECT uri, entity_type FROM emitted_entities").fetchall()
conn.close()   # release immediately — other workers can now read

# ... processing loop ...

# write-back phase
lock_path = str(args.entities_db) + ".lock"
with open(lock_path, "a") as lf:
    fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)   # blocks until it's our turn
    try:
        conn = duckdb.connect(str(args.entities_db))
        conn.executemany(
            "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO emitted_entities VALUES (?, ?)",
            list(emitted_entities.items()),
        )
        conn.close()
    finally:
        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)
```

**Limitation that remains**: workers don't see each other's discoveries during processing — only what was in the DB at startup. So if worker A and worker B both encounter `urn:ddbedm:dataset:X` for the first time in the same run, both will emit it. The duplicate is removed by `sort -u` at NT merge time. The DB still serves its cross-run purpose correctly.

Only `__main__.py` changes; no changes needed to `emitters.py`, `transform.py`, or the shell script. Shall I make the change?

1	"""
2	Purpose:    Transform DDB-EDM JSONL records to mocho-aligned N-Quads.
3	            Produces four named-graph streams: ddbedm (verbatim EDM passthrough),
4	            mocho (mocho-aligned triples), prov (PROV-O Layer 1), and a DuckDB
5	            werk_staging table for GND Werk linking (link_gnd_works.py, Phase 0).
6	            Reference implementation for the mocho ingest pipeline.
7	            Decisions: transform-adr.md D11/D15/D17, transform-script-adr.md D1–D27.
8	Usage:      python -m transform
9	                [--jsonl FILE] [--ids FILE] [--outdir DIR]
10	                [--alignment FILE] [--lido FILE] [--htype FILE]
11	                [--mediatype FILE] [--audio FILE]
12	                [--stats LEVEL] [--log-level LEVEL]
13	                [--limit N] [--total N] [--log-interval N] [--debug]
14	Inputs:     data/items-all-goethe-faust.json              JSONL, one record per line
15	            data/ids-all-goethe-faust.txt                  32-char object IDs, one per line
16	            output/config/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv  (target_class, edm_prop) → target_prop
17	            output/config/lido_event_types.csv             lido_uri → agent predicates per WEMI
18	            output/config/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv       htype_code → (rdf_type, rst_iris)
19	            output/config/lookup_mediatype_class.csv       (sparte, mediatype) → class dispatch row
20	            output/config/audio_type2class.json            mt001 dc:type → group (A/B/C)
21	Outputs:    output/transform/YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS/               run directory (one per invocation)
22	              <stem>.nq                                    combined N-Quads (all named graphs)
23	              <stem>-werk-staging.duckdb                  W-slot staging rows
24	              <stem>-stats.json                           run statistics
25	              <stem>-errors.jsonl                         per-record errors (written live)
26	              <stem>.log                                  run log
27	            <stem> is the input filename without extension (e.g. s2 → s2.nq)
28	Deps:       stdlib only + duckdb (pip install duckdb)
29	Assumes:    JSONL: one JSON object per line; record structure edm.RDF.*
30	            All config CSVs present at output/config/

315	                stats["record_errors"] += 1
316	                continue
317	
318	            if not streams:
319	                stats["filtered"] += 1
320	                continue
321	
322	            for graph_name, graph_lines in streams.items():
323	                for nq in graph_lines:

325	                    stats["triples_total"] += 1
326	                    stats[f"triples_{graph_name}"] += 1
327	
328	            stats["records_processed"]  += 1
329	            stats["uri_sanitized"]      += pred_info.get("uri_sanitized",   0)
330	            stats["uri_split"]          += pred_info.get("uri_split",       0)
331	            stats["uri_about_split"]    += pred_info.get("uri_about_split", 0)
332	
333	            if stats_level in ("dispatch", "full"):
334	                if dispatch_info.get("is_mt007"):
335	                    stats["skipped_mt007"] += 1
336	                elif dispatch_info.get("fallback"):
337	                    stats["dispatch_fallback"] += 1
338	                elif dispatch_info.get("htype_used"):
339	                    stats["dispatch_htype"] += 1
340	                else:
341	                    stats["dispatch_mediatype"] += 1
342	
343	                tc = dispatch_info.get("target_class", "")
344	                if tc:

346	                    class_counts[wemi][_to_curie(tc)] += 1
347	
348	                mt = (dispatch_info.get("mediatype", "") or "").split("/")[-1]
349	                if mt:
350	                    mt_dist[mt] += 1
351	
352	                rdf_top = record["edm"]["RDF"]
353	                cho_raw = rdf_top.get("ProvidedCHO") or {}
354	                if isinstance(cho_raw, list):
355	                    cho_raw = cho_raw[0] if cho_raw else {}
356	                raw_ht = (cho_raw.get("hierarchyType") or "").strip()
357	                if raw_ht:
358	                    ht_dist[raw_ht.replace("htype_", "ht")] += 1
359	
360	                ddbedm_cls.update(pred_info["ddbedm_classes"])
361	                ddbedm_preds.update(pred_info["ddbedm_preds"])
362	                mocho_preds_all.update(pred_info["mocho_preds_all"])
363	                mocho_preds_new.update(pred_info["mocho_preds_new"])
364	
365	            if werk_row and conn is not None:
366	                conn.execute(
367	                    "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO werk_staging VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)",
368	                    [
369	                        werk_row["ddb_obj_id"],
370	                        werk_row["cho_uri"],

372	                        werk_row["dc_title"],

374	                        werk_row["dc_created"],
375	                        werk_row["creator_uris"],
376	                        werk_row["creator_literals"],
377	                    ],
378	                )
379	                stats["werk_staging_rows"] += 1
380	                werk_by_class[_to_curie(werk_row["target_class"])] += 1
381	
382	            processed = stats["records_processed"]
383	            if args.log_interval and processed and processed % args.log_interval == 0:
384	                elapsed = time.monotonic() - start_time
385	                rate    = processed / elapsed if elapsed > 0 else 0
386	                errors  = stats["json_errors"] + stats["record_errors"]
387	                eta_str = ""
388	                if args.total and rate > 0:

390	                    eta_str   = f" | ETA {_fmt_duration(remaining)}"
391	                total_str = f"/{args.total:,}" if args.total else ""
392	                log.info(

394	                    " | %.0f rec/s | elapsed %s%s",
395	                    f"{processed:,}", total_str,

398	                )

403	            list(emitted_entities.items()),

407	
408	    if conn is not None:

412
