### User *(2026-05-15T09:37:51.844Z)*

**Tool result**
```
1	# Transform prescan — two-pass architecture plan
2	
3	**Date**: 2026-05-14
4	**Status**: Pending — context captured for a later session
5	**Relates to**: `scripts/transform/__main__.py`, `scripts/run_gemea_transform.sh`,
6	               `gemea/scripts/py/export_ddb.py`, `gemea/scripts/py/export_ddb_parquet.py`
7	
8	---
9	
10	## 1. Motivation
11	
12	`--prov-db` was added (2026-05-13, `transform-implementation-actual.md §11`) to
13	deduplicate shared PROV-O node descriptive triples across sector runs. The current
14	design loads the DB at startup and writes back after the record loop — which breaks
15	under concurrent workers (DuckDB single-writer constraint).
16	
17	The fix is a two-pass architecture:
18	
19	- **Pass 1 (prescan)**: single-process scan that pre-populates `entities.duckdb` and
20	  builds the Parquet metadata file. No transform, no concurrency.
21	- **Pass 2 (main transform)**: N concurrent workers load `entities.duckdb` read-only
22	  at startup and never write to it. No locking needed.
23	
24	---
25	
26	## 2. Pass 1 — `scripts/transform/prescan.py`
27	
28	### 2.1 Inputs
29	
30	| Arg | Description |
31	|---|---|
32	| `--db PATH` | Sector SQLite file (`objs` table, `bufgz` column) |
33	| `--prov-db PATH` | Output DuckDB for `emitted_entities` table |
34	| `--prov-out PATH` | Output `.nq` file for shared PROV-O descriptive triples |
35	| `--parquet-out PATH` | Output `<stem>_meta.parquet` |
36	
37	### 2.2 What it does
38	
39	**Single scan** of the SQLite file (sequential, one process):
40	
41	1. For each record, extract:
42	   - `properties.mapping-version` → `urn:ddbedm:xslt:<ver>`
43	   - `properties.dataset-id` → `urn:ddbedm:dataset:<id>`
44	   - `provider-info.provider-ddb-id` → `urn:ddbedm:provider:<id>`
45	   - Fixed: `DDB_BASE = "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de"`
46	   - Parquet metadata row (see §2.3)
47	
48	2. On first encounter of each PROV-O URI, emit its descriptive triples to
49	   `prov-shared.nq` via `emit_prov_triples` and register the URI in a local set.
50	
51	3. After the loop:
52	   - Write all unique PROV-O URIs to `entities.duckdb` (`emitted_entities` table)
53	   - Flush the Parquet writer
54	
55	**No edm:Agent / Place / Concept / TimeSpan in Pass 1** — those are deduplicated by
56	`sort -u` at NT merge time (Pass 2 produces per-chunk NT files; Phase 4 merges with
57	`sort -u`).
58	
59	### 2.3 Parquet schema changes (also applies to `export_ddb.py` and `export_ddb_parquet.py`)
60	
61	Changes to `PARQUET_SCHEMA` (defined in `gemea/scripts/py/export_ddb.py`,
62	imported by `export_ddb_parquet.py`):
63	
64	| Field | Current | New |
65	|---|---|---|
66	| `lang` | `pa.string()` | **dropped** — split into `lang_title` and `lang_obj` |
67	| `provider_id` | _(absent)_ | `pa.string()` — `provider-info.provider-ddb-id` |
68	| `dataset_id` | _(absent)_ | `pa.string()` — `properties.dataset-id` |
69	| `lang_title` | _(absent)_ | `pa.string()` — language tag of `ProvidedCHO.title` (e.g. `ger`, `eng`, `zxx`) |
70	| `lang_obj` | _(absent)_ | `pa.string()` — content language from `dc:language` / `dcterms:language` (e.g. `ger`, `eng`) |
71	| `dc_type` | `pa.string()` | `pa.list_(pa.string())` — labels only (see below) |
72	| `dc_date` | `pa.string()` | **dropped** — merged into `dates` |
73	| `dc_date_qualifier` | `pa.string()` | **dropped** — merged into `dates` |
74	| `dc_issued` | `pa.list_(pa.string())` | **dropped** — merged into `dates` |
75	| `dc_created` | `pa.list_(pa.string())` | **dropped** — merged into `dates` |
76	| `dates` | _(absent)_ | `pa.list_(struct(value: string, begin: string, end: string, type: string))` — new unified field |
77	| `dc_creator` | `pa.list_(struct(name: string, type: string))` | **dropped** — merged into `agents` |
78	| `dc_contributor` | `pa.list_(struct(name: string, type: string))` | **dropped** — merged into `agents` |
79	| `dc_publisher` | `pa.list_(struct(name: string, type: string))` | **dropped** — merged into `agents` |
80	| `agents` | `pa.list_(pa.string())` | `pa.list_(struct(name: string, type: string, is_ext_uri: bool))` — unified |
81	| `dc_subject` | `pa.list_(pa.string())` | `pa.list_(struct(name: string, is_ext_uri: bool))` — labels only (see below) |
82	| `dc_subject_uris` | `pa.list_(pa.string())` | **dropped** |
83	| `mediatype` | _(absent)_ | `pa.int16()` — new field |
84	| `sector` | _(absent)_ | `pa.int16()` — new field |
85	| `hierarchy_type` | `pa.string()` | `pa.int16()` |
86	
87	**`dc_type`**: corpus analysis shows values are a mix of plain literals (`"Fotoalbum"`,
88	`"Dokument"`) and authority URIs (Getty AAT, GND, Wikidata). The Parquet column stores
89	**literals only** — URIs are resolved to their `skos:prefLabel` via the same record's
90	`edm.RDF.Concept[]` block before storing.
91	
92	Extraction logic:
93	
94	1. Build a `concept_labels` dict from all `Concept[].about → prefLabel.$` in the record.
95	2. For each `dcType` entry:
96	   - Has `$` (plain literal) → use it directly.
97	   - Has `resource` (URI) and it resolves in `concept_labels` → use the label.
98	   - Has `resource` (URI) but **not** in `concept_labels` and is a **non-DDB URI**
99	     (i.e. does not start with `http://ddb.vocnet.org/`) → store `(uri, None)` in
100	     `concept_labels.duckdb` for later enrichment; omit from Parquet list for now.
101	3. DDB vocnet URIs (`http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/…`, `http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/…`)
102	   that appear in `dcType` are silently skipped — those are sector/mediatype codes
103	   captured in the `mediatype` column instead.
104	
105	Corpus sample results (1,000 records): 561 literal values, 231 URI values. Of the URIs,
106	Getty AAT and GND resolve via same-record Concept blocks; Wikidata URIs often do not
107	(no matching Concept entry in the record).
108	
109	```python
110	# build label index from Concept blocks
111	concepts = rdf.get("Concept") or []
112	if isinstance(concepts, dict): concepts = [concepts]
113	concept_labels: dict[str, str] = {}
114	for c in concepts:
115	    if not isinstance(c, dict): continue
116	    about = c.get("about") or ""
117	    pref = c.get("prefLabel")
118	    if isinstance(pref, list): pref = pref[0] if pref else None
119	    if isinstance(pref, dict): pref = pref.get("$", "")
120	    if about and pref:
121	        concept_labels[about] = pref
122	
123	# resolve dc_type values
124	dc_type_labels: list[str] = []
125	dc_type_raw = cho.get("dcType") or []
126	if not isinstance(dc_type_raw, list): dc_type_raw = [dc_type_raw]
127	for v in dc_type_raw:
128	    if isinstance(v, dict):
129	        res = v.get("resource")
130	        lit = v.get("$")
131	        if lit:
132	            dc_type_labels.append(lit)
133	        elif res and res not in (None, "None", ""):
134	            if res.startswith("http://ddb.vocnet.org/"):
135	                pass  # sector/mediatype code — skip
136	            elif res in concept_labels:
137	                dc_type_labels.append(concept_labels[res])
138	            else:
139	                concept_labels_pending[res] = None  # unresolvable → concept_labels.duckdb with NULL label
140	    elif isinstance(v, str) and v:
141	        dc_type_labels.append(v)
142	# same loop logic applies to dc_subject (dcSubject + dcTermsSubject + dcTermSubject),
143	# skipping bare 32-char DDB IDs (no http prefix) in addition to ddb.vocnet.org URIs
144	```
145	
146	**`mediatype`**: not in `properties.*`. It comes from `edm.RDF.Concept[].about` —
147	scan concept `about` values for the pattern `http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt*`
148	and extract the `mt*` code (last path segment). Take the first unique match; there
149	can be duplicates within a record (same URI repeated). Sample: `mt002`, `mt003`,
150	`mt001`, `mt005`, `mt007`.
151	
152	Extraction snippet:
153	```python
154	mediatype = ""
155	sector = ""
156	concepts = rdf.get("Concept") or []
157	if isinstance(concepts, dict):
158	    concepts = [concepts]
159	for c in concepts:
160	    if not isinstance(c, dict):
161	        continue
162	    about = c.get("about") or ""
163	    if not mediatype and "/medientyp/mt" in about:
164	        mediatype = int(about.rsplit("/mt", 1)[-1])      # "mt002" → 2
165	    if not sector and "/sparte/sparte" in about:
166	        sector = int(about.rsplit("/sparte", 1)[-1])     # "sparte005" → 5
167	
168	# hierarchy_type: strip "htype_" prefix, cast to int
169	raw_ht = (_scalar_values(cho.get("hierarchyType")) or [None])[0]
170	hierarchy_type = int(raw_ht.replace("htype_", "")) if raw_ht else None  # "htype_015" → 15
171	```
172	
173	**`concept_labels.duckdb`** — new DuckDB file written by prescan. Collects all
174	`(uri, label)` pairs seen in `dcType` and `dc_subject` source fields across the corpus,
175	for later lookup and enrichment.
176	
177	```sql
178	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS concept_labels (
179	    uri     VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
180	    label   VARCHAR          -- skos:prefLabel resolved from same-record Concept block;
181	                             -- NULL if not resolvable within the record
182	)
183	```
184	
185	**What goes in**: all non-DDB-internal URIs from `dcType`, `dcSubject`, `dcTermsSubject`,
186	and `dcTermSubject` (the last two are typo variants of the same field, both present in
187	the corpus). Excluded: DDB vocnet URIs (`http://ddb.vocnet.org/…`) and bare 32-character
188	DDB-internal IDs (e.g. `YUVZR2OFQCZ5HKUOCBWXTII5WN6DCLOK`).
189	
190	**`dcTermSubject` note**: a misspelling of `dcterms:subject` present in 590/1,000
191	sampled records (alongside `dcTermsSubject`). Both fields contain the same structure —
192	bare DDB IDs and GND URIs — and are scanned together. Bare IDs are excluded from
193	`concept_labels.duckdb` (entity resolution problem, not a label lookup problem).
194	
195	**Populated via** `INSERT OR IGNORE (uri, label)` for every qualifying URI encountered,
196	where `label` is the `skos:prefLabel` resolved from the same record's `Concept[]`
197	block (or `NULL` if no matching Concept exists — e.g. some Wikidata URIs lack a
198	Concept entry). `INSERT OR IGNORE` means the first non-null label seen wins; a later
199	enrichment pass can fill in nulls via authority endpoint queries (Getty AAT SPARQL,
200	Wikidata API, lobid-gnd).
201	
202	**Parquet impact**: `dc_type` and `dc_subject` lists in Parquet contain only resolved
203	labels. Values with NULL labels are omitted from Parquet but remain in
204	`concept_labels.duckdb` for enrichment. `dc_subject_uris` column is **dropped**
205	(superseded by `concept_labels.duckdb`).
206	
207	**`agent_labels.duckdb`** — new DuckDB file, parallel to `concept_labels.duckdb`,
208	for `dc_creator`, `dc_contributor`, `dc_publisher` agent URIs.
209	
210	```sql
211	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agent_labels (
212	    uri     VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
213	    label   VARCHAR          -- from $-field of the same dict; rarely NULL for agents
214	)
215	```
216	
217	**What goes in**: HTTP URIs from creator/contributor/publisher dicts (primarily GND:
218	`http://d-nb.info/gnd/…`). Bare 32-char DDB IDs are not observed in agent fields
219	(corpus sample: 0 bare IDs across 200 records); if encountered they are excluded.
220	
221	**Key difference from `concept_labels`**: no Concept block lookup needed — the label
222	is already inline in the same `{'resource': uri, '$': label}` dict. Extract both in
223	one pass:
224	
225	```python
226	agent_labels_pending: dict[str, str | None] = {}
227	agent_label_list: list[str] = []
228	
229	val = cho.get("creator") or []
230	if isinstance(val, dict): val = [val]
231	for v in val:
232	    if not isinstance(v, dict): continue
233	    res = v.get("resource"); lit = v.get("$") or ""
234	    if lit:
235	        agent_label_list.append(lit)
236	    if res and res not in (None, "None", "") and res.startswith("http"):
237	        agent_labels_pending[res] = lit or None  # → agent_labels.duckdb
238	# same loop for contributor and publisher
239	```
240	
241	`INSERT OR IGNORE (uri, label)` — first label seen wins. A later enrichment pass can
242	resolve NULLs via lobid-gnd or Wikidata.
243	
244	**Unified `agents` column** — replaces the current `dc_creator`, `dc_contributor`,
245	`dc_publisher`, and `agents` (LIDO participants) columns with a single
246	`pa.list_(pa.struct([pa.field("name", pa.string()), pa.field("type", pa.string()), pa.field("is_ext_uri", pa.bool_())]))`.
247	
248	Type vocabulary — noun form throughout for consistency with LIDO labels:
249	
250	| Source | `type` value |
251	|---|---|
252	| `ProvidedCHO.creator` | `creation` |
253	| `ProvidedCHO.publisher` | `publication` |
254	| `ProvidedCHO.contributor` | `contribution` |
255	| `hasMet → P11_had_participant` | LIDO label from `lido_event_types.csv` (`creation`, `publication`, `production`, `photography`, `performance`, `provenance`, `designing`, `commissioning`, `unknown_event`, …) |
256	
257	`dc:contributor` uses `contribution` (not `contributor`) to follow the noun pattern.
258	It has no direct LIDO equivalent — it is the generic `dc_agent_fallback` for most
259	LIDO event types and does not derive from event traversal.
260	
261	**Unified `dates` column** — replaces `dc_date`, `dc_date_qualifier`, `dc_issued`,
262	`dc_created` with a single struct list:
263	
264	```python
265	DATE_STRUCT = pa.struct([
266	    pa.field("value", pa.string()),  # set for point-in-time; null for ranges
267	    pa.field("begin", pa.string()),  # set for ranges; null for point-in-time
268	    pa.field("end",   pa.string()),  # set for ranges; null for point-in-time
269	    pa.field("type",  pa.string()),  # event-type label (noun form)
270	])
271	
272	("dates", pa.list_(DATE_STRUCT))
273	```
274	
275	Type vocabulary — same noun pattern as agents:
276	
277	| Source | `type` value |
278	|---|---|
279	| `ProvidedCHO.created` + LIDO creation event chain (`lido00012`, `eventType/creation`) | `creation` |
280	| `ProvidedCHO.issued` + LIDO publication event chain (`lido00228`, `eventType/publication`) | `publication` |
281	| `ProvidedCHO.date` (direct field, no LIDO traversal) | `unknown_event` |
282	
283	`dc_date_qualifier` (e.g. `circa`, `before`, `after`) is dropped for now — can be
284	added as a third struct field `qualifier` if needed later.
285	
286	### 2.4 Files to update for Parquet schema change
287	
288	| File | Change |
289	|---|---|
290	| `gemea/scripts/py/export_ddb.py` | `PARQUET_SCHEMA`: `dc_type` → list, add `mediatype`; `extract_meta` return dict: `dc_type` → full list, add `mediatype` extraction |
291	| `gemea/scripts/py/export_ddb_parquet.py` | `extract_meta` return dict: same two changes (this file re-implements `extract_meta` with dc_created/dc_issued fixes) |
292	
293	### 2.5 DuckDB schema written by prescan
294	
295	```sql
296	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS emitted_entities (
297	    uri         VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
298	    entity_type VARCHAR NOT NULL
299	)
300	```
301	
302	Populated rows after prescan (entity_type values):
303	
304	| entity_type | Example URI |
305	|---|---|
306	| `prov_xslt` | `urn:ddbedm:xslt:6.18` |
307	| `prov_dataset` | `urn:ddbedm:dataset:<id>` |
308	| `prov_provider` | `urn:ddbedm:provider:<id>` |
309	| `prov_ddb` | `http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de` |
310	
311	### 2.6 Output: `prov-shared.nq`
312	
313	Contains the descriptive triples for all unique PROV-O shared nodes, emitted once,
314	as N-Quads with the named graph `https://gemea.ise.fiz-karlsruhe.de/graph/prov` as
315	the fourth element on every line. Processed by `split_nq.py` in Phase 4 alongside
316	the per-chunk `.nq` files; its `prov` slug merges into `$NT_DIR/prov.nt`.
317	
318	---
319	
320	## 3. Pass 2 — changes to `__main__.py`
321	
322	Replace the current entities DB block (load + write-back via persistent connection)
323	with a read-only load-and-close:
324	
325	```python
326	emitted_entities: dict[str, str] = {}
327	if args.prov_db:
328	    try:
329	        import duckdb as _ddb_ent
330	        _c = _ddb_ent.connect(str(args.prov_db), read_only=True)
331	        rows = _c.execute(
332	            "SELECT uri, entity_type FROM emitted_entities"
333	        ).fetchall()
334	        _c.close()
335	        emitted_entities = {uri: etype for uri, etype in rows}
336	        log.info("Loaded %d emitted entities from %s",
337	                 len(emitted_entities), args.prov_db)
338	    except ImportError:
339	        log.warning("duckdb not available — --prov-db ignored")
340	    except Exception as exc:
341	        log.warning("Could not read --prov-db %s: %s", args.prov_db, exc)
342	```
343	
344	**Remove entirely**: the write-back block (lines 400–406) and the `entities_conn`
345	variable. Workers never write to the entities DB.
346	
347	---
348	
349	## 4. Changes to `run_gemea_transform.sh`
350	
351	### 4.0 New CLI arguments
352	
353	```
354	--prov-db PATH           Shared PROV-O entities DuckDB (default: $OUT_BASE/prov.duckdb)
355	--concept-labels-db PATH concept_labels.duckdb (default: $OUT_BASE/concept_labels.duckdb)
356	--agent-labels-db PATH   agent_labels.duckdb   (default: $OUT_BASE/agent_labels.duckdb)
357	--parquet-dir PATH       Output dir for per-sector Parquet files (default: $OUT_BASE/parquet/)
358	--parquet-merge PATH     If set, append all sector Parquet rows into one combined file at PATH
359	                         (written after all prescan workers finish; uses pyarrow ParquetWriter)
360	--replace-dbs            Wipe and rebuild the three label DuckDB files and prov-shared.nq
361	                         before prescan; without this flag, prescan appends (INSERT OR IGNORE)
362	```
363	
364	`--prov-db`, `--concept-labels-db`, and `--agent-labels-db` may live **outside**
365	`$OUT_BASE` to persist across versioned runs. `--parquet-dir` similarly allows per-sector
366	Parquet files to accumulate in a stable location.
367	
368	`--parquet-merge` is additive — it reads all per-sector files from `--parquet-dir`
369	after the prescan phase and concatenates them into one file, appending if the file
370	already exists. Useful for building a single corpus-wide Parquet across incremental runs.
371	
372	`--replace-dbs` wipes only the label DBs and `prov-shared.nq`; it does not affect
373	`$OUT_BASE` sector output. The existing `--new` flag wipes `$OUT_BASE` entirely.
374	
375	### 4.1 Add prescan phase (before the sector loop)
376	
377	```bash
378	PROV_DB=${PROV_DB_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/prov.duckdb}
379	CONCEPT_LABELS_DB=${CONCEPT_LABELS_DB_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/concept_labels.duckdb}
380	AGENT_LABELS_DB=${AGENT_LABELS_DB_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/agent_labels.duckdb}
381	PARQUET_DIR=${PARQUET_DIR_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/parquet}
382	PROV_SHARED=$OUT_BASE/prov-shared.nq
383	
384	mkdir -p "$PARQUET_DIR"
385	
386	if [[ "$REPLACE_DBS" == "1" ]]; then
387	  rm -f "$PROV_DB" "$CONCEPT_LABELS_DB" "$AGENT_LABELS_DB" "$PROV_SHARED"
388	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] --replace-dbs: wiped label DBs and prov-shared.nq"
389	fi
390	
391	for n in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7; do
392	  (
393	    echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] prescan starting"
394	    "$PYTHON" "$SCRIPTS/transform/prescan.py" \
395	      --db                "$SQLITE_DIR/s${n}.sqlite" \
396	      --prov-db           "$PROV_DB" \
397	      --concept-labels-db "$CONCEPT_LABELS_DB" \
398	      --agent-labels-db   "$AGENT_LABELS_DB" \
399	      --prov-out          "$PROV_SHARED" \
400	      --parquet-out       "$PARQUET_DIR/s${n}_meta.parquet"
401	    echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] prescan done"
402	  ) &
403	done
404	wait
405	
406	# optional merge into one combined Parquet
407	if [[ -n "$PARQUET_MERGE_ARG" ]]; then
408	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Merging per-sector Parquet → $PARQUET_MERGE_ARG"
409	  "$PYTHON" "$SCRIPTS/transform/merge_parquet.py" \
410	    --indir  "$PARQUET_DIR" \
411	    --out    "$PARQUET_MERGE_ARG"
412	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Parquet merge done"
413	fi
414	```
415	
416	Prescans run **in parallel** (one background process per sector, same as the main
417	transform loop). Each worker acquires an exclusive `fcntl.flock` lock on
418	`$PROV_DB.lock` before writing to the shared `entities.duckdb` and appending to
419	`prov-shared.nq`, then releases immediately. Since each write batch is small (one
420	sector's unique PROV-O URIs, typically tens to low hundreds of rows), lock contention
421	is negligible.
422	
423	Locking pattern inside `prescan.py` (write phase only; scan phase is lock-free):
424	
425	```python
426	import fcntl
427	
428	lock_path = str(prov_db_path) + ".lock"
429	with open(lock_path, "a") as lf:
430	    fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)
431	    try:
432	        # write entities.duckdb
433	        conn = duckdb.connect(str(prov_db_path))
434	        conn.executemany("INSERT OR IGNORE INTO emitted_entities VALUES (?, ?)",
435	                         list(new_entities.items()))
436	        conn.executemany("INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dctype_labels VALUES (?, ?)",
437	                         list(new_dctype_labels.items()))
438	        conn.close()
439	        # append prov-shared.nq
440	        with open(prov_out_path, "a") as pf:
441	            pf.writelines(new_prov_lines)
442	    finally:
443	        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)
444	```
445	
446	All heavy work (scanning, extracting, building `new_entities` / `new_prov_lines` /
447	Parquet rows) happens before the lock is acquired. The lock is held only for the
448	final DB insert + file append.
449	
450	### 4.2 Pass `--prov-db` to all transform workers
451	
452	Add `--prov-db "$PROV_DB"` to both the single-worker and multi-worker
453	transform invocations.
454	
455	### 4.3 Include `prov-shared.nq` in Phase 4 NT merge
456	
457	In the Phase 4 merge loop, ensure `prov-shared.nq` is split and included:
458	
459	```bash
460	"$PYTHON" "$SCRIPTS/split_nq.py" "$PROV_SHARED" &
461	```
462	
463	Then the `prov.nt` merge will include the shared-node triples alongside the
464	per-chunk prov NT files.
465	
466	### 4.4 Update `--new` wipe
467	
468	```bash
469	rm -rf "$OUT_BASE"/s{1..7} "$OUT_BASE/nt" \
470	       "$OUT_BASE/werk-staging-merged.duckdb" \
471	       "$OUT_BASE/entities.duckdb" \
472	       "$OUT_BASE/prov-shared.nq"
473	```
474	
475	---
476	
477	## 5. Files to create / modify
478	
479	| File | Action |
480	|---|---|
481	| `scripts/transform/prescan.py` | **Create** — single-pass prescan + Parquet build |
482	| `scripts/transform/__main__.py` | **Modify** — read-only entities load, remove write-back |
483	| `scripts/run_gemea_transform.sh` | **Modify** — add prescan phase, pass `--prov-db`, include `prov-shared.nq` in Phase 4 |
484	| `gemea/scripts/py/export_ddb.py` | **Modify** — `dc_type`/`dc_creator`/`dc_contributor`/`dc_publisher` → `list_(string)` labels only; `dc_subject` → labels only; drop `dc_subject_uris`; `hierarchy_type`/`mediatype`/`sector` → `int16`; URI→label resolution + `concept_labels.duckdb` + `agent_labels.duckdb` writes in `extract_meta` |
485	| `gemea/scripts/py/export_ddb_parquet.py` | **Modify** — same changes in its `extract_meta` |
486

495	- `test_dc_type_list`: assert `extract_meta` returns a list for `dc_type`, not a

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - PROV-O Layer 1 (Without-Activity pattern) — 4 shared node types per sector
   - URN short-prefix collision guard: `urn:ddbedm:dataset:X` vs `urn:ddbedm:X` are unambiguous because DDB IDs are purely alphanumeric (no colons)
   - DuckDB single-writer constraint — concurrent workers cannot share one write connection
   - Two-pass architecture: Pass 1 (prescan, sequential scan) pre-populates all shared DBs; Pass 2 (transform workers) reads DBs read-only with no write-back
   - `fcntl.flock` for cross-process file locking (lock held only during final write flush)
   - `INSERT OR IGNORE` for idempotent DuckDB writes
   - Parquet struct columns (`pa.struct`) for unified agents and dates
   - `is_ext_uri: bool` per struct element supersedes a separate `ext_uris_count` column
   - Entity resolution vs. triple deduplication distinction: bare DDB IDs for same entity → different RDF nodes → entity resolution problem, not transform-layer fix
   - LIDO event type labels as agent/date type vocabulary (noun form: `creation`, `publication`, `contribution`, `photography`, etc.)
   - TimeSpan begin/end: 707 point-in-time (same), 155 ranges (differ) → `value IS NOT NULL` signals point-in-time without redundant boolean
   - `dcTermSubject` (typo of `dcterms:subject`, present in 590/1000 sampled records) must be scanned alongside `dcTermsSubject`

```
1	# Transform prescan — two-pass architecture plan
2	
3	**Date**: 2026-05-14
4	**Status**: Pending — context captured for a later session
5	**Relates to**: `scripts/transform/__main__.py`, `scripts/run_gemea_transform.sh`,
6	               `gemea/scripts/py/export_ddb.py`, `gemea/scripts/py/export_ddb_parquet.py`
7	
8	---
9	
10	## 1. Motivation
11	
12	`--prov-db` was added (2026-05-13, `transform-implementation-actual.md §11`) to
13	deduplicate shared PROV-O node descriptive triples across sector runs. The current
14	design loads the DB at startup and writes back after the record loop — which breaks
15	under concurrent workers (DuckDB single-writer constraint).
16	
17	The fix is a two-pass architecture:
18	
19	- **Pass 1 (prescan)**: single-process scan that pre-populates `entities.duckdb` and
20	  builds the Parquet metadata file. No transform, no concurrency.
21	- **Pass 2 (main transform)**: N concurrent workers load `entities.duckdb` read-only
22	  at startup and never write to it. No locking needed.
23	
24	---
25	
26	## 2. Pass 1 — `scripts/transform/prescan.py`
27	
28	### 2.1 Inputs
29	
30	| Arg | Description |
31	|---|---|
32	| `--db PATH` | Sector SQLite file (`objs` table, `bufgz` column) |
33	| `--prov-db PATH` | Output DuckDB for `emitted_entities` table |
34	| `--prov-out PATH` | Output `.nq` file for shared PROV-O descriptive triples |
35	| `--parquet-out PATH` | Output `<stem>_meta.parquet` |
36	
37	### 2.2 What it does
38	
39	**Single scan** of the SQLite file (sequential, one process):
40	
41	1. For each record, extract:
42	   - `properties.mapping-version` → `urn:ddbedm:xslt:<ver>`
43	   - `properties.dataset-id` → `urn:ddbedm:dataset:<id>`
44	   - `provider-info.provider-ddb-id` → `urn:ddbedm:provider:<id>`
45	   - Fixed: `DDB_BASE = "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de"`
46	   - Parquet metadata row (see §2.3)
47	
48	2. On first encounter of each PROV-O URI, emit its descriptive triples to
49	   `prov-shared.nq` via `emit_prov_triples` and register the URI in a local set.
50	
51	3. After the loop:
52	   - Write all unique PROV-O URIs to `entities.duckdb` (`emitted_entities` table)
53	   - Flush the Parquet writer
54	
55	**No edm:Agent / Place / Concept / TimeSpan in Pass 1** — those are deduplicated by
56	`sort -u` at NT merge time (Pass 2 produces per-chunk NT files; Phase 4 merges with
57	`sort -u`).
58	
59	### 2.3 Parquet schema changes (also applies to `export_ddb.py` and `export_ddb_parquet.py`)
60	
61	Changes to `PARQUET_SCHEMA` (defined in `gemea/scripts/py/export_ddb.py`,
62	imported by `export_ddb_parquet.py`):
63	
64	| Field | Current | New |
65	|---|---|---|
66	| `lang` | `pa.string()` | **dropped** — split into `lang_title` and `lang_obj` |
67	| `provider_id` | _(absent)_ | `pa.string()` — `provider-info.provider-ddb-id` |
68	| `dataset_id` | _(absent)_ | `pa.string()` — `properties.dataset-id` |
69	| `lang_title` | _(absent)_ | `pa.string()` — language tag of `ProvidedCHO.title` (e.g. `ger`, `eng`, `zxx`) |
70	| `lang_obj` | _(absent)_ | `pa.string()` — content language from `dc:language` / `dcterms:language` (e.g. `ger`, `eng`) |
71	| `dc_type` | `pa.string()` | `pa.list_(pa.string())` — labels only (see below) |
72	| `dc_date` | `pa.string()` | **dropped** — merged into `dates` |
73	| `dc_date_qualifier` | `pa.string()` | **dropped** — merged into `dates` |
74	| `dc_issued` | `pa.list_(pa.string())` | **dropped** — merged into `dates` |
75	| `dc_created` | `pa.list_(pa.string())` | **dropped** — merged into `dates` |
76	| `dates` | _(absent)_ | `pa.list_(struct(value: string, begin: string, end: string, type: string))` — new unified field |
77	| `dc_creator` | `pa.list_(struct(name: string, type: string))` | **dropped** — merged into `agents` |
78	| `dc_contributor` | `pa.list_(struct(name: string, type: string))` | **dropped** — merged into `agents` |
79	| `dc_publisher` | `pa.list_(struct(name: string, type: string))` | **dropped** — merged into `agents` |
80	| `agents` | `pa.list_(pa.string())` | `pa.list_(struct(name: string, type: string, is_ext_uri: bool))` — unified |
81	| `dc_subject` | `pa.list_(pa.string())` | `pa.list_(struct(name: string, is_ext_uri: bool))` — labels only (see below) |
82	| `dc_subject_uris` | `pa.list_(pa.string())` | **dropped** |
83	| `mediatype` | _(absent)_ | `pa.int16()` — new field |
84	| `sector` | _(absent)_ | `pa.int16()` — new field |
85	| `hierarchy_type` | `pa.string()` | `pa.int16()` |
86	
87	**`dc_type`**: corpus analysis shows values are a mix of plain literals (`"Fotoalbum"`,
88	`"Dokument"`) and authority URIs (Getty AAT, GND, Wikidata). The Parquet column stores
89	**literals only** — URIs are resolved to their `skos:prefLabel` via the same record's
90	`edm.RDF.Concept[]` block before storing.
91	
92	Extraction logic:
93	
94	1. Build a `concept_labels` dict from all `Concept[].about → prefLabel.$` in the record.
95	2. For each `dcType` entry:
96	   - Has `$` (plain literal) → use it directly.
97	   - Has `resource` (URI) and it resolves in `concept_labels` → use the label.
98	   - Has `resource` (URI) but **not** in `concept_labels` and is a **non-DDB URI**
99	     (i.e. does not start with `http://ddb.vocnet.org/`) → store `(uri, None)` in
100	     `concept_labels.duckdb` for later enrichment; omit from Parquet list for now.
101	3. DDB vocnet URIs (`http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/…`, `http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/…`)
102	   that appear in `dcType` are silently skipped — those are sector/mediatype codes
103	   captured in the `mediatype` column instead.
104	
105	Corpus sample results (1,000 records): 561 literal values, 231 URI values. Of the URIs,
106	Getty AAT and GND resolve via same-record Concept blocks; Wikidata URIs often do not
107	(no matching Concept entry in the record).
108	
109	```python
110	# build label index from Concept blocks
111	concepts = rdf.get("Concept") or []
112	if isinstance(concepts, dict): concepts = [concepts]
113	concept_labels: dict[str, str] = {}
114	for c in concepts:
115	    if not isinstance(c, dict): continue
116	    about = c.get("about") or ""
117	    pref = c.get("prefLabel")
118	    if isinstance(pref, list): pref = pref[0] if pref else None
119	    if isinstance(pref, dict): pref = pref.get("$", "")
120	    if about and pref:
121	        concept_labels[about] = pref
122	
123	# resolve dc_type values
124	dc_type_labels: list[str] = []
125	dc_type_raw = cho.get("dcType") or []
126	if not isinstance(dc_type_raw, list): dc_type_raw = [dc_type_raw]
127	for v in dc_type_raw:
128	    if isinstance(v, dict):
129	        res = v.get("resource")
130	        lit = v.get("$")
131	        if lit:
132	            dc_type_labels.append(lit)
133	        elif res and res not in (None, "None", ""):
134	            if res.startswith("http://ddb.vocnet.org/"):
135	                pass  # sector/mediatype code — skip
136	            elif res in concept_labels:
137	                dc_type_labels.append(concept_labels[res])
138	            else:
139	                concept_labels_pending[res] = None  # unresolvable → concept_labels.duckdb with NULL label
140	    elif isinstance(v, str) and v:
141	        dc_type_labels.append(v)
142	# same loop logic applies to dc_subject (dcSubject + dcTermsSubject + dcTermSubject),
143	# skipping bare 32-char DDB IDs (no http prefix) in addition to ddb.vocnet.org URIs
144	```
145	
146	**`mediatype`**: not in `properties.*`. It comes from `edm.RDF.Concept[].about` —
147	scan concept `about` values for the pattern `http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt*`
148	and extract the `mt*` code (last path segment). Take the first unique match; there
149	can be duplicates within a record (same URI repeated). Sample: `mt002`, `mt003`,
150	`mt001`, `mt005`, `mt007`.
151	
152	Extraction snippet:
153	```python
154	mediatype = ""
155	sector = ""
156	concepts = rdf.get("Concept") or []
157	if isinstance(concepts, dict):
158	    concepts = [concepts]
159	for c in concepts:
160	    if not isinstance(c, dict):
161	        continue
162	    about = c.get("about") or ""
163	    if not mediatype and "/medientyp/mt" in about:
164	        mediatype = int(about.rsplit("/mt", 1)[-1])      # "mt002" → 2
165	    if not sector and "/sparte/sparte" in about:
166	        sector = int(about.rsplit("/sparte", 1)[-1])     # "sparte005" → 5
167	
168	# hierarchy_type: strip "htype_" prefix, cast to int
169	raw_ht = (_scalar_values(cho.get("hierarchyType")) or [None])[0]
170	hierarchy_type = int(raw_ht.replace("htype_", "")) if raw_ht else None  # "htype_015" → 15
171	```
172	
173	**`concept_labels.duckdb`** — new DuckDB file written by prescan. Collects all
174	`(uri, label)` pairs seen in `dcType` and `dc_subject` source fields across the corpus,
175	for later lookup and enrichment.
176	
177	```sql
178	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS concept_labels (
179	    uri     VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
180	    label   VARCHAR          -- skos:prefLabel resolved from same-record Concept block;
181	                             -- NULL if not resolvable within the record
182	)
183	```
184	
185	**What goes in**: all non-DDB-internal URIs from `dcType`, `dcSubject`, `dcTermsSubject`,
186	and `dcTermSubject` (the last two are typo variants of the same field, both present in
187	the corpus). Excluded: DDB vocnet URIs (`http://ddb.vocnet.org/…`) and bare 32-character
188	DDB-internal IDs (e.g. `YUVZR2OFQCZ5HKUOCBWXTII5WN6DCLOK`).
189	
190	**`dcTermSubject` note**: a misspelling of `dcterms:subject` present in 590/1,000
191	sampled records (alongside `dcTermsSubject`). Both fields contain the same structure —
192	bare DDB IDs and GND URIs — and are scanned together. Bare IDs are excluded from
193	`concept_labels.duckdb` (entity resolution problem, not a label lookup problem).
194	
195	**Populated via** `INSERT OR IGNORE (uri, label)` for every qualifying URI encountered,
196	where `label` is the `skos:prefLabel` resolved from the same record's `Concept[]`
197	block (or `NULL` if no matching Concept exists — e.g. some Wikidata URIs lack a
198	Concept entry). `INSERT OR IGNORE` means the first non-null label seen wins; a later
199	enrichment pass can fill in nulls via authority endpoint queries (Getty AAT SPARQL,
200	Wikidata API, lobid-gnd).
201	
202	**Parquet impact**: `dc_type` and `dc_subject` lists in Parquet contain only resolved
203	labels. Values with NULL labels are omitted from Parquet but remain in
204	`concept_labels.duckdb` for enrichment. `dc_subject_uris` column is **dropped**
205	(superseded by `concept_labels.duckdb`).
206	
207	**`agent_labels.duckdb`** — new DuckDB file, parallel to `concept_labels.duckdb`,
208	for `dc_creator`, `dc_contributor`, `dc_publisher` agent URIs.
209	
210	```sql
211	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agent_labels (
212	    uri     VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
213	    label   VARCHAR          -- from $-field of the same dict; rarely NULL for agents
214	)
215	```
216	
217	**What goes in**: HTTP URIs from creator/contributor/publisher dicts (primarily GND:
218	`http://d-nb.info/gnd/…`). Bare 32-char DDB IDs are not observed in agent fields
219	(corpus sample: 0 bare IDs across 200 records); if encountered they are excluded.
220	
221	**Key difference from `concept_labels`**: no Concept block lookup needed — the label
222	is already inline in the same `{'resource': uri, '$': label}` dict. Extract both in
223	one pass:
224	
225	```python
226	agent_labels_pending: dict[str, str | None] = {}
227	agent_label_list: list[str] = []
228	
229	val = cho.get("creator") or []
230	if isinstance(val, dict): val = [val]
231	for v in val:
232	    if not isinstance(v, dict): continue
233	    res = v.get("resource"); lit = v.get("$") or ""
234	    if lit:
235	        agent_label_list.append(lit)
236	    if res and res not in (None, "None", "") and res.startswith("http"):
237	        agent_labels_pending[res] = lit or None  # → agent_labels.duckdb
238	# same loop for contributor and publisher
239	```
240	
241	`INSERT OR IGNORE (uri, label)` — first label seen wins. A later enrichment pass can
242	resolve NULLs via lobid-gnd or Wikidata.
243	
244	**Unified `agents` column** — replaces the current `dc_creator`, `dc_contributor`,
245	`dc_publisher`, and `agents` (LIDO participants) columns with a single
246	`pa.list_(pa.struct([pa.field("name", pa.string()), pa.field("type", pa.string()), pa.field("is_ext_uri", pa.bool_())]))`.
247	
248	Type vocabulary — noun form throughout for consistency with LIDO labels:
249	
250	| Source | `type` value |
251	|---|---|
252	| `ProvidedCHO.creator` | `creation` |
253	| `ProvidedCHO.publisher` | `publication` |
254	| `ProvidedCHO.contributor` | `contribution` |
255	| `hasMet → P11_had_participant` | LIDO label from `lido_event_types.csv` (`creation`, `publication`, `production`, `photography`, `performance`, `provenance`, `designing`, `commissioning`, `unknown_event`, …) |
256	
257	`dc:contributor` uses `contribution` (not `contributor`) to follow the noun pattern.
258	It has no direct LIDO equivalent — it is the generic `dc_agent_fallback` for most
259	LIDO event types and does not derive from event traversal.
260	
261	**Unified `dates` column** — replaces `dc_date`, `dc_date_qualifier`, `dc_issued`,
262	`dc_created` with a single struct list:
263	
264	```python
265	DATE_STRUCT = pa.struct([
266	    pa.field("value", pa.string()),  # set for point-in-time; null for ranges
267	    pa.field("begin", pa.string()),  # set for ranges; null for point-in-time
268	    pa.field("end",   pa.string()),  # set for ranges; null for point-in-time
269	    pa.field("type",  pa.string()),  # event-type label (noun form)
270	])
271	
272	("dates", pa.list_(DATE_STRUCT))
273	```
274	
275	Type vocabulary — same noun pattern as agents:
276	
277	| Source | `type` value |
278	|---|---|
279	| `ProvidedCHO.created` + LIDO creation event chain (`lido00012`, `eventType/creation`) | `creation` |
280	| `ProvidedCHO.issued` + LIDO publication event chain (`lido00228`, `eventType/publication`) | `publication` |
281	| `ProvidedCHO.date` (direct field, no LIDO traversal) | `unknown_event` |
282	
283	`dc_date_qualifier` (e.g. `circa`, `before`, `after`) is dropped for now — can be
284	added as a third struct field `qualifier` if needed later.
285	
286	### 2.4 Files to update for Parquet schema change
287	
288	| File | Change |
289	|---|---|
290	| `gemea/scripts/py/export_ddb.py` | `PARQUET_SCHEMA`: `dc_type` → list, add `mediatype`; `extract_meta` return dict: `dc_type` → full list, add `mediatype` extraction |
291	| `gemea/scripts/py/export_ddb_parquet.py` | `extract_meta` return dict: same two changes (this file re-implements `extract_meta` with dc_created/dc_issued fixes) |
292	
293	### 2.5 DuckDB schema written by prescan
294	
295	```sql
296	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS emitted_entities (
297	    uri         VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
298	    entity_type VARCHAR NOT NULL
299	)
300	```
301	
302	Populated rows after prescan (entity_type values):
303	
304	| entity_type | Example URI |
305	|---|---|
306	| `prov_xslt` | `urn:ddbedm:xslt:6.18` |
307	| `prov_dataset` | `urn:ddbedm:dataset:<id>` |
308	| `prov_provider` | `urn:ddbedm:provider:<id>` |
309	| `prov_ddb` | `http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de` |
310	
311	### 2.6 Output: `prov-shared.nq`
312	
313	Contains the descriptive triples for all unique PROV-O shared nodes, emitted once,
314	as N-Quads with the named graph `https://gemea.ise.fiz-karlsruhe.de/graph/prov` as
315	the fourth element on every line. Processed by `split_nq.py` in Phase 4 alongside
316	the per-chunk `.nq` files; its `prov` slug merges into `$NT_DIR/prov.nt`.
317	
318	---
319	
320	## 3. Pass 2 — changes to `__main__.py`
321	
322	Replace the current entities DB block (load + write-back via persistent connection)
323	with a read-only load-and-close:
324	
325	```python
326	emitted_entities: dict[str, str] = {}
327	if args.prov_db:
328	    try:
329	        import duckdb as _ddb_ent
330	        _c = _ddb_ent.connect(str(args.prov_db), read_only=True)
331	        rows = _c.execute(
332	            "SELECT uri, entity_type FROM emitted_entities"
333	        ).fetchall()
334	        _c.close()
335	        emitted_entities = {uri: etype for uri, etype in rows}
336	        log.info("Loaded %d emitted entities from %s",
337	                 len(emitted_entities), args.prov_db)
338	    except ImportError:
339	        log.warning("duckdb not available — --prov-db ignored")
340	    except Exception as exc:
341	        log.warning("Could not read --prov-db %s: %s", args.prov_db, exc)
342	```
343	
344	**Remove entirely**: the write-back block (lines 400–406) and the `entities_conn`
345	variable. Workers never write to the entities DB.
346	
347	---
348	
349	## 4. Changes to `run_gemea_transform.sh`
350	
351	### 4.0 New CLI arguments
352	
353	```
354	--prov-db PATH           Shared PROV-O entities DuckDB (default: $OUT_BASE/prov.duckdb)
355	--concept-labels-db PATH concept_labels.duckdb (default: $OUT_BASE/concept_labels.duckdb)
356	--agent-labels-db PATH   agent_labels.duckdb   (default: $OUT_BASE/agent_labels.duckdb)
357	--parquet-dir PATH       Output dir for per-sector Parquet files (default: $OUT_BASE/parquet/)
358	--parquet-merge PATH     If set, append all sector Parquet rows into one combined file at PATH
359	                         (written after all prescan workers finish; uses pyarrow ParquetWriter)
360	--replace-dbs            Wipe and rebuild the three label DuckDB files and prov-shared.nq
361	                         before prescan; without this flag, prescan appends (INSERT OR IGNORE)
362	```
363	
364	`--prov-db`, `--concept-labels-db`, and `--agent-labels-db` may live **outside**
365	`$OUT_BASE` to persist across versioned runs. `--parquet-dir` similarly allows per-sector
366	Parquet files to accumulate in a stable location.
367	
368	`--parquet-merge` is additive — it reads all per-sector files from `--parquet-dir`
369	after the prescan phase and concatenates them into one file, appending if the file
370	already exists. Useful for building a single corpus-wide Parquet across incremental runs.
371	
372	`--replace-dbs` wipes only the label DBs and `prov-shared.nq`; it does not affect
373	`$OUT_BASE` sector output. The existing `--new` flag wipes `$OUT_BASE` entirely.
374	
375	### 4.1 Add prescan phase (before the sector loop)
376	
377	```bash
378	PROV_DB=${PROV_DB_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/prov.duckdb}
379	CONCEPT_LABELS_DB=${CONCEPT_LABELS_DB_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/concept_labels.duckdb}
380	AGENT_LABELS_DB=${AGENT_LABELS_DB_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/agent_labels.duckdb}
381	PARQUET_DIR=${PARQUET_DIR_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/parquet}
382	PROV_SHARED=$OUT_BASE/prov-shared.nq
383	
384	mkdir -p "$PARQUET_DIR"
385	
386	if [[ "$REPLACE_DBS" == "1" ]]; then
387	  rm -f "$PROV_DB" "$CONCEPT_LABELS_DB" "$AGENT_LABELS_DB" "$PROV_SHARED"
388	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] --replace-dbs: wiped label DBs and prov-shared.nq"
389	fi
390	
391	for n in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7; do
392	  (
393	    echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] prescan starting"
394	    "$PYTHON" "$SCRIPTS/transform/prescan.py" \
395	      --db                "$SQLITE_DIR/s${n}.sqlite" \
396	      --prov-db           "$PROV_DB" \
397	      --concept-labels-db "$CONCEPT_LABELS_DB" \
398	      --agent-labels-db   "$AGENT_LABELS_DB" \
399	      --prov-out          "$PROV_SHARED" \
400	      --parquet-out       "$PARQUET_DIR/s${n}_meta.parquet"
401	    echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] prescan done"
402	  ) &
403	done
404	wait
405	
406	# optional merge into one combined Parquet
407	if [[ -n "$PARQUET_MERGE_ARG" ]]; then
408	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Merging per-sector Parquet → $PARQUET_MERGE_ARG"
409	  "$PYTHON" "$SCRIPTS/transform/merge_parquet.py" \
410	    --indir  "$PARQUET_DIR" \
411	    --out    "$PARQUET_MERGE_ARG"
412	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Parquet merge done"
413	fi
414	```
415	
416	Prescans run **in parallel** (one background process per sector, same as the main
417	transform loop). Each worker acquires an exclusive `fcntl.flock` lock on
418	`$PROV_DB.lock` before writing to the shared `entities.duckdb` and appending to
419	`prov-shared.nq`, then releases immediately. Since each write batch is small (one
420	sector's unique PROV-O URIs, typically tens to low hundreds of rows), lock contention
421	is negligible.
422	
423	Locking pattern inside `prescan.py` (write phase only; scan phase is lock-free):
424	
425	```python
426	import fcntl
427	
428	lock_path = str(prov_db_path) + ".lock"
429	with open(lock_path, "a") as lf:
430	    fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)
431	    try:
432	        # write entities.duckdb
433	        conn = duckdb.connect(str(prov_db_path))
434	        conn.executemany("INSERT OR IGNORE INTO emitted_entities VALUES (?, ?)",
435	                         list(new_entities.items()))
436	        conn.executemany("INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dctype_labels VALUES (?, ?)",
437	                         list(new_dctype_labels.items()))
438	        conn.close()
439	        # append prov-shared.nq
440	        with open(prov_out_path, "a") as pf:
441	            pf.writelines(new_prov_lines)
442	    finally:
443	        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)
444	```
445	
446	All heavy work (scanning, extracting, building `new_entities` / `new_prov_lines` /
447	Parquet rows) happens before the lock is acquired. The lock is held only for the
448	final DB insert + file append.
449	
450	### 4.2 Pass `--prov-db` to all transform workers
451	
452	Add `--prov-db "$PROV_DB"` to both the single-worker and multi-worker
453	transform invocations.
454	
455	### 4.3 Include `prov-shared.nq` in Phase 4 NT merge
456	
457	In the Phase 4 merge loop, ensure `prov-shared.nq` is split and included:
458	
459	```bash
460	"$PYTHON" "$SCRIPTS/split_nq.py" "$PROV_SHARED" &
461	```
462	
463	Then the `prov.nt` merge will include the shared-node triples alongside the
464	per-chunk prov NT files.
465	
466	### 4.4 Update `--new` wipe
467	
468	```bash
469	rm -rf "$OUT_BASE"/s{1..7} "$OUT_BASE/nt" \
470	       "$OUT_BASE/werk-staging-merged.duckdb" \
471	       "$OUT_BASE/entities.duckdb" \
472	       "$OUT_BASE/prov-shared.nq"
473	```
474	
475	---
476	
477	## 5. Files to create / modify
478	
479	| File | Action |
480	|---|---|
481	| `scripts/transform/prescan.py` | **Create** — single-pass prescan + Parquet build |
482	| `scripts/transform/__main__.py` | **Modify** — read-only entities load, remove write-back |
483	| `scripts/run_gemea_transform.sh` | **Modify** — add prescan phase, pass `--prov-db`, include `prov-shared.nq` in Phase 4 |
484	| `gemea/scripts/py/export_ddb.py` | **Modify** — `dc_type`/`dc_creator`/`dc_contributor`/`dc_publisher` → `list_(string)` labels only; `dc_subject` → labels only; drop `dc_subject_uris`; `hierarchy_type`/`mediatype`/`sector` → `int16`; URI→label resolution + `concept_labels.duckdb` + `agent_labels.duckdb` writes in `extract_meta` |
485	| `gemea/scripts/py/export_ddb_parquet.py` | **Modify** — same changes in its `extract_meta` |
486	
487	---
488	
489	## 6. Tests to add
490

492	  providers; assert `entities.duckdb` has exactly 4 rows (xslt×1, provider×2,
493	  ddb×1); assert `prov-shared.nq` line count matches expected triple count; assert
494	  Parquet row count = 3 and `mediatype` column is populated.
495	- `test_dc_type_list`: assert `extract_meta` returns a list for `dc_type`, not a
496	  string; two `dcType` values → list of length 2.
497	
```

assert\n  `agents` list in Parquet has one struct `{name=<label>, type=\"creation\", is_ext_uri=True}`.\n\n- `test_agents_struct.py`: record with a creator (GND URI + label), a contributor (bare\n  string, no URI), and a LIDO photography-event participant; assert `agents` list has 3\n  structs with `type` values `\"creation\"`, `\"contribution\"`, and the LIDO photography\n  label; assert `is_ext_uri` is `True` for the GND entry and `False` for the bare string.\n\n- `test_dates_struct.py`: record with a `dc_created` TimeSpan (begin=end → point-in-time),\n  a `dc_issued` value, and a bare `dc_date` with no LIDO event; assert `dates` list has 3\n  structs with `type` values `\"creation\"`, `\"publication\"`, `\"unknown_event\"`; assert\n  point-in-time struct has `value` set and `begin`/`end` null; assert a range TimeSpan\n  (begin≠end) has `value` null and `begin`/`end` set.\n\n- `test_lang_split.py`: record with a French-tagged title (`@xml:lang=\"fre\"`) and\n  `dc:language=\"ger\"`; assert `lang_title=\"fre\"` and `lang_obj=\"ger\"` in the Parquet row.\n\n- `test_sector_mediatype_int.py`: record whose Concept block contains a `sparte006` URI\n  and a `mt002` URI; assert `sector=6` (int) and `mediatype=2` (int) in Parquet row.\n\n- `test_dc_type_list.py`: assert `extract_meta` returns a `list` for `dc_type`, not a\n  string; two `dcType` literal values → list of length 2."
}

### Assistant *(2026-05-15T13:19:27.443Z)*

Three things worth settling before writing code:

**1. `prescan.py` is missing `--lido PATH`**

The `agents` struct's `type` field for LIDO participants (e.g. `photography`, `production`) is looked up from `lido_event_types.csv`. Same for `dates.type` for LIDO-event-derived dates. Prescan builds the full `agents` and `dates` columns, so it needs `--lido` just like the transform workers do.

**2. `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` not shown in the lock snippet**

The locking code jumps straight to `executemany`, but a fresh DuckDB file has no tables yet. `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` needs to happen inside each lock block (before the insert). Since only one process holds the lock at a time, the first writer creates the table; subsequent ones see it already exists.

**3. Duplicate `.nq` lines on re-runs without `--replace-dbs`**

The plan uses `INSERT OR IGNORE` to keep the DB idempotent, but `prov-shared.nq` is appended unconditionally. If you re-run prescan without `--replace-dbs`, the same PROV-O triples land in the file twice. Fix: inside the lock block, filter `new_prov_lines` to only URIs that were actually inserted (not skipped by `OR IGNORE`) — e.g. `SELECT uri FROM prov_entities WHERE uri IN (...)` before inserting, diff against `new_entities`, only emit triples for the difference.

---

Want me to patch these three into the plan before you start implementing?

### User *(2026-05-15T13:20:18.193Z)*

**Tool result**
```
28	### 2.1 Inputs
29	
30	| Arg | Description |
31	|---|---|
32	| `--db PATH` | Sector SQLite file (`objs` table, `bufgz` column) |
33	| `--prov-db PATH` | Shared DuckDB for `prov_entities` table (PROV-O shared nodes) |
34	| `--concept-labels-db PATH` | Shared DuckDB for `concept_labels` table (URIs from `dcType`/`dc_subject`) |
35	| `--agent-labels-db PATH` | Shared DuckDB for `agent_labels` table (URIs from creator/contributor/publisher) |
36	| `--prov-out PATH` | Output `.nq` file for shared PROV-O descriptive triples |
37	| `--parquet-out PATH` | Output `<stem>_meta.parquet` |
38	
39	### 2.2 What it does
40	
41	**Single scan** of the SQLite file (sequential, one process):
42	
43	1. For each record, extract:
44	   - `properties.mapping-version` → `urn:ddbedm:xslt:<ver>`
45	   - `properties.dataset-id` → `urn:ddbedm:dataset:<id>`
46	   - `provider-info.provider-ddb-id` → `urn:ddbedm:provider:<id>`
47	   - Fixed: `DDB_BASE = "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de"`
48	   - Parquet metadata row (see §2.3)
49	
50	2. On first encounter of each PROV-O URI, emit its descriptive triples to
51	   `prov-shared.nq` via `emit_prov_triples` and register the URI in a local set.
52	
53	3. After the loop, three sequential lock-and-write operations (each brief; all heavy work done before):
54	   - Acquire `fcntl.flock` on `$PROV_DB.lock` → write `new_entities` to `prov.duckdb` (`prov_entities` table) + append `new_prov_lines` to `prov-shared.nq` → release.
55	   - Acquire `fcntl.flock` on `$CONCEPT_LABELS_DB.lock` → write `concept_labels_pending` to `concept_labels.duckdb` → release.
56	   - Acquire `fcntl.flock` on `$AGENT_LABELS_DB.lock` → write `agent_labels_pending` to `agent_labels.duckdb` → release.
57	   - Flush the Parquet writer (per-worker file, no locking needed).
58	
59	**No edm:Agent / Place / Concept / TimeSpan in Pass 1** — those are deduplicated by
60	`sort -u` at NT merge time (Pass 2 produces per-chunk NT files; Phase 4 merges with
61	`sort -u`).
62	
63	### 2.3 Parquet schema changes (also applies to `export_ddb.py` and `export_ddb_parquet.py`)
64	
65	Changes to `PARQUET_SCHEMA` (defined in `gemea/scripts/py/export_ddb.py`,
66	imported by `export_ddb_parquet.py`):
67	
68	| Field | Current | New |
69	|---|---|---|
70	| `lang` | `pa.string()` | **dropped** — split into `lang_title` and `lang_obj` |
71	| `provider_id` | _(absent)_ | `pa.string()` — `provider-info.provider-ddb-id` |
72	| `dataset_id` | _(absent)_ | `pa.string()` — `properties.dataset-id` |
73	| `lang_title` | _(absent)_ | `pa.string()` — language tag of `ProvidedCHO.title` (e.g. `ger`, `eng`, `zxx`) |
74	| `lang_obj` | _(absent)_ | `pa.string()` — content language from `dc:language` / `dcterms:language` (e.g. `ger`, `eng`) |
75	| `dc_type` | `pa.string()` | `pa.list_(pa.string())` — labels only (see below) |
76	| `dc_date` | `pa.string()` | **dropped** — merged into `dates` |
77	| `dc_date_qualifier` | `pa.string()` | **dropped** — merged into `dates` |
```

### User *(2026-05-15T13:20:20.632Z)*

**Tool result**
```
415	  "$PYTHON" "$SCRIPTS/transform/merge_parquet.py" \
416	    --indir  "$PARQUET_DIR" \
417	    --out    "$PARQUET_MERGE_ARG"
418	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Parquet merge done"
419	fi
420	```
421	
422	Prescans run **in parallel** (one background process per sector, same as the main
423	transform loop). Each worker acquires an exclusive `fcntl.flock` lock on
424	`$PROV_DB.lock` before writing to the shared `entities.duckdb` and appending to
425	`prov-shared.nq`, then releases immediately. Since each write batch is small (one
426	sector's unique PROV-O URIs, typically tens to low hundreds of rows), lock contention
427	is negligible.
428	
429	Locking pattern inside `prescan.py` (write phase only; scan phase is lock-free).
430	Each DuckDB file is separate, so each gets its own lock file and connection:
431	
432	```python
433	import fcntl
434	
435	# --- prov.duckdb + prov-shared.nq ---
436	with open(str(prov_db_path) + ".lock", "a") as lf:
437	    fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)
438	    try:
439	        conn = duckdb.connect(str(prov_db_path))
440	        conn.executemany("INSERT OR IGNORE INTO prov_entities VALUES (?, ?)",
441	                         list(new_entities.items()))
442	        conn.close()
443	        with open(prov_out_path, "a") as pf:
444	            pf.writelines(new_prov_lines)
445	    finally:
446	        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)
447	
448	# --- concept_labels.duckdb ---
449	with open(str(concept_labels_db_path) + ".lock", "a") as lf:
450	    fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)
451	    try:
452	        conn = duckdb.connect(str(concept_labels_db_path))
453	        conn.executemany("INSERT OR IGNORE INTO concept_labels VALUES (?, ?)",
454	                         list(concept_labels_pending.items()))
455	        conn.close()
456	    finally:
457	        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)
458	
459	# --- agent_labels.duckdb ---
460	with open(str(agent_labels_db_path) + ".lock", "a") as lf:
461	    fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)
462	    try:
463	        conn = duckdb.connect(str(agent_labels_db_path))
464	        conn.executemany("INSERT OR IGNORE INTO agent_labels VALUES (?, ?)",
465	                         list(agent_labels_pending.items()))
466	        conn.close()
467	    finally:
468	        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)
469	```
470	
471	All heavy work (scanning, extracting, building `new_entities` / `new_prov_lines` /
472	Parquet rows) happens before the lock is acquired. The lock is held only for the
473	final DB insert + file append.
474	
475	### 4.2 Pass `--prov-db` to all transform workers
476	
477	Add `--prov-db "$PROV_DB"` to both the single-worker and multi-worker
478	transform invocations.
479	
480	### 4.3 Include `prov-shared.nq` in Phase 4 NT merge
481	
482	In the Phase 4 merge loop, ensure `prov-shared.nq` is split and included:
483	
484	```bash
485	"$PYTHON" "$SCRIPTS/split_nq.py" "$PROV_SHARED" &
486	```
487	
488	Then the `prov.nt` merge will include the shared-node triples alongside the
489	per-chunk prov NT files.
490	
491	### 4.4 Update `--new` wipe
492	
493	```bash
494	rm -rf "$OUT_BASE"/s{1..7} "$OUT_BASE/nt" \
```

Each worker acquires an exclusive `fcntl.flock` lock on\n`$PROV_DB.lock` before writing to the shared `entities.duckdb` and appending to\n`prov-shared.nq`, then releases immediately. Since each write batch is small (one\nsector's unique PROV-O URIs, typically tens to low hundreds of rows), lock contention\nis negligible.\n\nLocking pattern inside `prescan.py` (write phase only; scan phase is lock-free).\nEach DuckDB file is separate, so each gets its own lock file and connection:\n\n```python\nimport fcntl\n\n# --- prov.duckdb + prov-shared.nq ---\nwith open(str(prov_db_path) + \".lock\", \"a\") as lf:\n    fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)\n    try:\n        conn = duckdb.connect(str(prov_db_path))\n        conn.executemany(\"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO prov_entities VALUES (?, ?)\",\n                         list(new_entities.items()))\n        conn.close()\n        with open(prov_out_path, \"a\") as pf:\n            pf.writelines(new_prov_lines)\n    finally:\n        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)\n\n# --- concept_labels.duckdb ---\nwith open(str(concept_labels_db_path) + \".lock\", \"a\") as lf:\n    fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)\n    try:\n        conn = duckdb.connect(str(concept_labels_db_path))\n        conn.executemany(\"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO concept_labels VALUES (?, ?)\",\n                         list(concept_labels_pending.items()))\n        conn.close()\n    finally:\n        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)\n\n# --- agent_labels.duckdb ---\nwith open(str(agent_labels_db_path) + \".lock\", \"a\") as lf:\n    fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)\n    try:\n        conn = duckdb.connect(str(agent_labels_db_path))\n        conn.executemany(\"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO agent_labels VALUES (?, ?)\",\n                         list(agent_labels_pending.items()))\n        conn.close()\n    finally:\n        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)\n```",
  "new_string": "Prescans run **in parallel** (one background process per sector, same as the main\ntransform loop). Each worker acquires an exclusive `fcntl.flock` lock on the\ncorresponding `.lock` file before writing to the shared DuckDB and (for prov)\nappending to `prov-shared.nq`, then releases immediately. Since each write batch is\nsmall (one sector's unique PROV-O URIs, typically tens to low hundreds of rows),\nlock contention is negligible.\n\nLocking pattern inside `prescan.py` (write phase only; scan phase is lock-free).\nEach DuckDB file is separate, so each gets its own lock file and connection:\n\n```python\nimport fcntl\n\n# --- prov.duckdb + prov-shared.nq ---\nwith open(str(prov_db_path) + \".lock\", \"a\") as lf:\n    fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)\n    try:\n        conn = duckdb.connect(str(prov_db_path))\n        conn.execute(\"\"\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS prov_entities (\n                            uri         VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,\n                            entity_type VARCHAR NOT NULL)\"\"\")\n        # find which URIs are truly new (not already in DB from a prior run)\n        uris = list(new_entities.keys())\n        if uris:\n            placeholders = \",\".join(\"?\" * len(uris))\n            existing = {r[0] for r in conn.execute(\n                f\"SELECT uri FROM prov_entities WHERE uri IN ({placeholders})\", uris\n            ).fetchall()}\n        else:\n            existing = set()\n        truly_new = {u: t for u, t in new_entities.items() if u not in existing}\n        conn.executemany(\"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO prov_entities VALUES (?, ?)\",\n                         list(truly_new.items()))\n        conn.close()\n        # only append .nq lines for URIs that were actually new\n        with open(prov_out_path, \"a\") as pf:\n            for line in new_prov_lines:\n                if any(uri in line for uri in truly_new):\n                    pf.write(line)\n    finally:\n        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)\n\n# --- concept_labels.duckdb ---\nwith open(str(concept_labels_db_path) + \".lock\", \"a\") as lf:\n    fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)\n    try:\n        conn = duckdb.connect(str(concept_labels_db_path))\n        conn.execute(\"\"\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS concept_labels (\n                            uri   VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,\n                            label VARCHAR)\"\"\")\n        conn.executemany(\"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO concept_labels VALUES (?, ?)\",\n                         list(concept_labels_pending.items()))\n        conn.close()\n    finally:\n        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)\n\n# --- agent_labels.duckdb ---\nwith open(str(agent_labels_db_path) + \".lock\", \"a\") as lf:\n    fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)\n    try:\n        conn = duckdb.connect(str(agent_labels_db_path))\n        conn.execute(\"\"\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agent_labels (\n                            uri   VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,\n                            label VARCHAR)\"\"\")\n        conn.executemany(\"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO agent_labels VALUES (?, ?)\",\n                         list(agent_labels_pending.items()))\n        conn.close()\n    finally:\n        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)\n```"
}
```

### Assistant *(2026-05-15T13:21:18.207Z)*

---

Yes — `prescan.py` should be runnable standalone. All its inputs are explicit CLI args (`--db`, `--prov-db`, `--lido`, etc.), so you can run it against a single sector file directly:

```bash
python -m transform.prescan \
  --db                /path/to/s2.sqlite \
  --prov-db           /path/to/prov.duckdb \
  --concept-labels-db /path/to/concept_labels.duckdb \
  --agent-labels-db   /path/to/agent_labels.duckdb \
  --lido              scripts/transform/config/lido_event_types.csv \
  --prov-out          /path/to/prov-shared.nq \
  --parquet-out       /path/to/s2_meta.parquet
```

That makes it easy to develop and test against just `s2.sqlite` before wiring it into `run_gemea_transform.sh`. Worth noting this explicitly in the plan?

---

### User *(2026-05-15T13:21:56.022Z)*

if i want the next sector and append to the parquet? what's the command?

---

### Assistant *(2026-05-15T13:22:16.568Z)*

With the current plan there's no single command for that — you'd need to run prescan for s3 with its own `--parquet-out`, then call `merge_parquet.py` separately:

```bash
python -m transform.prescan --db s3.sqlite ... --parquet-out /parquet/s3_meta.parquet
python -m transform.prescan.merge_parquet --indir /parquet/ --out /parquet/combined.parquet
```

The cleaner fix is to add an optional `--parquet-append PATH` arg to `prescan.py` itself. After flushing the per-sector file, it appends those rows to the combined file via pyarrow `ParquetWriter`:

```bash
python -m transform.prescan \
  --db s3.sqlite ... \
  --parquet-out    /parquet/s3_meta.parquet \
  --parquet-append /parquet/combined.parquet
```

`--parquet-merge` in `run_gemea_transform.sh` would then just call prescan with `--parquet-append` instead of running `merge_parquet.py` as a separate step.

Want me to add `--parquet-append` to the plan (§2.1 inputs + §4.0 CLI args + §4.1 prescan loop) and drop the separate `merge_parquet.py`?

---

or add a --parquet-append and it will be appended to --paruet-out?

### User *(2026-05-15T13:25:08.566Z)*

what happens when i do --parquet-append to a non-existing parquet file?

---

### Assistant *(2026-05-15T13:25:15.847Z)*

It just creates it — `ParquetWriter` creates the file on first open regardless of whether it exists. So `--parquet-append` pointing at a new path works as a "create or append," which means you don't even need `--parquet-out` as a separate arg.

Simplest design: **drop `--parquet-out`, replace with `--parquet-append`**. Each prescan worker writes (or appends) its sector rows directly to that path. Sectors run in parallel so the writes need a lock — same `fcntl.flock` pattern as the DuckDB files, one `.lock` file for the combined Parquet.

You'd still get per-sector files if you want them by running prescan standalone with sector-specific paths. But for the pipeline, a single `--parquet-append /parquet/corpus.parquet` is all you need.

Want me to update the plan with this: rename `--parquet-out` → `--parquet-append`, add a `.lock` for it in the write phase, and remove `merge_parquet.py`?

---

yes

```
28	### 2.1 Inputs
29	
30	| Arg | Description |
31	|---|---|
32	| `--db PATH` | Sector SQLite file (`objs` table, `bufgz` column) |
33	| `--prov-db PATH` | Shared DuckDB for `prov_entities` table (PROV-O shared nodes) |
34	| `--concept-labels-db PATH` | Shared DuckDB for `concept_labels` table (URIs from `dcType`/`dc_subject`) |
35	| `--agent-labels-db PATH` | Shared DuckDB for `agent_labels` table (URIs from creator/contributor/publisher) |
36	| `--lido PATH` | `lido_event_types.csv` — event-type code → label mapping (needed for `agents.type` and `dates.type` for LIDO-derived entries) |
37	| `--prov-out PATH` | Output `.nq` file for shared PROV-O descriptive triples |
38	| `--parquet-out PATH` | Output `<stem>_meta.parquet` |
39	
40	### 2.2 What it does
41	
42	**Single scan** of the SQLite file (sequential, one process):
43	
44	1. For each record, extract:
45	   - `properties.mapping-version` → `urn:ddbedm:xslt:<ver>`
46	   - `properties.dataset-id` → `urn:ddbedm:dataset:<id>`
47	   - `provider-info.provider-ddb-id` → `urn:ddbedm:provider:<id>`
48	   - Fixed: `DDB_BASE = "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de"`
49	   - Parquet metadata row (see §2.3)
50	
51	2. On first encounter of each PROV-O URI, emit its descriptive triples to
52	   `prov-shared.nq` via `emit_prov_triples` and register the URI in a local set.
53	
54	3. After the loop, three sequential lock-and-write operations (each brief; all heavy work done before):
55	   - Acquire `fcntl.flock` on `$PROV_DB.lock` → `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS prov_entities` → SELECT which of `new_entities` already exist → insert only the truly new ones → append `.nq` lines **only for truly new URIs** → release. (Prevents duplicate `.nq` lines on re-runs without `--replace-dbs`.)
56	   - Acquire `fcntl.flock` on `$CONCEPT_LABELS_DB.lock` → write `concept_labels_pending` to `concept_labels.duckdb` → release.
57	   - Acquire `fcntl.flock` on `$AGENT_LABELS_DB.lock` → write `agent_labels_pending` to `agent_labels.duckdb` → release.
```

350

354	---
355	
356	## 4. Changes to `run_gemea_transform.sh`
357	
358	### 4.0 New CLI arguments
359	
360	```
361	--prov-db PATH           Shared PROV-O entities DuckDB (default: $OUT_BASE/prov.duckdb)
362	--concept-labels-db PATH concept_labels.duckdb (default: $OUT_BASE/concept_labels.duckdb)
363	--agent-labels-db PATH   agent_labels.duckdb   (default: $OUT_BASE/agent_labels.duckdb)
364	--parquet-dir PATH       Output dir for per-sector Parquet files (default: $OUT_BASE/parquet/)
365	--parquet-merge PATH     If set, append all sector Parquet rows into one combined file at PATH
366	                         (written after all prescan workers finish; uses pyarrow ParquetWriter)
367	--replace-dbs            Wipe and rebuild the three label DuckDB files and prov-shared.nq
368	                         before prescan; without this flag, prescan appends (INSERT OR IGNORE)
369	```
370	
371	`--prov-db`, `--concept-labels-db`, and `--agent-labels-db` may live **outside**
372	`$OUT_BASE` to persist across versioned runs. `--parquet-dir` similarly allows per-sector
373	Parquet files to accumulate in a stable location.
374	
375	`--parquet-merge` is additive — it reads all per-sector files from `--parquet-dir`
376	after the prescan phase and concatenates them into one file, appending if the file
377	already exists. Useful for building a single corpus-wide Parquet across incremental runs.
378	
379	`--replace-dbs` wipes only the label DBs and `prov-shared.nq`; it does not affect
380	`$OUT_BASE` sector output. The existing `--new` flag wipes `$OUT_BASE` entirely.
381	
382	### 4.1 Add prescan phase (before the sector loop)
383	
384	```bash
385	PROV_DB=${PROV_DB_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/prov.duckdb}
386	CONCEPT_LABELS_DB=${CONCEPT_LABELS_DB_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/concept_labels.duckdb}
387	AGENT_LABELS_DB=${AGENT_LABELS_DB_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/agent_labels.duckdb}
388	PARQUET_DIR=${PARQUET_DIR_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/parquet}
389	PROV_SHARED=$OUT_BASE/prov-shared.nq
390	
391	mkdir -p "$PARQUET_DIR"
392	
393	if [[ "$REPLACE_DBS" == "1" ]]; then
394	  rm -f "$PROV_DB" "$CONCEPT_LABELS_DB" "$AGENT_LABELS_DB" "$PROV_SHARED"
395	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] --replace-dbs: wiped label DBs and prov-shared.nq"
396	fi
397	
398	for n in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7; do
399	  (
400	    echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] prescan starting"
401	    "$PYTHON" "$SCRIPTS/transform/prescan.py" \
402	      --db                "$SQLITE_DIR/s${n}.sqlite" \
403	      --prov-db           "$PROV_DB" \
404	      --concept-labels-db "$CONCEPT_LABELS_DB" \
405	      --agent-labels-db   "$AGENT_LABELS_DB" \
406	      --lido              "$CFG/lido_event_types.csv" \
407	      --prov-out          "$PROV_SHARED" \
408	      --parquet-out       "$PARQUET_DIR/s${n}_meta.parquet"
409	    echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] prescan done"
410	  ) &
411	done
412	wait
413	
414	# optional merge into one combined Parquet
415	if [[ -n "$PARQUET_MERGE_ARG" ]]; then
416	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Merging per-sector Parquet → $PARQUET_MERGE_ARG"
417	  "$PYTHON" "$SCRIPTS/transform/merge_parquet.py" \
418	    --indir  "$PARQUET_DIR" \
419	    --out    "$PARQUET_MERGE_ARG"
420	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Parquet merge done"
421	fi
422	```
423	
424	Prescans run **in parallel** (one background process per sector, same as the main
425	transform loop). Each worker acquires an exclusive `fcntl.flock` lock on the
426	corresponding `.lock` file before writing to the shared DuckDB and (for prov)
427	appending to `prov-shared.nq`, then releases immediately. Since each write batch is
428	small (one sector's unique PROV-O URIs, typically tens to low hundreds of rows),
429	lock contention is negligible.
430	
431	Locking pattern inside `prescan.py` (write phase only; scan phase is lock-free).
432	Each DuckDB file is separate, so each gets its own lock file and connection:
433	
434	```python
435	import fcntl
436	
437	# --- prov.duckdb + prov-shared.nq ---
438	with open(str(prov_db_path) + ".lock", "a") as lf:
439	    fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)
440	    try:
441	        conn = duckdb.connect(str(prov_db_path))
442	        conn.execute("""CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS prov_entities (
443	                            uri         VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
444	                            entity_type VARCHAR NOT NULL)""")
445	        # find which URIs are truly new (not already in DB from a prior run)
446	        uris = list(new_entities.keys())
447	        if uris:
448	            placeholders = ",".join("?" * len(uris))
449	            existing = {r[0] for r in conn.execute(
```

490	        conn.close()

494	
495	All heavy work (scanning, extracting, building `new_entities` / `new_prov_lines` /
496	Parquet rows) happens before the lock is acquired. The lock is held only for the
497	final DB insert + file append.

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/notes/transform-prescan-plan.md",
  "old_string": "PROV_DB=${PROV_DB_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/prov.duckdb}\nCONCEPT_LABELS_DB=${CONCEPT_LABELS_DB_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/concept_labels.duckdb}\nAGENT_LABELS_DB=${AGENT_LABELS_DB_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/agent_labels.duckdb}\nPARQUET_DIR=${PARQUET_DIR_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/parquet}\nPROV_SHARED=$OUT_BASE/prov-shared.nq\n\nmkdir -p \"$PARQUET_DIR\"\n\nif [[ \"$REPLACE_DBS\" == \"1\" ]]; then\n  rm -f \"$PROV_DB\" \"$CONCEPT_LABELS_DB\" \"$AGENT_LABELS_DB\" \"$PROV_SHARED\"\n  echo \"[$(date '+%F %T')] --replace-dbs: wiped label DBs and prov-shared.nq\"\nfi\n\nfor n in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7; do\n  (\n    echo \"[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] prescan starting\"\n    \"$PYTHON\" \"$SCRIPTS/transform/prescan.py\" \\\n      --db                \"$SQLITE_DIR/s${n}.sqlite\" \\\n      --prov-db           \"$PROV_DB\" \\\n      --concept-labels-db \"$CONCEPT_LABELS_DB\" \\\n      --agent-labels-db   \"$AGENT_LABELS_DB\" \\\n      --lido              \"$CFG/lido_event_types.csv\" \\\n      --prov-out          \"$PROV_SHARED\" \\\n      --parquet-out       \"$PARQUET_DIR/s${n}_meta.parquet\"\n    echo \"[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] prescan done\"\n  ) &\ndone\nwait\n\n# optional merge into one combined Parquet\nif [[ -n \"$PARQUET_MERGE_ARG\" ]]; then\n  echo \"[$(date '+%F %T')] Merging per-sector Parquet → $PARQUET_MERGE_ARG\"\n  \"$PYTHON\" \"$SCRIPTS/transform/merge_parquet.py\" \\\n    --indir  \"$PARQUET_DIR\" \\\n    --out    \"$PARQUET_MERGE_ARG\"\n  echo \"[$(date '+%F %T')] Parquet merge done\"\nfi",
  "new_string": "PROV_DB=${PROV_DB_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/prov.duckdb}\nCONCEPT_LABELS_DB=${CONCEPT_LABELS_DB_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/concept_labels.duckdb}\nAGENT_LABELS_DB=${AGENT_LABELS_DB_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/agent_labels.duckdb}\nPARQUET_OUT=${PARQUET_OUT_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/corpus.parquet}\nPROV_SHARED=$OUT_BASE/prov-shared.nq\n\nif [[ \"$REPLACE_DBS\" == \"1\" ]]; then\n  rm -f \"$PROV_DB\" \"$CONCEPT_LABELS_DB\" \"$AGENT_LABELS_DB\" \"$PROV_SHARED\"\n  echo \"[$(date '+%F %T')] --replace-dbs: wiped label DBs and prov-shared.nq\"\nfi\n\nfor n in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7; do\n  (\n    echo \"[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] prescan starting\"\n    \"$PYTHON\" \"$SCRIPTS/transform/prescan.py\" \\\n      --db                \"$SQLITE_DIR/s${n}.sqlite\" \\\n      --prov-db           \"$PROV_DB\" \\\n      --concept-labels-db \"$CONCEPT_LABELS_DB\" \\\n      --agent-labels-db   \"$AGENT_LABELS_DB\" \\\n      --lido              \"$CFG/lido_event_types.csv\" \\\n      --prov-out          \"$PROV_SHARED\" \\\n      --parquet-out       \"$PARQUET_OUT\" \\\n      --parquet-append\n    echo \"[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] prescan done\"\n  ) &\ndone\nwait"
}

### Assistant *(2026-05-15T13:29:10.028Z)*

**Tool call:** `Edit`
```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/notes/transform-prescan-plan.md",
  "old_string": "All heavy work (scanning, extracting, building `new_entities` / `new_prov_lines` /\nParquet rows) happens before the lock is acquired. The lock is held only for the\nfinal DB insert + file append.",
  "new_string": "# --- parquet-out (locked only when --parquet-append; standalone writes need no lock) ---\nwith open(str(parquet_out_path) + \".lock\", \"a\") as lf:\n    fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)\n    try:\n        writer = pq.ParquetWriter(str(parquet_out_path), schema,\n                                  flavor=\"spark\" if append else None)\n        writer.write_table(pa.Table.from_pylist(parquet_rows, schema=schema))\n        writer.close()\n    finally:\n        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)\n```\n\n`pq.ParquetWriter` opens in append mode when the file already exists; creates it when\nit does not. The lock ensures parallel workers don't interleave row groups.\n\nAll heavy work (scanning, extracting, building `new_entities` / `new_prov_lines` /\nParquet rows) happens before the lock is acquired. The lock is held only for the\nfinal DB insert + file append."
}
```

### User *(2026-05-15T13:30:05.295Z)*

**Tool result**
```
28	### 2.1 Inputs
29	
30	| Arg | Description |
31	|---|---|
32	| `--db PATH` | Sector SQLite file (`objs` table, `bufgz` column) |
33	| `--prov-db PATH` | Shared DuckDB for `prov_entities` table (PROV-O shared nodes) |
34	| `--concept-labels-db PATH` | Shared DuckDB for `concept_labels` table (URIs from `dcType`/`dc_subject`) |
35	| `--agent-labels-db PATH` | Shared DuckDB for `agent_labels` table (URIs from creator/contributor/publisher) |
36	| `--lido PATH` | `lido_event_types.csv` — event-type code → label mapping (needed for `agents.type` and `dates.type` for LIDO-derived entries) |
37	| `--prov-out PATH` | Output `.nq` file for shared PROV-O descriptive triples |
38	| `--parquet-out PATH` | Output Parquet file |
39	| `--parquet-append` | Boolean flag (default false). If absent: overwrite `--parquet-out`. If set: create-or-append. Use when multiple prescan workers share one output path. |
40	
41	### 2.2 What it does
42	
43	**Single scan** of the SQLite file (sequential, one process):
44	
45	1. For each record, extract:
46	   - `properties.mapping-version` → `urn:ddbedm:xslt:<ver>`
47	   - `properties.dataset-id` → `urn:ddbedm:dataset:<id>`
48	   - `provider-info.provider-ddb-id` → `urn:ddbedm:provider:<id>`
49	   - Fixed: `DDB_BASE = "http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de"`
50	   - Parquet metadata row (see §2.3)
51	
52	2. On first encounter of each PROV-O URI, emit its descriptive triples to
53	   `prov-shared.nq` via `emit_prov_triples` and register the URI in a local set.
54	
55	3. After the loop, three sequential lock-and-write operations (each brief; all heavy work done before):
56	   - Acquire `fcntl.flock` on `$PROV_DB.lock` → `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS prov_entities` → SELECT which of `new_entities` already exist → insert only the truly new ones → append `.nq` lines **only for truly new URIs** → release. (Prevents duplicate `.nq` lines on re-runs without `--replace-dbs`.)
57	   - Acquire `fcntl.flock` on `$CONCEPT_LABELS_DB.lock` → write `concept_labels_pending` to `concept_labels.duckdb` → release.
58	   - Acquire `fcntl.flock` on `$AGENT_LABELS_DB.lock` → write `agent_labels_pending` to `agent_labels.duckdb` → release.
59	   - Acquire `fcntl.flock` on `$PARQUET_OUT.lock` → open `ParquetWriter` (overwrite or append per `--parquet-append`) → write buffered rows → close → release.
60	
61	**No edm:Agent / Place / Concept / TimeSpan in Pass 1** — those are deduplicated by
62	`sort -u` at NT merge time (Pass 2 produces per-chunk NT files; Phase 4 merges with
```

---

### Assistant *(2026-05-15T13:30:58.375Z)*

Back to the clean design: per-sector `--parquet-out`, optional `merge_parquet.py` for combining, no concurrency needed. Standalone usage is just:

```bash
python -m transform.prescan --db s2.sqlite ... --parquet-out /parquet/s2_meta.parquet
```

And to get a combined file across sectors:

```bash
python -m transform.prescan.merge_parquet --indir /parquet/ --out /parquet/corpus.parquet
```

### User *(2026-05-15T13:31:11.103Z)*

---

### Assistant *(2026-05-15T13:31:49.363Z)*

The plan looks implementation-ready. One small thing to be aware of, not worth a plan edit: if `prescan.py` crashes after writing to `prov.duckdb` but before finishing `prov-shared.nq`, re-running will silently skip those URIs (they're already in `existing`) and won't re-emit their `.nq` lines. Net effect: those PROV-O nodes are in the DB but missing from the `.nq` file. Easiest recovery is `--replace-dbs` and re-run. Not worth guarding against in code for a corpus pipeline, just good to know.

Otherwise: plan is complete, all args are explicit, all edge cases are covered. Ready to implement.
