```
1	"""
2	Purpose:    Transform DDB-EDM JSONL records to mocho-aligned N-Quads.
3	            Produces four named-graph streams: ddbedm (verbatim EDM passthrough),
4	            mocho (mocho-aligned triples), prov (PROV-O Layer 1), and a DuckDB
5	            werk_staging table for GND Werk linking (link_gnd_works.py, Phase 0).
6	            Reference implementation for the mocho ingest pipeline.
7	            Decisions: transform-adr.md D11/D15/D17, transform-script-adr.md D1–D27.
8	Usage:      python -m transform
9	                [--jsonl FILE] [--ids FILE] [--outdir DIR]
10	                [--alignment FILE] [--lido FILE] [--htype FILE]
11	                [--mediatype FILE] [--audio FILE]
12	                [--stats LEVEL] [--log-level LEVEL]
13	                [--limit N] [--total N] [--log-interval N] [--debug]
14	Inputs:     data/items-all-goethe-faust.json              JSONL, one record per line
15	            data/ids-all-goethe-faust.txt                  32-char object IDs, one per line
16	            output/config/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv  (target_class, edm_prop) → target_prop
17	            output/config/lido_event_types.csv             lido_uri → agent predicates per WEMI
18	            output/config/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv       htype_code → (rdf_type, rst_iris)
19	            output/config/lookup_mediatype_class.csv       (sparte, mediatype) → class dispatch row
20	            output/config/audio_type2class.json            mt001 dc:type → group (A/B/C)
21	Outputs:    output/transform/YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS/               run directory (one per invocation)
22	              <stem>.nq                                    combined N-Quads (all named graphs)
23	              <stem>-werk-staging.duckdb                  W-slot staging rows
24	              <stem>-stats.json                           run statistics
25	              <stem>-errors.jsonl                         per-record errors (written live)
26	              <stem>.log                                  run log
27	            <stem> is the input filename without extension (e.g. s2 → s2.nq)
28	Deps:       stdlib only + duckdb (pip install duckdb)
29	Assumes:    JSONL: one JSON object per line; record structure edm.RDF.*
30	            All config CSVs present at output/config/
31	            Graceful exit on SIGINT/SIGTERM: partial stats and errors are written.
32	"""
33	
34	from __future__ import annotations
35	
36	import argparse
37	import json
38	import logging
39	import signal
40	import sys
41	import time
42	import traceback
43	from collections import Counter
44	from datetime import datetime
45	from pathlib import Path
46	
47	from .constants import (
48	    DEFAULT_JSONL, DEFAULT_ALIGNMENT, DEFAULT_LIDO, DEFAULT_HTYPE,
49	    DEFAULT_MEDIATYPE, DEFAULT_AUDIO, DEFAULT_OUTPUT_BASE,
50	)
51	from .utils import get_object_id, _to_curie
52	from .loaders import (
53	    load_ids, load_class_prop_alignment, load_lido_event_types,
54	    load_htype_map, load_mediatype_class, load_audio_type2class,
55	)
56	from .transform import transform_record
57	
58	
59	_stop = False
60	
61	
62	def _handle_signal(sig, frame) -> None:
63	    global _stop
64	    _stop = True
65	    print(f"\nSignal {sig} received — stopping after current record ...", file=sys.stderr)
66	
67	
68	signal.signal(signal.SIGINT,  _handle_signal)
69	signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handle_signal)
70	
71	
72	def _fmt_duration(seconds: float) -> str:
73	    h, r = divmod(int(seconds), 3600)
74	    m, s = divmod(r, 60)
75	    return f"{h:02d}:{m:02d}:{s:02d}"
76	
77	
78	def main() -> None:
79	    if len(sys.argv) > 1 and sys.argv[1] == "merge":
80	        from .merge import main as _merge_main
81	        sys.argv = [sys.argv[0]] + sys.argv[2:]
82	        _merge_main()
83	        return
84	
85	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
86	        description="Transform DDB-EDM JSONL to mocho N-Quads (§8)"
87	    )
88	
89	    io = parser.add_argument_group("I/O")
90	    io.add_argument("--jsonl",  type=Path, default=DEFAULT_JSONL,
91	                    help="JSONL input file (one DDB-EDM JSON object per line); "
92	                         "default: data/items-all-goethe-faust.json")
93	    io.add_argument("--db",     type=Path, default=None,
94	                    help="SQLite sector file — reads directly without intermediate JSONL export; "
95	                         "mutually exclusive with --jsonl")
96	    io.add_argument("--offset", type=int,  default=0,
97	                    help="Skip first N rows in SQLite (for parallel workers; use with --db and --limit)")
98	    io.add_argument("--ids",    type=str,  default=None,
99	                    help="Path to ID allowlist file (one 32-char DDB ID per line), "
100	                         "or '-' to read from stdin; omit to process all records")
101	    io.add_argument("--outdir", type=Path, default=None,
102	                    help="Output directory; auto-timestamped if omitted "
103	                         "(default: output/transform/YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS)")
104	    io.add_argument("--stem", type=str, default=None,
105	                    help="Output filename stem (overrides the input filename); "
106	                         "e.g. --stem items-all-goethe-faust → items-all-goethe-faust.nq, etc.")
107	    io.add_argument("--entities-db", type=Path, default=None,
108	                    dest="entities_db",
109	                    help="Shared cross-run entity-dedup DuckDB; created if absent. "
110	                         "Stores URIs of already-emitted shared nodes (PROV-O, "
111	                         "edm:Agent, edm:Place, skos:Concept, edm:TimeSpan). "
112	                         "Omit for within-run dedup only (no persistence).")
113	
114	    cfg = parser.add_argument_group("Config")
115	    cfg.add_argument("--alignment", type=Path, default=DEFAULT_ALIGNMENT,
116	                     help="Property alignment lookup CSV "
117	                          "(target_class, edm_prop) → mocho property; "
118	                          "default: output/config/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv")
119	    cfg.add_argument("--lido",      type=Path, default=DEFAULT_LIDO,
120	                     help="LIDO event type dispatch CSV "
121	                          "(event URI → agent predicates per WEMI level); "
122	                          "default: output/config/lido_event_types.csv")
123	    cfg.add_argument("--htype",     type=Path, default=DEFAULT_HTYPE,
124	                     help="htype → class lookup CSV "
125	                          "(htype_code → rdf:type IRIs for §1.1 dispatch); "
126	                          "default: output/config/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv")
127	    cfg.add_argument("--mediatype", type=Path, default=DEFAULT_MEDIATYPE,
128	                     help="Mediatype × sector → class lookup CSV "
129	                          "(sector, mediatype → WEMI class IRIs); "
130	                          "default: output/config/lookup_mediatype_class.csv")
131	    cfg.add_argument("--audio",     type=Path, default=DEFAULT_AUDIO,
132	                     help="mt001 dc:type → audio group JSON "
133	                          "(dc:type value → A/B/C group for audio class dispatch); "
134	                          "default: output/config/audio_type2class.json")
135	
136	    run = parser.add_argument_group("Run control")
137	    run.add_argument("--stats",     choices=["none", "basic", "dispatch", "full"],
138	                     default="basic",
139	                     help="Stats verbosity written to transform_stats.json: "
140	                          "none=nothing written, basic=run/records/triples/werk_staging, "
141	                          "dispatch=basic+WEMI class counts (recommended for full-corpus runs), "
142	                          "full=dispatch+per-predicate mocho counts (slow — use with --limit); "
143	                          "default: basic")
144	    run.add_argument("--log-level", default="INFO",
145	                     choices=["DEBUG", "INFO", "WARNING", "ERROR"],
146	                     dest="log_level",
147	                     help="Console and file log verbosity; default: INFO")
148	    run.add_argument("--limit",        type=int, default=None,
149	                     help="Stop after N records — for smoke-testing and sampling")
150	    run.add_argument("--total",        type=int, default=None,
151	                     help="Expected total records — enables ETA in progress log")
152	    run.add_argument("--log-interval", type=int, default=100_000, dest="log_interval",
153	                     help="Log a progress line every N records (default: 100000)")
154	    run.add_argument("--debug",     action="store_true",
155	                     help="Enable DEBUG logging (shorthand for --log-level DEBUG)")
156	
157	    args = parser.parse_args()
158	    if args.debug:
159	        args.log_level = "DEBUG"
160	
161	    ts     = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
162	    outdir = args.outdir or (DEFAULT_OUTPUT_BASE / ts)
163	    outdir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
164	
165	    if args.stem:
166	        stem = args.stem
167	    elif args.db:
168	        stem = args.db.stem + (f"-{args.offset}" if args.offset else "")
169	    else:
170	        stem = Path(args.jsonl).stem
171	    out_path    = outdir / f"{stem}.nq"
172	    werk_path   = outdir / f"{stem}-werk-staging.duckdb"
173	    stats_path  = outdir / f"{stem}-stats.json"
174	    errors_path = outdir / f"{stem}-errors.jsonl"
175	    log_path    = outdir / f"{stem}.log"
176	
177	    logging.basicConfig(
178	        filename=str(log_path),
179	        level=getattr(logging, args.log_level),
180	        format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(message)s",
181	    )
182	    log = logging.getLogger(__name__)
183	
184	    log.info("Loading config tables")
185	    class_prop_align    = load_class_prop_alignment(args.alignment)
186	    lido_dispatch       = load_lido_event_types(args.lido)
187	    htype_map           = load_htype_map(args.htype)
188	    mediatype_class_map = load_mediatype_class(args.mediatype)
189	    audio_type2class    = load_audio_type2class(args.audio)
190	
191	    ids_set: set[str] | None = None
192	    if args.ids == "-":
193	        ids_set = {line.strip() for line in sys.stdin if line.strip()}
194	    elif args.ids:
195	        ids_set = load_ids(Path(args.ids))
196	
197	    try:
198	        import duckdb
199	        conn = duckdb.connect(str(werk_path))
200	    except ImportError:
201	        log.warning("duckdb not available — werk_staging will not be written")
202	        conn = None
203	    if conn is not None:
204	        conn.execute("""
205	        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS werk_staging (
206	            ddb_obj_id       VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
207	            cho_uri          VARCHAR,
208	            target_class     VARCHAR,
209	            dc_title         VARCHAR,
210	            dc_alternative   VARCHAR[],
211	            dc_created       VARCHAR,
212	            creator_uris     VARCHAR[],
213	            creator_literals VARCHAR[]
214	        )
215	    """)
216	
217	    emitted_entities: dict[str, str] = {}
218	    entities_conn = None
219	    if args.entities_db:
220	        try:
221	            import duckdb as _ddb_ent
222	            entities_conn = _ddb_ent.connect(str(args.entities_db))
223	            entities_conn.execute("""
224	                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS emitted_entities (
225	                    uri         VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
226	                    entity_type VARCHAR NOT NULL
227	                )
228	            """)
229	            rows = entities_conn.execute(
230	                "SELECT uri, entity_type FROM emitted_entities"
231	            ).fetchall()
232	            emitted_entities = {uri: etype for uri, etype in rows}
233	            log.info("Loaded %d emitted entities from %s",
234	                     len(emitted_entities), args.entities_db)
235	        except ImportError:
236	            log.warning("duckdb not available — --entities-db ignored")
237	        except Exception as exc:
238	            log.warning("Could not open --entities-db %s: %s", args.entities_db, exc)
239	
240	    stats_level = args.stats
241	
242	    stats:  Counter = Counter()
243	
244	    class_counts: dict[str, Counter] = {"W": Counter(), "E": Counter(), "M": Counter(), "": Counter()}
245	    mt_dist:         Counter = Counter()
246	    ht_dist:         Counter = Counter()
247	    ddbedm_cls:      Counter = Counter()
248	    ddbedm_preds:    Counter = Counter()
249	    mocho_preds_all: Counter = Counter()
250	    mocho_preds_new: Counter = Counter()
251	    werk_by_class:   Counter = Counter()
252	
253	    start_time = time.monotonic()
254	    interrupted = False
255	
256	    def _iter_input():
257	        if args.db:
258	            import gzip
259	            import sqlite3 as _sqlite3
260	            conn = _sqlite3.connect(str(args.db))
261	            try:
262	                q = "SELECT uid, bufgz FROM objs"
263	                if args.offset:
264	                    q += f" LIMIT -1 OFFSET {args.offset}"
265	                for uid, buf in conn.execute(q):
266	                    try:
267	                        yield json.dumps(json.loads(gzip.decompress(buf)))
268	                    except Exception as exc:
269	                        log.warning("%s uid=%s: %s", args.db.name, uid, exc)
270	                        yield ""
271	            finally:
272	                conn.close()
273	        else:
274	            with open(args.jsonl, encoding="utf-8") as f:
275	                yield from f
276	
277	    with open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out, \
278	         open(errors_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as err_fh:
279	
280	        for line_no, raw in enumerate(_iter_input(), 1):
281	            if _stop:
282	                interrupted = True
283	                log.warning("Interrupted at line %d — writing partial output", line_no)
284	                break
285	
286	            raw = raw.strip()
287	            if not raw:
288	                continue
289	            if args.limit and line_no > args.limit:
290	                break
291	
292	            try:
293	                record = json.loads(raw)
294	            except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
295	                entry = {"line": line_no, "issue": f"JSON parse error: {exc}"}
296	                err_fh.write(json.dumps(entry, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
297	                stats["json_errors"] += 1
298	                continue
299	
300	            obj_id = get_object_id(record) or f"line:{line_no}"
301	            try:
302	                streams, werk_row, dispatch_info, pred_info = transform_record(
303	                    record, ids_set,
304	                    mediatype_class_map, htype_map, audio_type2class,
305	                    class_prop_align, lido_dispatch,
306	                    emitted_entities=emitted_entities,
307	                )
308	            except Exception as exc:
309	                entry = {
310	                    "id":        obj_id,
311	                    "issue":     str(exc),
312	                    "traceback": traceback.format_exc(),
313	                }
314	                err_fh.write(json.dumps(entry, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
315	                stats["record_errors"] += 1
316	                continue
317	
318	            if not streams:
319	                stats["filtered"] += 1
320	                continue
321	
322	            for graph_name, graph_lines in streams.items():
323	                for nq in graph_lines:
324	                    out.write(nq + "\n")
325	                    stats["triples_total"] += 1
326	                    stats[f"triples_{graph_name}"] += 1
327	
328	            stats["records_processed"]  += 1
329	            stats["uri_sanitized"]      += pred_info.get("uri_sanitized",   0)
330	            stats["uri_split"]          += pred_info.get("uri_split",       0)
331	            stats["uri_about_split"]    += pred_info.get("uri_about_split", 0)
332	
333	            if stats_level in ("dispatch", "full"):
334	                if dispatch_info.get("is_mt007"):
335	                    stats["skipped_mt007"] += 1
336	                elif dispatch_info.get("fallback"):
337	                    stats["dispatch_fallback"] += 1
338	                elif dispatch_info.get("htype_used"):
339	                    stats["dispatch_htype"] += 1
340	                else:
341	                    stats["dispatch_mediatype"] += 1
342	
343	                tc = dispatch_info.get("target_class", "")
344	                if tc:
345	                    wemi = dispatch_info.get("wemi", "M")
346	                    class_counts[wemi][_to_curie(tc)] += 1
347	
348	                mt = (dispatch_info.get("mediatype", "") or "").split("/")[-1]
349	                if mt:
350	                    mt_dist[mt] += 1
351	
352	                rdf_top = record["edm"]["RDF"]
353	                cho_raw = rdf_top.get("ProvidedCHO") or {}
354	                if isinstance(cho_raw, list):
355	                    cho_raw = cho_raw[0] if cho_raw else {}
356	                raw_ht = (cho_raw.get("hierarchyType") or "").strip()
357	                if raw_ht:
358	                    ht_dist[raw_ht.replace("htype_", "ht")] += 1
359	
360	                ddbedm_cls.update(pred_info["ddbedm_classes"])
361	                ddbedm_preds.update(pred_info["ddbedm_preds"])
362	                mocho_preds_all.update(pred_info["mocho_preds_all"])
363	                mocho_preds_new.update(pred_info["mocho_preds_new"])
364	
365	            if werk_row and conn is not None:
366	                conn.execute(
367	                    "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO werk_staging VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)",
368	                    [
369	                        werk_row["ddb_obj_id"],
370	                        werk_row["cho_uri"],
371	                        werk_row["target_class"],
372	                        werk_row["dc_title"],
373	                        werk_row["dc_alternative"],
374	                        werk_row["dc_created"],
375	                        werk_row["creator_uris"],
376	                        werk_row["creator_literals"],
377	                    ],
378	                )
379	                stats["werk_staging_rows"] += 1
380	                werk_by_class[_to_curie(werk_row["target_class"])] += 1
381	
382	            processed = stats["records_processed"]
383	            if args.log_interval and processed and processed % args.log_interval == 0:
384	                elapsed = time.monotonic() - start_time
385	                rate    = processed / elapsed if elapsed > 0 else 0
386	                errors  = stats["json_errors"] + stats["record_errors"]
387	                eta_str = ""
388	                if args.total and rate > 0:
389	                    remaining = (args.total - processed) / rate
390	                    eta_str   = f" | ETA {_fmt_duration(remaining)}"
391	                total_str = f"/{args.total:,}" if args.total else ""
392	                log.info(
393	                    "Progress: %s%s records | triples %d | errors %d"
394	                    " | %.0f rec/s | elapsed %s%s",
395	                    f"{processed:,}", total_str,
396	                    stats["triples_total"], errors,
397	                    rate, _fmt_duration(elapsed), eta_str,
398	                )
399	
400	    if entities_conn is not None:
401	        entities_conn.executemany(
402	            "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO emitted_entities VALUES (?, ?)",
403	            list(emitted_entities.items()),
404	        )
405	        entities_conn.close()
406	        log.info("Wrote %d entities to %s", len(emitted_entities), args.entities_db)
407	
408	    if conn is not None:
409	        conn.close()
410	
411	    elapsed_total = time.monotonic() - start_time
412	
413	    if stats_level != "none":
414	        stats_out: dict = {
415	            "run": {
416	                "timestamp":   ts,
417	                "input":       str(args.jsonl),
418	                "stats_level": stats_level,
419	                "elapsed_s":   round(elapsed_total, 1),
420	                "interrupted": interrupted,
421	            },
422	            "records": {
423	                "processed":          stats["records_processed"],
424	                "skipped_not_in_ids": stats["filtered"],
425	                "by_mediatype": dict(mt_dist.most_common()),
426	                "by_htype":     dict(ht_dist.most_common()),
427	                "uri_sanitized":   stats["uri_sanitized"],
428	                "uri_split":       stats["uri_split"],
429	                "uri_about_split": stats["uri_about_split"],
430	                "errors": {
431	                    "json_parse": stats["json_errors"],
432	                    "transform":  stats["record_errors"],
433	                },
434	            },
435	            "triples": {
436	                "total":    stats["triples_total"],
437	                "by_graph": {
438	                    "ddbedm": stats["triples_ddbedm"],
439	                    "mocho":  stats["triples_mocho"],
440	                    "prov":   stats["triples_prov"],
441	                },
442	            },
443	            "werk_staging": {
444	                "rows":     stats["werk_staging_rows"],
445	                "by_class": dict(werk_by_class.most_common()),
446	            },
447	        }
448	
449	        if stats_level in ("dispatch", "full"):
450	            stats_out["dispatch"] = {
451	                "htype_hits":            stats["dispatch_htype"],
452	                "mediatype_hits":        stats["dispatch_mediatype"],
453	                "fallback_d9":           stats["dispatch_fallback"],
454	                "skipped_mt007":         stats["skipped_mt007"],
455	                "work_classes":          dict(class_counts["W"].most_common()),
456	                "expression_classes":    dict(class_counts["E"].most_common()),
457	                "manifestation_classes": dict(class_counts["M"].most_common()),
458	                "rico_classes":          dict(class_counts[""].most_common()),
459	            }
460	            stats_out["ddbedm_classes"] = dict(ddbedm_cls.most_common())
461	            stats_out["ddbedm_vocab"]   = {"properties_all": dict(ddbedm_preds.most_common())}
462	            stats_out["mocho_vocab"]    = {
463	                "properties_all": dict(mocho_preds_all.most_common()),
464	                "properties_new": dict(mocho_preds_new.most_common()),
465	            }
466	
467	        with open(stats_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
468	            json.dump(stats_out, f, indent=2)
469	
470	    status = "Interrupted" if interrupted else "Done"
471	    log.info(
472	        "%s: %d records in %s (%.0f rec/s) | triples %d (mocho %d) | errors %d",
473	        status,
474	        stats["records_processed"],
475	        _fmt_duration(elapsed_total),
476	        stats["records_processed"] / elapsed_total if elapsed_total > 0 else 0,
477	        stats["triples_total"],
478	        stats["triples_mocho"],
479	        stats["record_errors"] + stats["json_errors"],
480	    )
481	
482	
483	if __name__ == "__main__":
484	    main()
485	
```
```

241	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
242	# Parquet schema
243	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
244	
245	AGENT_STRUCT = pa.struct([pa.field("label", pa.string()), pa.field("uri", pa.string())])
246	
247	PARQUET_SCHEMA = pa.schema([
248	    ("obj_id",           pa.string()),
249	    ("lang",             pa.string()),
250	    ("title",            pa.string()),
251	    ("dc_type",          pa.string()),
252	    ("dc_creator",       pa.list_(AGENT_STRUCT)),
253	    ("dc_contributor",   pa.list_(AGENT_STRUCT)),
254	    ("dc_publisher",     pa.list_(AGENT_STRUCT)),
255	    ("dc_subject",       pa.list_(pa.string())),

257	    ("dc_date",          pa.string()),
258	    ("dc_date_qualifier", pa.string()),
259	    ("dc_issued",        pa.list_(pa.string())),
260	    ("dc_created",       pa.list_(pa.string())),
261	    ("agents",           pa.list_(pa.string())),
262	    ("hierarchy_type",   pa.string()),
263	    ("is_part_of",       pa.bool_()),
264	])
265	
266	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
267	# URI helpers
268	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
269	
270	# Characters forbidden inside N-Triples IRI references (RFC 3987 + NT spec)
271	_IRI_UNSAFE_RE = re.compile(r'[\x00-\x20<>"{}|\\^`\x7f]')
272	
273	
274	def _sanitize_iri(iri: str) -> str:
275	    """Percent-encode characters that are illegal inside NT IRI references."""
276	    return _IRI_UNSAFE_RE.sub(lambda m: f"%{ord(m.group()):02X}", iri)
277	
278	
279	def to_named_node(val: str, entity_type: str) -> px.NamedNode | None:
280	    """Mint a NamedNode for a URI or bare DDB ID.
281	
282	    ProvidedCHO bare IDs → http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/<id>
283	    All other entity types → urn:ddbedm:<ClassName>:<id>
284	    """
285	    if not val or not isinstance(val, str):
286	        return None
287	    if val.startswith("http") or val.startswith("urn"):
288	        try:
289	            return px.NamedNode(_sanitize_iri(val))
290	        except Exception:
291	            return None
292	    if entity_type == "ProvidedCHO":
293	        return px.NamedNode(DDB_ITEM + val)
294	    try:
295	        return px.NamedNode(_sanitize_iri(DDBEDM + entity_type + ":" + val))
296	    except Exception:
297	        return None
298	
299	
300	def value_to_rdf_object(val, entity_type: str) -> list[px.NamedNode | px.Literal]:
301	    """Convert a cortex JSON value to a list of RDF objects."""
302	    if val is None:
303	        return []
304	    if isinstance(val, list):
305	        result = []
306	        for item in val:
307	            result.extend(value_to_rdf_object(item, entity_type))
308	        return result
309	    if isinstance(val, dict):
310	        resource = val.get("resource")
311	        text = val.get("$")
312	        lang = val.get("lang")
313	        if resource:
314	            node = to_named_node(resource, entity_type)
315	            return [node] if node else []
316	        if text:
317	            if lang and lang not in ("", "zxx", "und"):
318	                try:
319	                    return [px.Literal(str(text), language=lang)]
320	                except Exception:
321	                    return [px.Literal(str(text))]
322	            return [px.Literal(str(text))]
323	        return []
324	    if isinstance(val, str) and val:
325	        return [px.Literal(val)]
326	    if isinstance(val, (int, float)):
327	        return [px.Literal(str(val))]
328	    return []
329	
330	
331	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
332	# EDM → RDF triples
333	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
334	
335	def build_about_type_map(rdf: dict) -> dict[str, str]:
336	    """Map each entity's bare about-value to its entity type for URI minting."""
337	    mapping = {}
338	    for entity_type, val in rdf.items():
339	        if entity_type not in PRED:
340	            continue
341	        entities = val if isinstance(val, list) else [val]
342	        for entity in entities:
343	            if not isinstance(entity, dict):
344	                continue
345	            about = entity.get("about")
346	            if about and isinstance(about, str):
347	                mapping[about] = entity_type
348	    return mapping
349	
350	
351	def entity_to_triples(
352	    entity_type: str,
353	    entity: dict,
354	    about_type_map: dict,
355	) -> list[px.Triple]:
356	    """Return all triples for one entity."""
357	    triples = []
358	    about = entity.get("about")
359	    if not about:
360	        return triples
361	
362	    entity_type_for_about = about_type_map.get(about, entity_type)
363	    subj = to_named_node(about, entity_type_for_about)
364	    if subj is None:
365	        return triples
366	
367	    rdf_type = ENTITY_RDF_TYPE.get(entity_type)
368	    if rdf_type:
369	        triples.append(px.Triple(subj, px.NamedNode(RDF_TYPE_URI), px.NamedNode(rdf_type)))
370	
371	    pred_map = PRED.get(entity_type, {})
372	    for field, raw_val in entity.items():
373	        if field == "about" or raw_val is None:
374	            continue
375	        pred_uri = pred_map.get(field)
376	        if not pred_uri:
377	            continue
378	        pred = px.NamedNode(pred_uri)
379	        for obj in value_to_rdf_object(raw_val, entity_type):
380	            triples.append(px.Triple(subj, pred, obj))
381	
382	    return triples
383	
384	
385	def record_to_triples(data: dict) -> bytes:
386	    """Convert one cortex JSON blob to N-Triples bytes."""
387	    rdf = data.get("edm", {}).get("RDF") or {}
388	    all_triples = []
389	
390	    about_type_map = build_about_type_map(rdf)
391	
392	    for entity_type, val in rdf.items():
393	        if val is None:
394	            continue
395	        entities = val if isinstance(val, list) else [val]
396	        for entity in entities:
397	            if not isinstance(entity, dict):
398	                continue
399	            try:
400	                all_triples.extend(entity_to_triples(entity_type, entity, about_type_map))
401	            except Exception:
402	                pass
403	
404	    return px.serialize(all_triples, format=px.RdfFormat.N_TRIPLES)
405	
406	
407	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
408	# Parquet metadata extraction
409	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
410	
411	_DATE_QUALIFIER_RE = re.compile(r"\(([^)]+)\)")
412	_DATE_YYYYMMDD_RE  = re.compile(r"^(\d{4})(\d{2})(\d{2})$")
413	_DATE_YYYY_RE      = re.compile(r"\d{4}")
414	
415	
416	def parse_dc_date(raw: str) -> tuple[str | None, str | None]:
417	    """Return (normalised_date, qualifier) from a free-text dc:date string."""
418	    if not raw:
419	        return None, None
420	    qualifier = None
421	    m = _DATE_QUALIFIER_RE.search(raw)
422	    if m:
423	        qualifier = m.group(1).strip()
424	        raw = raw[:m.start()].strip()
425	
426	    raw = raw.strip("[]").strip()
427	
428	    # yyyymmdd → yyyy-mm-dd
429	    m = _DATE_YYYYMMDD_RE.match(raw)
430	    if m:
431	        return f"{m.group(1)}-{m.group(2)}-{m.group(3)}", qualifier
432	
433	    # already ISO or range — keep as-is if it starts with a digit
434	    if raw and raw[0].isdigit():
435	        return raw, qualifier
436	
437	    # fallback: extract first 4-digit year
438	    m = _DATE_YYYY_RE.search(raw)
439	    if m:
440	        return m.group(0), qualifier
441	
442	    return None, qualifier
443	
444	
445	def _scalar_values(field_val) -> list[str]:
446	    """Extract string values from a cortex JSON field (scalar, dict, or list)."""
447	    if field_val is None:
448	        return []
449	    if isinstance(field_val, str):
450	        return [field_val] if field_val else []
451	    if isinstance(field_val, dict):
452	        v = field_val.get("$") or field_val.get("resource") or ""
453	        return [v] if v else []
454	    if isinstance(field_val, list):
455	        out = []
456	        for item in field_val:
457	            out.extend(_scalar_values(item))
458	        return out
459	    return [str(field_val)]
460	
461	
462	def _resource_values(field_val, entity_type: str) -> list[str]:
463	    """Extract minted URI strings from resource references."""
464	    if field_val is None:
465	        return []
466	    if isinstance(field_val, list):
467	        out = []
468	        for item in field_val:
469	            out.extend(_resource_values(item, entity_type))
470	        return out
471	    if isinstance(field_val, dict):
472	        r = field_val.get("resource")
473	        if r:
474	            node = to_named_node(r, entity_type)
475	            return [node.value] if node else []
476	    return []
477	
478	
479	def _agent_structs(field_val, entity_type: str) -> list[dict]:
480	    """Extract {label, uri} dicts from a cortex agent field, pairing label and URI
481	    when both appear in the same dict entry."""
482	    if field_val is None:
483	        return []
484	    if isinstance(field_val, list):
485	        out = []
486	        for item in field_val:
487	            out.extend(_agent_structs(item, entity_type))
488	        return out
489	    if isinstance(field_val, dict):
490	        label    = field_val.get("$") or None
491	        resource = field_val.get("resource") or None
492	        uri = None
493	        if resource:
494	            node = to_named_node(resource, entity_type)
495	            uri = node.value if node else None
496	        if label or uri:
497	            return [{"label": label, "uri": uri}]
498	        return []
499	    if isinstance(field_val, str) and field_val:
500	        return [{"label": field_val, "uri": None}]
501	    return []
502	
503	
504	def extract_meta(data: dict) -> dict:
505	    """Extract ProvidedCHO metadata for the Parquet row."""
506	    props = data.get("properties", {})
507	    rdf   = data.get("edm", {}).get("RDF") or {}
508	    cho   = rdf.get("ProvidedCHO") or {}
509	    agg   = rdf.get("Aggregation") or {}
510	
511	    # build Event and TimeSpan lookup for date extraction
512	    events    = rdf.get("Event") or []
513	    if isinstance(events, dict):
514	        events = [events]
515	    timespans = rdf.get("TimeSpan") or []
516	    if isinstance(timespans, dict):
517	        timespans = [timespans]
518	    ts_map = {t["about"]: t for t in timespans if isinstance(t, dict) and t.get("about")}
519	
520	    # dc:date (normalised)
521	    dc_date_raw = _scalar_values(cho.get("date"))
522	    dc_date, dc_date_qualifier = (None, None)
523	    if dc_date_raw:
524	        dc_date, dc_date_qualifier = parse_dc_date(dc_date_raw[0])
525	
526	    # dc_created via event chain
527	    dc_created = []
528	    for ev in events:
529	        if not isinstance(ev, dict):
530	            continue
531	        ht = (ev.get("hasType") or {})
532	        ht_uri = ht.get("resource") if isinstance(ht, dict) else None
533	        if ht_uri not in LIDO_CREATED:
534	            continue
535	        oc = ev.get("occuredAt")
536	        if not oc:
537	            continue
538	        ts_ref = oc.get("resource") if isinstance(oc, dict) else oc
539	        ts = ts_map.get(ts_ref, {}) if isinstance(ts_ref, str) else {}
540	        for key in ("begin", "end"):
541	            vals = _scalar_values(ts.get(key))
542	            dc_created.extend(vals)
543	
544	    # dc_issued via event chain + ProvidedCHO.issued
545	    dc_issued = _scalar_values(cho.get("issued"))
546	    for ev in events:
547	        if not isinstance(ev, dict):
548	            continue
549	        ht = (ev.get("hasType") or {})
550	        ht_uri = ht.get("resource") if isinstance(ht, dict) else None
551	        if ht_uri not in LIDO_ISSUED:
552	            continue
553	        oc = ev.get("occuredAt")
554	        if not oc:
555	            continue
556	        ts_ref = oc.get("resource") if isinstance(oc, dict) else oc
557	        ts = ts_map.get(ts_ref, {}) if isinstance(ts_ref, str) else {}
558	        for key in ("begin", "end"):
559	            vals = _scalar_values(ts.get(key))
560	            dc_issued.extend(vals)
561	
562	    # agents via hasMet → Event.P11_had_participant
563	    agents = []
564	    hasMet = cho.get("hasMet") or []
565	    if isinstance(hasMet, dict):
566	        hasMet = [hasMet]
567	    event_map = {
568	        e["about"]: e for e in events
569	        if isinstance(e, dict) and e.get("about")
570	    }
571	    about_type_map = build_about_type_map(rdf)
572	    for hm in hasMet:
573	        if not isinstance(hm, dict):
574	            continue
575	        ev_ref = hm.get("resource")
576	        if not ev_ref:
577	            continue
578	        ev = event_map.get(ev_ref, {})
579	        participants = ev.get("P11_had_participant") or []
580	        if isinstance(participants, dict):
581	            participants = [participants]
582	        for p in participants:
583	            if not isinstance(p, dict):
584	                continue
585	            r = p.get("resource")
586	            if r:
587	                et = about_type_map.get(r, "Agent")
588	                node = to_named_node(r, et)
589	                if node:
590	                    agents.append(node.value)
591	
592	    # dc_publisher: ProvidedCHO.publisher + Aggregation.dataProvider
593	    # dataProvider entries may be separate label/URI dicts in the same list, not paired
594	    dp = agg.get("dataProvider") or []
595	    if isinstance(dp, dict):
596	        dp = [dp]
597	    publisher_structs = (
598	        _agent_structs(cho.get("publisher"), "Agent")
599	        + _agent_structs(dp, "Organization")
600	    )
601	
602	    return {
603	        "obj_id":        props.get("item-id", ""),
604	        "lang":          cho.get("language") or "",
605	        "title":         (_scalar_values(cho.get("title")) or [""])[0],
606	        "dc_type":       (_scalar_values(cho.get("dcType")) or [""])[0],
607	        "dc_creator":    _agent_structs(cho.get("creator"), "Agent"),
608	        "dc_contributor": _agent_structs(cho.get("contributor"), "Agent"),
609	        "dc_publisher":  publisher_structs,
610	        "dc_subject":       _scalar_values(cho.get("dcSubject")),
611	        "dc_subject_uris":  _resource_values(cho.get("dcTermsSubject"), "Concept"),
612	        "dc_date":          dc_date,
613	        "dc_date_qualifier": dc_date_qualifier,
614	        "dc_issued":        dc_issued,
615	        "dc_created":       dc_created,
616	        "agents":           agents,
617	        "hierarchy_type":   (_scalar_values(cho.get("hierarchyType")) or [None])[0],
618	        "is_part_of":       bool(cho.get("isPartOf")),
619	    }
620	
621	
622	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
623	# Worker process
624	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
625	
626	def _iter_json(path: str):
627	    """Yield (uid, json_bytes) for each record in a JSON array or JSON Lines file."""
628	    with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
629	        first = f.read(1)
630	        f.seek(0)
631	        if first == "[":
632	            records = json.load(f)
633	            for rec in records:
634	                uid = (rec.get("properties") or {}).get("item-id", "")
635	                yield uid, json.dumps(rec, ensure_ascii=False).encode()
636	        else:
637	            for line in f:
638	                line = line.strip()
639	                if not line:
640	                    continue
641	                rec = json.loads(line)
642	                uid = (rec.get("properties") or {}).get("item-id", "")
643	                yield uid, json.dumps(rec, ensure_ascii=False).encode()
644	
645	
646	def worker(
647	    worker_id: int,
648	    work_q: multiprocessing.Queue,
649	    meta_q: multiprocessing.Queue,
650	    compressed: bool = True,
651	    stem: str = "out",
652	) -> None:
653	    batch_triples: list[bytes] = []
654	    batch_meta:    list[dict]  = []
655	    batch_num = _next_batch_num(worker_id, stem)
656	    processed = 0
657	
658	    def flush_nt():
659	        nonlocal batch_num
660	        if not batch_triples:
661	            return
662	        path = Path(OUTPUT_DIR) / f"ddbedm-{stem}_{worker_id:02d}_{batch_num:04d}.nt"
663	        with open(path, "wb") as f:
664	            for chunk in batch_triples:
665	                f.write(chunk)
666	        batch_triples.clear()
667	        batch_num += 1
668	
669	    def flush_meta():
670	        if not batch_meta:
671	            return
672	        meta_q.put(list(batch_meta))
673	        batch_meta.clear()
674	
675	    while True:
676	        item = work_q.get()
677	        if item is None:
678	            flush_nt()
679	            flush_meta()
680	            break
681	        uid, blob = item
682	        try:
683	            data = json.loads(gzip.decompress(blob) if compressed else blob)
684	            nt_bytes = record_to_triples(data)
685	            meta_row = extract_meta(data)
686	            batch_triples.append(nt_bytes)
687	            batch_meta.append(meta_row)
688	            processed += 1
689	        except Exception:
690	            traceback.print_exc()
691	            continue
692	
693	        if processed % WORKER_REPORT_INTERVAL == 0:
694	            logging.info(f"Worker {worker_id}: processed {processed:,} records")
695	
696	        if len(batch_triples) >= BATCH_SIZE:
697	            flush_nt()
698	            flush_meta()
699	
700	
701	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
702	# Meta writer process
703	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
704	
705	def meta_writer(meta_q: multiprocessing.Queue, output_path: str) -> None:
706	    tmp_path = output_path + ".tmp"
707	    writer = None
708	    total = 0
709	    buffer: list[dict] = []
710	
711	    def flush():
712	        nonlocal writer, total
713	        if not buffer:
714	            return
715	        arrays = {col: [row.get(col) for row in buffer] for col in PARQUET_SCHEMA.names}
716	        table = pa.table(arrays, schema=PARQUET_SCHEMA)
717	        if writer is None:
718	            writer = pq.ParquetWriter(tmp_path, PARQUET_SCHEMA)
719	        writer.write_table(table)
720	        total += len(buffer)
721	        buffer.clear()
722	
723	    while True:
724	        try:
725	            batch = meta_q.get(timeout=5)
726	        except queue.Empty:
727	            continue
728	        if batch is None:
729	            break
730	        buffer.extend(batch)
731	        if len(buffer) >= PARQUET_CHUNK:
732	            flush()
733	
734	    flush()
735	    if writer:
736	        writer.close()
737	        Path(tmp_path).rename(output_path)
738	        logging.info(f"Parquet writer: {total:,} rows written → {output_path}")
739	    else:
740	        logging.warning("Parquet writer: no rows written")
741	
742	
743	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
744	# Checkpoint helpers
745	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
746	
747	def _checkpoint_path() -> Path:
748	    return Path(OUTPUT_DIR) / ".export_progress.json"
749	
750	
751	def _load_checkpoint() -> int:
752	    """Return last rowid successfully queued, or 0 if no checkpoint."""
753	    p = _checkpoint_path()
754	    if p.exists():
755	        try:
756	            return int(json.loads(p.read_text()).get("last_rowid", 0))
757	        except Exception:
758	            return 0
759	    return 0
760	
761	
762	def _save_checkpoint(last_rowid: int) -> None:
763	    _checkpoint_path().write_text(json.dumps({"last_rowid": last_rowid}))
764	
765	
766	def _next_batch_num(worker_id: int, stem: str) -> int:
767	    """Scan OUTPUT_DIR to find the next unused batch number for this worker."""
768	    existing = sorted(Path(OUTPUT_DIR).glob(f"ddbedm-{stem}_{worker_id:02d}_*.nt"))
769	    if not existing:
770	        return 0
771	    return int(existing[-1].stem.split("_")[-1]) + 1
772	
773	
774	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
775	# Main
776	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
777	
778	def main() -> None:
779	    if len(sys.argv) != 2:
780	        print(f"Usage: {sys.argv[0]} <input.sqlite|input.json>", file=sys.stderr)
781	        sys.exit(1)
782	
783	    input_path = sys.argv[1]
784	    suffix     = Path(input_path).suffix.lower()
785	    is_json    = suffix == ".json"
786	    compressed = not is_json
787	
788	    Path(OUTPUT_DIR).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
789	    stem         = Path(input_path).stem
790	    parquet_path = str(Path(OUTPUT_DIR) / f"{stem}_meta.parquet")
791	
792	    logging.info(f"Workers: {MAX_WORKERS}, batch size: {BATCH_SIZE:,}")
793	    logging.info(f"Output dir: {OUTPUT_DIR}")
794	
795	    work_q = multiprocessing.Queue(MAX_WORKERS * 4)
796	    meta_q = multiprocessing.Queue(1000)
797	
798	    workers = [
799	        multiprocessing.Process(target=worker, args=(i, work_q, meta_q, compressed, stem), daemon=True)
800	        for i in range(MAX_WORKERS)
801	    ]
802	    for w in workers:
803	        w.start()
804	
805	    writer_proc = multiprocessing.Process(
806	        target=meta_writer, args=(meta_q, parquet_path), daemon=True
807	    )
808	    writer_proc.start()
809	
810	    count = 0
811	
812	    if is_json:
813	        records = list(_iter_json(input_path))
814	        total   = len(records)
815	        logging.info(f"Processing {total:,} records from {input_path}")
816	        for uid, blob in records:
817	            work_q.put((uid, blob))
818	            count += 1
819	            if count % REPORT_INTERVAL == 0:
820	                logging.info(f"Queued {count:,} / {total:,}")
821	
822	    else:
823	        last_rowid = _load_checkpoint()
824	        if last_rowid:
825	            logging.info(f"Resuming from rowid {last_rowid:,}")
826	            if Path(parquet_path).exists():
827	                logging.warning(
828	                    f"Existing {parquet_path} found — this run will write a new file "
829	                    f"covering only rows after rowid {last_rowid:,}. "
830	                    f"Concatenate both files afterwards."
831	                )
832	                parquet_path = str(Path(OUTPUT_DIR) / f"{stem}_meta_resume_{last_rowid}.parquet")
833	
834	        db  = sqlite3.connect(input_path)
835	        cur = db.cursor()
836	        cur.execute("SELECT max(rowid) FROM objs")
837	        total = (cur.fetchone()[0] or 0) - last_rowid
838	        logging.info(f"Processing ~{total:,} records from {input_path}")
839	
840	        cur.execute(
841	            "SELECT rowid, uid, bufgz FROM objs WHERE bufgz IS NOT NULL AND rowid > ? ORDER BY rowid",
842	            (last_rowid,),
843	        )
844	        last_seen_rowid = last_rowid
845	        for rowid, uid, blob in cur:
846	            work_q.put((uid, blob))
847	            count += 1
848	            last_seen_rowid = rowid
849	            if count % REPORT_INTERVAL == 0:
850	                _save_checkpoint(last_seen_rowid)
851	                logging.info(f"Queued {count:,} / ~{total:,} (rowid {last_seen_rowid:,})")
852	
853	        _checkpoint_path().unlink(missing_ok=True)
854	
855	    for _ in range(MAX_WORKERS):
856	        work_q.put(None)
857	
858	    for w in workers:
859	        w.join()
860	
861	    meta_q.put(None)
862	    writer_proc.join()
863	
864	    logging.info(f"Done. {count:,} records processed.")
865	
866	
867	if __name__ == "__main__":
868	    main()
869	
```

```
45	def extract_meta(data: dict) -> dict:
46	    """Extract ProvidedCHO metadata for the Parquet row.
47	
48	    Fixes vs. export_ddb.extract_meta:
49	      - dc_created: read dcterms:created from ProvidedCHO directly first,
50	        then extend with LIDO creation-event chain.
51	      - dc_issued:  read dcterms:issued from ProvidedCHO directly first (was
52	        already done), then extend with LIDO publication-event chain.
53	    Both are now consistent: direct ProvidedCHO field → hasMet traversal.
54	    """
55	    props = data.get("properties", {})
56	    rdf   = data.get("edm", {}).get("RDF") or {}
57	    cho   = rdf.get("ProvidedCHO") or {}
58	    agg   = rdf.get("Aggregation") or {}
59	
60	    # build Event and TimeSpan lookup for date extraction
61	    events = rdf.get("Event") or []
62	    if isinstance(events, dict):
63	        events = [events]
64	    timespans = rdf.get("TimeSpan") or []
65	    if isinstance(timespans, dict):
66	        timespans = [timespans]
67	    ts_map = {t["about"]: t for t in timespans if isinstance(t, dict) and t.get("about")}
68	
69	    # dc:date (normalised)
70	    dc_date_raw = _scalar_values(cho.get("date"))
71	    dc_date, dc_date_qualifier = (None, None)
72	    if dc_date_raw:
73	        dc_date, dc_date_qualifier = parse_dc_date(dc_date_raw[0])
74	
75	    # dc_created: ProvidedCHO.dcterms:created first, then hasMet traversal
76	    dc_created = _scalar_values(cho.get("created"))
77	    for ev in events:
78	        if not isinstance(ev, dict):
79	            continue
80	        ht = (ev.get("hasType") or {})
81	        ht_uri = ht.get("resource") if isinstance(ht, dict) else None
82	        if ht_uri not in LIDO_CREATED:
83	            continue
84	        oc = ev.get("occuredAt")
85	        if not oc:
86	            continue
87	        ts_ref = oc.get("resource") if isinstance(oc, dict) else oc
88	        ts = ts_map.get(ts_ref, {}) if isinstance(ts_ref, str) else {}
89	        for key in ("begin", "end"):
90	            dc_created.extend(_scalar_values(ts.get(key)))
91	
92	    # dc_issued: ProvidedCHO.dcterms:issued first, then hasMet traversal
93	    dc_issued = _scalar_values(cho.get("issued"))
94	    for ev in events:
95	        if not isinstance(ev, dict):
96	            continue
97	        ht = (ev.get("hasType") or {})
98	        ht_uri = ht.get("resource") if isinstance(ht, dict) else None
99	        if ht_uri not in LIDO_ISSUED:
100	            continue
101	        oc = ev.get("occuredAt")
102	        if not oc:
103	            continue
104	        ts_ref = oc.get("resource") if isinstance(oc, dict) else oc
105	        ts = ts_map.get(ts_ref, {}) if isinstance(ts_ref, str) else {}
106	        for key in ("begin", "end"):
107	            dc_issued.extend(_scalar_values(ts.get(key)))
108	
109	    # agents via hasMet → Event.P11_had_participant
110	    agents = []

112	    if isinstance(hasMet, dict):
113	        hasMet = [hasMet]
114	    event_map = {
115	        e["about"]: e for e in events
116	        if isinstance(e, dict) and e.get("about")
117	    }
118	    about_type_map = build_about_type_map(rdf)
119	    for hm in hasMet:
120	        if not isinstance(hm, dict):
121	            continue
122	        ev_ref = hm.get("resource")
123	        if not ev_ref:
124	            continue
125	        ev = event_map.get(ev_ref, {})
126	        participants = ev.get("P11_had_participant") or []
127	        if isinstance(participants, dict):
128	            participants = [participants]
129	        for p in participants:
130	            if not isinstance(p, dict):
131	                continue
132	            r = p.get("resource")
133	            if r:
134	                et = about_type_map.get(r, "Agent")
135	                node = to_named_node(r, et)
136	                if node:
137	                    agents.append(node.value)
138	
139	    # dc_publisher: ProvidedCHO.publisher + Aggregation.dataProvider
140	    dp = agg.get("dataProvider") or []

142	        dp = [dp]
143	    publisher_structs = (
144	        _agent_structs(cho.get("publisher"), "Agent")
145	        + _agent_structs(dp, "Organization")
146	    )
147	
148	    return {
149	        "obj_id":            props.get("item-id", ""),
150	        "lang":              cho.get("language") or "",
151	        "title":             (_scalar_values(cho.get("title")) or [""])[0],
152	        "dc_type":           (_scalar_values(cho.get("dcType")) or [""])[0],
153	        "dc_creator":        _agent_structs(cho.get("creator"), "Agent"),
154	        "dc_contributor":    _agent_structs(cho.get("contributor"), "Agent"),

157	        "dc_subject_uris":   _resource_values(cho.get("dcTermsSubject"), "Concept"),

168	MAX_WORKERS   = int(os.environ.get("MAX_WORKERS", max(1, multiprocessing.cpu_count() - 2)))
169	PARQUET_CHUNK = int(os.environ.get("PARQUET_CHUNK", 500_000))

181	
182	def worker(
183	    worker_id: int,
184	    work_q: multiprocessing.Queue,
185	    meta_q: multiprocessing.Queue,
186	    compressed: bool = True,
187	) -> None:
188	    processed = 0
189	    batch: list[dict] = []
190	
191	    def flush():
192	        if not batch:
193	            return
194	        meta_q.put(list(batch))
195	        batch.clear()
196	
197	    while True:
198	        item = work_q.get()
199	        if item is None:
200	            flush()
201	            break
202	        uid, blob = item
203	        try:
204	            data = json.loads(gzip.decompress(blob) if compressed else blob)
205	            batch.append(extract_meta(data))
206	            processed += 1
207	        except Exception:
208	            traceback.print_exc()
209	            continue
210	
211	        if processed % WORKER_REPORT_INTERVAL == 0:
212	            logging.info(f"Worker {worker_id}: processed {processed:,} records")
213	
214	        if len(batch) >= 10_000:
215	            flush()

221	
222	def meta_writer(meta_q: multiprocessing.Queue, output_path: str) -> None:
223	    tmp_path = output_path + ".tmp"
224	    writer = None
225	    total = 0
226	    buffer: list[dict] = []
227	
228	    def flush():
229	        nonlocal writer, total
230	        if not buffer:
231	            return
232	        arrays = {col: [row.get(col) for row in buffer] for col in PARQUET_SCHEMA.names}
233	        table = pa.table(arrays, schema=PARQUET_SCHEMA)
234	        if writer is None:
235	            writer = pq.ParquetWriter(tmp_path, PARQUET_SCHEMA)
236	        writer.write_table(table)
237	        total += len(buffer)
238	        buffer.clear()
239	
240	    while True:
241	        try:
242	            batch = meta_q.get(timeout=5)
243	        except queue.Empty:
244	            continue
245	        if batch is None:
246	            break
247	        buffer.extend(batch)
248	        if len(buffer) >= PARQUET_CHUNK:
249	            flush()
250	
251	    flush()
252	    if writer:
253	        writer.close()
254	        Path(tmp_path).rename(output_path)
255	        logging.info(f"Parquet writer: {total:,} rows written → {output_path}")
256	    else:
257	        logging.warning("Parquet writer: no rows written")
258	
259	
260	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
261	# Main
262	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
263	
264	def main() -> None:
265	    if len(sys.argv) != 2:
266	        print(f"Usage: {sys.argv[0]} <input.sqlite|input.json>", file=sys.stderr)
267	        sys.exit(1)
268	
269	    input_path = sys.argv[1]
270	    is_json    = Path(input_path).suffix.lower() == ".json"
271	    compressed = not is_json
272	
273	    Path(OUTPUT_DIR).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
274	    stem         = Path(input_path).stem
275	    parquet_path = str(Path(OUTPUT_DIR) / f"{stem}_meta.parquet")
276	
277	    logging.info(f"Workers: {MAX_WORKERS}")
278	    logging.info(f"Output: {parquet_path}")
279	
280	    work_q = multiprocessing.Queue(MAX_WORKERS * 4)
281	    meta_q = multiprocessing.Queue(1000)
282	
283	    workers = [
284	        multiprocessing.Process(target=worker, args=(i, work_q, meta_q, compressed), daemon=True)
285	        for i in range(MAX_WORKERS)
286	    ]
287	    for w in workers:
288	        w.start()
289	
290	    writer_proc = multiprocessing.Process(
291	        target=meta_writer, args=(meta_q, parquet_path), daemon=True
292	    )
293	    writer_proc.start()
294	
295	    count = 0
296	
297	    if is_json:
298	        records = list(_iter_json(input_path))
299	        total   = len(records)
300	        logging.info(f"Processing {total:,} records from {input_path}")
301	        for uid, blob in records:
302	            work_q.put((uid, blob))
303	            count += 1
304	            if count % REPORT_INTERVAL == 0:
305	                logging.info(f"Queued {count:,} / {total:,}")
306	    else:
307	        db = sqlite3.connect(input_path)
308	        cur = db.cursor()
309	        cur.execute("SELECT max(rowid) FROM objs")
310	        total = cur.fetchone()[0] or 0
311	        logging.info(f"Processing ~{total:,} records from {input_path}")
312	
313	        cur.execute("SELECT uid, bufgz FROM objs WHERE bufgz IS NOT NULL ORDER BY rowid")
314	        for uid, blob in cur:
315	            work_q.put((uid, blob))
316	            count += 1
317	            if count % REPORT_INTERVAL == 0:
318	                logging.info(f"Queued {count:,} / ~{total:,}")
319	
320	    for _ in range(MAX_WORKERS):
321	        work_q.put(None)
322	
323	    for w in workers:
324	        w.join()
325	
326	    meta_q.put(None)
327	    writer_proc.join()
328	
329	    logging.info(f"Done. {count:,} records processed.")
330	
331	
332	if __name__ == "__main__":
333	    main()
334	
```

### User *(2026-05-15T13:33:12.021Z)*

**Tool result**
```
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Purpose:   Full GeMeA corpus transform — 128 workers across 7 sectors in parallel (Option C, teach03)
3	# Usage:     bash scripts/run_gemea_transform.sh --new      # wipe previous output and restart
4	#            bash scripts/run_gemea_transform.sh --resume   # skip sectors already done
5	#            Run from the goethe-faust project root, inside a tmux/screen session.
6	# Inputs:    /data/ddb/data/s{1..7}.sqlite
7	# Outputs:   $OUT_BASE/s{1..7}/   (per-sector .nq, .duckdb, -stats.json, -errors.jsonl, .log)
8	#            $OUT_BASE/werk-staging-merged.duckdb
9	#            $OUT_BASE/nt/ddbedm.nt, mocho.nt, prov.nt
10	# Deps:      .venv/ created by scripts/setup_venv.sh (optional), duckdb via pip3 --user,
11	#            scripts/split_nq.py (stdlib only)
12	# Notes:     Sectors with >1 worker: export → split JSONL → N parallel transforms.
13	#            Sectors with 1 worker:  export → transform (no split).
14	#            cd into scripts/ before python3 -m transform so duckdb (user site-packages)
15	#            is visible without PYTHONPATH.
16	
17	set -euo pipefail
18	
19	# ── Args ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
20	MODE=""
21	for arg in "$@"; do
22	  case "$arg" in
23	    --new)    MODE=new ;;
24	    --resume) MODE=resume ;;
25	    *) echo "Usage: $0 [--new|--resume]" >&2; exit 1 ;;
26	  esac
27	done
28	if [[ -z "$MODE" ]]; then
29	  echo "Error: specify --new (wipe and restart) or --resume (skip completed sectors)" >&2
30	  exit 1
31	fi
32	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
33	
34	# ── Paths ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
35	GOETHE="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
36	SQLITE_DIR=/data/ddb/data
37	EXPORT_DIR=/data/ddb/gemea/json-export
38	SCRIPTS=$GOETHE/scripts
39	HASH=$(find "$SCRIPTS/transform" -name "*.py" | sort | xargs sha256sum | sha256sum | cut -c1-4)
40	VERSION="$(date '+%Y%m%d')-$(date '+%H')${HASH}"
41	OUT_BASE=/data/ddb/gemea/mocho-transform/$VERSION
42	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
43	
44	CFG=$GOETHE/output/config
45	PYTHON=$( [[ -x "$GOETHE/.venv/bin/python3" ]] && echo "$GOETHE/.venv/bin/python3" || echo python3 )
46	
47	# Workers per sector — 128 total (50% of 256 cores on teach03)
48	# Distributed proportionally to record count so all sectors finish simultaneously.
49	declare -A WORKERS=([1]=17 [2]=86 [3]=1 [4]=6 [5]=8 [6]=9 [7]=1)
50	
51	mkdir -p "$EXPORT_DIR" "$OUT_BASE"
52	
53	if [[ "$MODE" == "new" ]]; then
54	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] --new: wiping previous output"
55	  rm -rf "$OUT_BASE"/s{1..7} "$OUT_BASE/nt" "$OUT_BASE/werk-staging-merged.duckdb"
56	fi
57	
58	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Starting GeMeA transform (${MODE}) — 128 workers across 7 sectors"
59	echo "  GOETHE     = $GOETHE"
60	echo "  SQLITE_DIR = $SQLITE_DIR"
61	echo "  EXPORT_DIR = $EXPORT_DIR"
62	echo "  OUT_BASE   = $OUT_BASE"
63	echo "  Workers    = s1:${WORKERS[1]} s2:${WORKERS[2]} s3:${WORKERS[3]} s4:${WORKERS[4]}" \
64	     "s5:${WORKERS[5]} s6:${WORKERS[6]} s7:${WORKERS[7]}"
65	
66	# ── Phase 1+2: export then transform (chunked where W>1) ──────────────────────
67	for n in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7; do
68	  W=${WORKERS[$n]}
69	  (
70	    mkdir -p "$OUT_BASE/s${n}"
71	
72	    # --resume: skip if any .nq already exists and is non-empty
73	    if [[ "$MODE" == "resume" ]]; then
74	      existing=$(find "$OUT_BASE/s${n}" -name "*.nq" -size +0 2>/dev/null | head -1)
75	      if [[ -n "$existing" ]]; then
76	        echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] skipping — output exists"
77	        exit 0
78	      fi
79	    fi
80	
81	    # Export full sector JSONL
82	    echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] export starting"
83	    cd "$SCRIPTS"
84	    "$PYTHON" -m transform.sqlite_export \
85	      --db  "$SQLITE_DIR/s${n}.sqlite" \
86	      --out "$EXPORT_DIR/s${n}.jsonl" \
87	      2>> "$OUT_BASE/s${n}/export.log"
88	    echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] export done"
89	
90	    if [[ $W -eq 1 ]]; then
91	      # Single worker — transform directly
92	      TOTAL=$(wc -l < "$EXPORT_DIR/s${n}.jsonl")
93	      echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] transform starting (1 worker, ${TOTAL} records)"
94	      cd "$SCRIPTS"
95	      "$PYTHON" -m transform \
96	        --jsonl      "$EXPORT_DIR/s${n}.jsonl" \
97	        --outdir     "$OUT_BASE/s${n}" \
98	        --stats      dispatch \
99	        --total      "$TOTAL" \
100	        --alignment  "$CFG/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv" \
101	        --lido       "$CFG/lido_event_types.csv" \
102	        --htype      "$CFG/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv" \
103	        --mediatype  "$CFG/lookup_mediatype_class.csv" \
104	        --audio      "$CFG/audio_type2class.json"
105	    else
106	      # Multiple workers — split JSONL into W chunks then transform in parallel
107	      CHUNK_DIR="$EXPORT_DIR/s${n}_chunks"
108	      mkdir -p "$CHUNK_DIR"
109	      echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] splitting into ${W} chunks"
110	      split -n l/$W "$EXPORT_DIR/s${n}.jsonl" "$CHUNK_DIR/chunk_"
111	
112	      echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] launching ${W} transform workers"
113	      for chunk in "$CHUNK_DIR"/chunk_*; do
114	        stem=$(basename "$chunk")
115	        TOTAL=$(wc -l < "$chunk")
116	        mkdir -p "$OUT_BASE/s${n}/$stem"
117	        (
118	          cd "$SCRIPTS"
119	          "$PYTHON" -m transform \
120	            --jsonl      "$chunk" \
121	            --outdir     "$OUT_BASE/s${n}/$stem" \
122	            --stats      dispatch \
123	            --total      "$TOTAL" \
124	            --alignment  "$CFG/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv" \
125	            --lido       "$CFG/lido_event_types.csv" \
126	            --htype      "$CFG/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv" \
127	            --mediatype  "$CFG/lookup_mediatype_class.csv" \
128	            --audio      "$CFG/audio_type2class.json"
129	        ) &
130	      done
131	      wait  # wait for all chunk workers of this sector
132	    fi
133	
134	    echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] done"
135	  ) &
136	done
137	
138	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] All sector workers launched — waiting for completion..."
139	wait
140	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] All sectors complete — merging"
141	
142	# ── Phase 3: merge DuckDB werk_staging ───────────────────────────────────────
143	OUT_BASE="$OUT_BASE" "$PYTHON" <<'PYEOF'
144	import glob, os, sys
145	try:
146	    import duckdb
147	except ImportError:
148	    print("duckdb not available — skipping werk_staging merge")
149	    sys.exit(0)
150	out_base = os.environ["OUT_BASE"]
151	shards = sorted(glob.glob(f"{out_base}/**/*-werk-staging.duckdb", recursive=True))
152	if not shards:
153	    print("No werk_staging shards found — skipping DuckDB merge")
154	    sys.exit(0)
155	out = f"{out_base}/werk-staging-merged.duckdb"
156	conn = duckdb.connect(out)
157	conn.execute(f"CREATE TABLE werk_staging AS SELECT * FROM '{shards[0]}'")
158	for p in shards[1:]:
159	    conn.execute(f"INSERT INTO werk_staging SELECT * FROM '{p}'")
160	rows = conn.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM werk_staging").fetchone()[0]
161	conn.close()
162	print(f"werk_staging merged ({len(shards)} shards, {rows} rows) → {out}")
163	PYEOF
164	
165	# ── Phase 4: split each .nq → per-chunk .nt, then merge by graph ─────────────
166	NT_DIR=$OUT_BASE/nt
167	mkdir -p "$NT_DIR"
168	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Splitting N-Quads into per-chunk .nt files"
169	while IFS= read -r nq; do
170	  "$PYTHON" "$SCRIPTS/split_nq.py" "$nq" &
171	done < <(find "$OUT_BASE" -name "*.nq" | sort)
172	wait
173	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] All splits done — merging by graph → $NT_DIR"
174	
175	declare -A seen
176	while IFS= read -r f; do
177	  s="${f##*-}"; s="${s%.nt}"
178	  seen["$s"]=1
179	done < <(find "$OUT_BASE" -name "*-*.nt" ! -path "$NT_DIR/*")
180	for slug in "${!seen[@]}"; do
181	  find "$OUT_BASE" -name "*-${slug}.nt" ! -path "$NT_DIR/*" | sort | xargs cat > "$NT_DIR/${slug}.nt"
182	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] → $NT_DIR/${slug}.nt"
183	done
184	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Split+merge complete"
185	
186	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Done. Output: $OUT_BASE"
187	
```

```
164:        xslt_nt = f"<{xslt_uri}>"

166:        lines.append(make_nq(xslt_nt, f"<{DCTERMS_HAS_VER}>",
168:        lines.append(make_nq(xslt_nt, f"<{PROV_ON_BEHALF}>", f"<{DDB_BASE}>", graph_iri))
173:            emitted[DDB_BASE] = "prov_ddb"
175:        lines.append(make_nq(ddb_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{PROV_AGENT}>", graph_iri))
181:    if prov_uri and (emitted is None or prov_uri not in emitted):
183:            emitted[prov_uri] = "prov_provider"
184:        prov_nt = f"<{prov_uri}>"
185:        lines.append(make_nq(prov_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{PROV_AGENT}>", graph_iri))
186:        lines.append(make_nq(prov_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{FOAF_ORG}>",   graph_iri))
187:        if provider_name:
188:            lines.append(make_nq(prov_nt, f"<{FOAF_NAME}>",
189:                                 f'"{_escape_literal(provider_name)}"', graph_iri))
190:        if provider_uri:
191:            lines.append(make_nq(prov_nt, f"<{SCHEMA_URL}>", f"<{provider_uri}>", graph_iri))
192:        if provider_id:
193:            lines.append(make_nq(prov_nt, f"<{DCTERMS_ID}>",
194:                                 f'"{_escape_literal(provider_id)}"', graph_iri))
195:        if provider_isil:
196:            lines.append(make_nq(prov_nt, f"<{MOCHO_ISIL}>",
197:                                 f"<{_sanitize_iri(provider_isil)}>", graph_iri))
203:        lines.append(make_nq(src_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{PROV_ENTITY}>", graph_iri))
```

```
21	    _CLASS_WEMI, _CONTRIBUTOR_COL, _W_SLOT_CLASSES, SUBJECT_KEYS,
22	)
23	from .utils import (
24	    make_nq, coerce_list, mint_bare_id, _escape_literal, _sanitize_iri,
25	    value_to_nt_obj, normalize_date, is_ddb_or_gnd, resolve_agent, _to_curie,
26	    build_bare_id_index, expand_obj_nt, resource_uris,
27	)
28	
29	# Entity types whose descriptive triples are deduplicated across records/runs via the
30	# emitted dict (uri → entity_type).  ProvidedCHO, Aggregation, WebResource are excluded
31	# — they are unique per record.
32	_DEDUP_ENTITY_TYPES: dict[str, str] = {
33	    "Agent":    "edm_agent",
34	    "Place":    "edm_place",
35	    "Concept":  "skos_concept",
36	    "TimeSpan": "edm_timespan",
37	}
38	
39	
40	def emit_ddbedm_triples(
41	    rdf: dict,
42	    graph_iri: str,
43	    lang_coll: set[str] | None = None,
44	    emitted: dict[str, str] | None = None,
45	) -> tuple[NQList, Counter, Counter, Counter]:
46	    """Emit verbatim EDM passthrough triples for all entity types in rdf (§6.1).
47	
48	    Subject: first URI in entity['about']; owl:sameAs emitted for any additional URIs.
49	    Includes mt007 records.
50	    Returns (lines, class_ctr, pred_ctr, sani_ctr) where sani_ctr tracks
51	    uri_sanitized, uri_split, and uri_about_split counts.
52	    """
53	    lines:     NQList  = []
54	    class_ctr: Counter = Counter()
55	    pred_ctr:  Counter = Counter()
56	    sani_ctr:  Counter = Counter()
57	    _skip = frozenset({"about"})
58	    bare_id_to_uri = build_bare_id_index(rdf)
59	    for entity_type, entities in rdf.items():
60	        edm_class = _EDM_ENTITY_TYPES.get(entity_type)
61	        for entity in coerce_list(entities):
62	            if not isinstance(entity, dict):
63	                continue
64	            raw_about = (entity.get("about") or "").strip()
65	            if not raw_about:
66	                continue
67	            about_parts = raw_about.split()
68	            subj_uri = mint_bare_id(entity_type, _sanitize_iri(about_parts[0]))
69	            subj_nt  = f"<{subj_uri}>"
70	            if emitted is not None and entity_type in _DEDUP_ENTITY_TYPES:
71	                if subj_uri in emitted:
72	                    continue
73	                emitted[subj_uri] = _DEDUP_ENTITY_TYPES[entity_type]
74	            if len(about_parts) > 1:
75	                sani_ctr["uri_about_split"] += len(about_parts) - 1
76	                for alt in about_parts[1:]:
77	                    lines.append(make_nq(subj_nt, f"<{OWL_SAMEAS}>",
78	                                         f"<{_sanitize_iri(alt)}>", graph_iri))
79	            if edm_class:
80	                lines.append(make_nq(subj_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{edm_class}>", graph_iri))
81	                class_ctr[_to_curie(edm_class)] += 1
82	            for key, val in entity.items():
83	                if key in _skip:
84	                    continue
85	                pred_iri = _DDBEDM_PROP.get(key)
86	                if not pred_iri:
87	                    continue
88	                pred_nt = f"<{pred_iri}>"
89	                curie   = _to_curie(pred_iri)
90	                for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(val, sani_ctr, lang_coll):
91	                    lines.append(make_nq(subj_nt, pred_nt,
92	                                         expand_obj_nt(obj_nt, bare_id_to_uri), graph_iri))
93	                    pred_ctr[curie] += 1
94	    return lines, class_ctr, pred_ctr, sani_ctr
95	
96	
97	def emit_prov_triples(record: dict, ddb_cho_uri: str, graph_iri: str, emitted: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> NQList:
98	    """Emit PROV-O Layer 1 (Without-Activity) for one record (§6.2, ddbedm-prov-o-plan.md §2)."""
99	    lines: NQList = []
100	    props = record.get("properties") or {}
```

```
1	"""Utility functions: N-Quads formatting, URI minting, value normalisation."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import re
6	from collections import Counter
7	from functools import lru_cache
8	from pathlib import Path
9	
10	import langcodes
11	
12	from .constants import (
13	    AgentDict, NQuad, NQList,
14	    GEMEA_BASE, DDB_ITEM_BASE,
15	    _PREFIXES, _MEDIATYPE_PREFIX, _SECTOR_PREFIX,
16	)
17	
18	# Characters forbidden inside N-Triples IRI references (RFC 3987 + NT spec)
19	_IRI_UNSAFE_RE = re.compile(r'[\x00-\x20<>"{}|\\^`\x7f]')
20	_BR_RE         = re.compile(r'<br\s*/?\s*>', re.IGNORECASE)
21	
22	
23	def _sanitize_iri(iri: str) -> str:
24	    """Percent-encode characters illegal inside NT IRI references."""
25	    return _IRI_UNSAFE_RE.sub(lambda m: f"%{ord(m.group()):02X}", iri)
26	
27	
28	def make_nq(s_nt: str, p_nt: str, o_nt: str, graph_iri: str) -> NQuad:
29	    """Return one N-Quads line."""
30	    return f"{s_nt} {p_nt} {o_nt} <{graph_iri}> ."
31	
32	
33	def coerce_list(val: object) -> list:
34	    """Normalise None/dict/list to a list."""
35	    if val is None:
36	        return []
37	    if isinstance(val, dict):
38	        return [val]
39	    if isinstance(val, list):
40	        return val
41	    return []
42	
43	
44	def _expand_prefix(curie: str) -> str:
45	    """Expand a CURIE (e.g. 'rdam:P30134') to a full IRI."""
46	    for prefix, base in _PREFIXES.items():
47	        if curie.startswith(prefix + ":"):
48	            return base + curie[len(prefix) + 1:]
49	    return curie
50	
51	
52	def _to_curie(iri: str) -> str:
53	    """Collapse a full IRI to a CURIE using _PREFIXES; return IRI unchanged if no match."""
54	    for prefix, base in _PREFIXES.items():
55	        if iri.startswith(base):
56	            return f"{prefix}:{iri[len(base):]}"
57	    return iri
58	
59	
60	def mint_cho_uri(obj_id: str) -> str:
61	    """Return the minted GeMeA mocho CHO URI for a 32-char DDB object ID (D22)."""
62	    return GEMEA_BASE + obj_id
63	
64	
65	def mint_bare_id(entity_class: str, raw_id: str) -> str:
66	    """Expand a bare 32-char ID to a full URI (D27).
67	
68	    ProvidedCHO bare IDs → DDB item URI.
69	    All other entity types → urn:ddbedm:<id>.
70	    Full URIs and URNs are returned unchanged.
71	    """
72	    if raw_id.startswith(("http", "urn")):
73	        return raw_id
74	    if entity_class == "ProvidedCHO":
75	        return DDB_ITEM_BASE + raw_id
76	    return f"urn:ddbedm:{raw_id}"
77	
78	
79	def _escape_literal(s: str) -> str:
80	    """Escape characters illegal in N-Triples/N-Quads literal content."""
```

### User *(2026-05-15T13:36:38.661Z)*

**Tool result**
```
def coerce_list(val: object) -> list:
    """Normalise None/dict/list to a list."""
    if val is None:
        return []
    if isinstance(val, dict):
        return [val]
    if isinstance(val, list):
        return val
    return []


def _expand_prefix(curie: str) -> str:
    """Expand a CURIE (e.g. 'rdam:P30134') to a full IRI."""
    for prefix, base in _PREFIXES.items():
        if curie.startswith(prefix + ":"):
            return base + curie[len(prefix) + 1:]
    return curie


def _to_curie(iri: str) -> str:
    """Collapse a full IRI to a CURIE using _PREFIXES; return IRI unchanged if no match."""
    for prefix, base in _PREFIXES.items():
        if iri.startswith(base):
            return f"{prefix}:{iri[len(base):]}"
    return iri


def mint_cho_uri(obj_id: str) -> str:
    """Return the minted GeMeA mocho CHO URI for a 32-char DDB object ID (D22)."""
    return GEMEA_BASE + obj_id

--
def normalize_date(s: str) -> list[str]:
    """Normalise a dc:date string to ISO 8601.

    8-digit compact YYYYMMDD → YYYY-MM-DD.
    ISO interval begin/end → [begin, end].
    All other values returned unchanged.
    """
    s = s.strip()
    if "/" in s:
        parts = s.split("/", 1)
        return [normalize_date(p)[0] for p in parts]
    if len(s) == 8 and s.isdigit():
        return [f"{s[:4]}-{s[4:6]}-{s[6:]}"]
    return [s]


def build_bare_id_index(rdf: dict) -> dict[str, str]:
    """Map bare about IDs → expanded URIs for every entity in the record (D27)."""
    index: dict[str, str] = {}
    for entity_type, entities in rdf.items():
        for entity in coerce_list(entities):
            if not isinstance(entity, dict):
                continue
            raw_about = (entity.get("about") or "").strip()
            for part in raw_about.split():
                if part and not part.startswith(("http", "urn")):
                    index[part] = mint_bare_id(entity_type, _sanitize_iri(part))
    return index
```

transform.prescan\n                --db <sector>.sqlite\n                --prov-db prov.duckdb\n                --concept-labels-db concept_labels.duckdb\n                --agent-labels-db agent_labels.duckdb\n                --lido lido_event_types.csv\n                --prov-out prov-shared.nq\n                --parquet-out <stem>_meta.parquet\nInputs:     <sector>.sqlite          —
