```
1	"""
2	Purpose:    Pass 1 of the two-pass GeMeA transform.
3	            Scans one sector SQLite file to:
4	              1. Emit unique PROV-O shared-node descriptive triples → prov-shared.nq
5	              2. Write PROV-O node URIs              → prov.duckdb (prov_entities table)
6	              3. Collect authority URIs from dcType / dc_subject → concept_labels.duckdb
7	              4. Collect agent URIs from creator/contributor/publisher → agent_labels.duckdb
8	              5. Write a per-sector Parquet metadata file
9	            All shared-DB writes are protected by fcntl.flock so sectors can run in
10	            parallel without concurrent writers.
11	Usage:      python -m transform.prescan
12	                --db <sector>.sqlite
13	                --prov-db prov.duckdb
14	                --concept-labels-db concept_labels.duckdb
15	                --agent-labels-db agent_labels.duckdb
16	                --lido lido_event_types.csv
17	                --prov-out prov-shared.nq
18	                --parquet-out <stem>_meta.parquet
19	Inputs:     <sector>.sqlite          — table objs, column bufgz (gzip-compressed cortex JSON)
20	            prov.duckdb              — shared; created if absent
21	            concept_labels.duckdb    — shared; created if absent
22	            agent_labels.duckdb      — shared; created if absent
23	            lido_event_types.csv     — resource → label mapping
24	Outputs:    prov-shared.nq           — N-Quads for PROV-O shared nodes (appended, deduped)
25	            <stem>_meta.parquet      — per-sector ProvidedCHO metadata
26	            prov.duckdb              — updated prov_entities table
27	            concept_labels.duckdb    — updated concept_labels table
28	            agent_labels.duckdb      — updated agent_labels table
29	Deps:       stdlib + duckdb + pyarrow
30	Assumes:    SQLite has objs(uid VARCHAR, bufgz BLOB); JSON follows DDB EDM cortex format.
31	"""
32	
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	import argparse
36	import csv
37	import fcntl
38	import gzip
39	import json
40	import logging
41	import re
42	import sqlite3
43	from pathlib import Path
44	
45	import pyarrow as pa
46	import pyarrow.parquet as pq
47	
48	from .constants import GRAPH_PROV, DDB_ITEM_BASE
49	from .emitters import emit_prov_triples
50	from .utils import coerce_list, normalize_date
51	
52	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
53	# Parquet schema
54	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
55	
56	AGENT_STRUCT = pa.struct([
57	    pa.field("name",       pa.string()),
58	    pa.field("type",       pa.string()),
59	    pa.field("is_ext_uri", pa.bool_()),
60	])
61	
62	DATE_STRUCT = pa.struct([
63	    pa.field("value", pa.string()),
64	    pa.field("begin", pa.string()),
65	    pa.field("end",   pa.string()),
66	    pa.field("type",  pa.string()),
67	])
68	
69	SUBJECT_STRUCT = pa.struct([
70	    pa.field("name",       pa.string()),
71	    pa.field("is_ext_uri", pa.bool_()),
72	])
73	
74	PARQUET_SCHEMA = pa.schema([
75	    ("obj_id",         pa.string()),
76	    ("title",          pa.string()),
77	    ("lang_title",     pa.string()),
78	    ("lang_obj",       pa.string()),
79	    ("provider_id",    pa.string()),
80	    ("dataset_id",     pa.string()),
81	    ("dc_type",        pa.list_(pa.string())),
82	    ("agents",         pa.list_(AGENT_STRUCT)),
83	    ("dates",          pa.list_(DATE_STRUCT)),
84	    ("dc_subject",     pa.list_(SUBJECT_STRUCT)),
85	    ("hierarchy_type", pa.int16()),
86	    ("mediatype",      pa.int16()),
87	    ("sector",         pa.int16()),
88	    ("is_part_of",     pa.bool_()),
89	])
90	
91	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
92	# Constants
93	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
94	
95	_DDB_VOCNET = "http://ddb.vocnet.org/"
96	_BARE_ID_RE = re.compile(r'^[A-Z0-9]{32}$')
97	_QUALIFIER_RE = re.compile(r'\([^)]*\)')
98	
99	LIDO_CREATED: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
100	    "http://terminology.lido-schema.org/lido00012",
101	    "http://terminology.lido-schema.org/eventType/creation",
102	    "http://terminology.lido-schema.org/lido00007",
103	})
104	LIDO_ISSUED: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
105	    "http://terminology.lido-schema.org/lido00228",
106	    "http://terminology.lido-schema.org/eventType/publication",
107	})
108	
109	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
110	# Helpers
111	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
112	
113	def _is_ext_uri(resource: str) -> bool:
114	    """True if resource is an HTTP URI that is not a bare 32-char DDB ID."""
115	    return bool(resource) and resource.startswith("http") and not _BARE_ID_RE.match(resource)
116	
117	
118	def _scalar_str(val) -> str | None:
119	    """Return the first non-empty string value from a cortex field."""
120	    if val is None:
121	        return None
122	    if isinstance(val, str):
123	        return val or None
124	    if isinstance(val, dict):
125	        return val.get("$") or None
126	    if isinstance(val, list):
127	        for item in val:
128	            result = _scalar_str(item)
129	            if result:
130	                return result
131	    return None
132	
133	
134	def _scalar_values(val) -> list[str]:
135	    """Extract all string values from a cortex field (scalar / dict / list)."""
136	    if val is None:
137	        return []
138	    if isinstance(val, str):
139	        return [val] if val else []
140	    if isinstance(val, dict):
141	        v = val.get("$") or ""
142	        return [v] if v else []
143	    if isinstance(val, list):
144	        out: list[str] = []
145	        for item in val:
146	            out.extend(_scalar_values(item))
147	        return out
148	    return []
149	
150	
151	def _parse_dc_date(raw: str) -> str | None:
152	    """Strip parenthesized qualifiers and brackets, return first normalized date value."""
153	    if not raw:
154	        return None
155	    clean = _QUALIFIER_RE.sub("", raw).strip().strip("[]").strip()
156	    if not clean:
157	        return None
158	    vals = normalize_date(clean)
159	    return vals[0] if vals else None
160	
161	
162	def _build_concept_labels(rdf: dict) -> dict[str, str]:
163	    """Build URI → prefLabel map from all Concept blocks in the record."""
164	    result: dict[str, str] = {}
165	    for c in coerce_list(rdf.get("Concept")):
166	        if not isinstance(c, dict):
167	            continue
168	        about = (c.get("about") or "").strip()
169	        if not about:
170	            continue
171	        for item in coerce_list(c.get("prefLabel")):
172	            if isinstance(item, dict):
173	                label = item.get("$") or ""
174	            elif isinstance(item, str):
175	                label = item
176	            else:
177	                continue
178	            if label:
179	                result[about] = label
180	                break
181	    return result
182	
183	
184	def _build_agent_name_map(rdf: dict) -> dict[str, str]:
185	    """Build URI → name map from Agent and Organization entities in the record."""
186	    result: dict[str, str] = {}
187	    for entity_type in ("Agent", "Organization"):
188	        for ent in coerce_list(rdf.get(entity_type)):
189	            if not isinstance(ent, dict):
190	                continue
191	            about = (ent.get("about") or "").strip()
192	            if not about:
193	                continue
194	            for key in ("prefLabel", "name", "altLabel"):
195	                for item in coerce_list(ent.get(key)):
196	                    if isinstance(item, dict):
197	                        label = item.get("$") or ""
198	                    elif isinstance(item, str):
199	                        label = item
200	                    else:
201	                        continue
202	                    if label:
203	                        result[about] = label
204	                        break
205	                if about in result:
206	                    break
207	    return result
208	
209	
210	def _agent_field(
211	    field_val,
212	    agent_type: str,
213	    agent_labels_pending: dict[str, str | None],
214	) -> list[dict]:
215	    """Convert creator/contributor/publisher field to {name, type, is_ext_uri} structs."""
216	    out: list[dict] = []
217	    for v in coerce_list(field_val):
218	        if isinstance(v, dict):
219	            name = v.get("$") or ""
220	            res  = v.get("resource") or ""
221	            ext  = _is_ext_uri(res)
222	            if ext:
223	                agent_labels_pending.setdefault(res, name or None)
224	            if name or res:
225	                out.append({"name": name, "type": agent_type, "is_ext_uri": ext})
226	        elif isinstance(v, str) and v:
227	            out.append({"name": v, "type": agent_type, "is_ext_uri": False})
228	    return out
229	
230	
231	def _lido_agents(
232	    cho: dict,
233	    rdf: dict,
234	    lido_labels: dict[str, str],
235	    agent_name_map: dict[str, str],
236	) -> list[dict]:
237	    """Extract LIDO participants from hasMet → Event.P11_had_participant."""
238	    out: list[dict] = []
239	    event_map = {
240	        e["about"]: e for e in coerce_list(rdf.get("Event"))
241	        if isinstance(e, dict) and e.get("about")
242	    }
243	    for hm in coerce_list(cho.get("hasMet")):
244	        if not isinstance(hm, dict):
245	            continue
246	        ev_ref = hm.get("resource")
247	        if not ev_ref:
248	            continue
249	        ev = event_map.get(ev_ref, {})
250	        ht = ev.get("hasType") or {}
251	        ht_uri = ht.get("resource") if isinstance(ht, dict) else None
252	        event_label = lido_labels.get(ht_uri or "", "unknown_event")
253	        for p in coerce_list(ev.get("P11_had_participant")):
254	            if not isinstance(p, dict):
255	                continue
256	            res  = p.get("resource") or ""
257	            name = p.get("$") or agent_name_map.get(res, "")
258	            ext  = _is_ext_uri(res)
259	            out.append({"name": name, "type": event_label, "is_ext_uri": ext})
260	    return out
261	
262	
263	def _timespan_to_date(ts: dict, date_type: str) -> dict | None:
264	    """Convert a TimeSpan dict to a {value, begin, end, type} struct."""
265	    ts_begin = (_scalar_values(ts.get("begin")) or [None])[0]
266	    ts_end   = (_scalar_values(ts.get("end"))   or [None])[0]
267	    if ts_begin is None and ts_end is None:
268	        pref = (_scalar_values(ts.get("prefLabel")) or [None])[0]
269	        return {"value": pref, "begin": None, "end": None, "type": date_type} if pref else None
270	    if ts_begin and ts_end and ts_begin == ts_end:
271	        return {"value": ts_begin, "begin": None, "end": None, "type": date_type}
272	    return {"value": None, "begin": ts_begin, "end": ts_end, "type": date_type}
273	
274	
275	def _lido_dates(
276	    events: list,
277	    ts_map: dict[str, dict],
278	    lido_uris: frozenset[str],
279	    date_type: str,
280	) -> list[dict]:
281	    """Extract date structs from LIDO events matching lido_uris via TimeSpan lookup."""
282	    out: list[dict] = []
283	    for ev in events:
284	        if not isinstance(ev, dict):
285	            continue
286	        ht = ev.get("hasType") or {}
287	        ht_uri = ht.get("resource") if isinstance(ht, dict) else None
288	        if ht_uri not in lido_uris:
289	            continue
290	        oc = ev.get("occuredAt") or ev.get("occurredAt")
291	        if not oc:
292	            continue
293	        ts_ref = oc.get("resource") if isinstance(oc, dict) else oc
294	        ts = ts_map.get(ts_ref, {}) if isinstance(ts_ref, str) else {}
295	        d = _timespan_to_date(ts, date_type)
296	        if d:
297	            out.append(d)
298	    return out
299	
300	
301	def _resolve_subject_items(
302	    field_val,
303	    concept_labels: dict[str, str],
304	    concept_labels_pending: dict[str, str | None],
305	) -> list[dict]:
306	    """Convert subject field values to {name, is_ext_uri} structs."""
307	    out: list[dict] = []
308	    for v in coerce_list(field_val):
309	        if isinstance(v, dict):
310	            lit = v.get("$") or ""
311	            res = v.get("resource") or ""
312	            if lit:
313	                out.append({"name": lit, "is_ext_uri": False})
314	            elif res and _is_ext_uri(res) and not res.startswith(_DDB_VOCNET):
315	                label = concept_labels.get(res)
316	                if label:
317	                    out.append({"name": label, "is_ext_uri": True})
318	                else:
319	                    concept_labels_pending.setdefault(res, None)
320	        elif isinstance(v, str) and v and not _BARE_ID_RE.match(v):
321	            out.append({"name": v, "is_ext_uri": False})
322	    return out
323	
324	
325	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
326	# Record extraction
327	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
328	
329	def extract_record(
330	    data: dict,
331	    lido_labels: dict[str, str],
332	) -> tuple[dict, dict[str, str | None], dict[str, str | None]]:
333	    """Extract a Parquet row + pending label DB inserts from one record.
334	
335	    Returns:
336	        (parquet_row, concept_labels_pending, agent_labels_pending)
337	    """
338	    props     = data.get("properties") or {}
339	    prov_info = data.get("provider-info") or {}
340	    rdf       = (data.get("edm") or {}).get("RDF") or {}
341	    cho       = rdf.get("ProvidedCHO") or {}
342	    if isinstance(cho, list):
343	        cho = cho[0] if cho else {}
344	
345	    events    = coerce_list(rdf.get("Event"))
346	    timespans = coerce_list(rdf.get("TimeSpan"))
347	    ts_map    = {
348	        t["about"]: t for t in timespans
349	        if isinstance(t, dict) and t.get("about")
350	    }
351	
352	    concept_labels        = _build_concept_labels(rdf)
353	    agent_name_map        = _build_agent_name_map(rdf)
354	    concept_labels_pending: dict[str, str | None] = {}
355	    agent_labels_pending:   dict[str, str | None] = {}
356	
357	    # -- title + lang_title --
358	    title_raw  = cho.get("title")
359	    title_str  = ""
360	    lang_title = ""
361	    if isinstance(title_raw, list):
362	        title_raw = title_raw[0] if title_raw else None
363	    if isinstance(title_raw, dict):
364	        title_str  = title_raw.get("$") or ""
365	        lang_title = title_raw.get("lang") or ""
366	    elif isinstance(title_raw, str):
367	        title_str = title_raw
368	
369	    # -- lang_obj --
370	    lang_obj = _scalar_str(cho.get("language") or cho.get("dcTermsLanguage")) or ""
371	
372	    # -- dc_type labels --
373	    dc_type_labels: list[str] = []
374	    for v in coerce_list(cho.get("dcType")):
375	        if isinstance(v, dict):
376	            lit = v.get("$") or ""
377	            res = v.get("resource") or ""
378	            if lit:
379	                dc_type_labels.append(lit)
380	            elif res and not res.startswith(_DDB_VOCNET) and _is_ext_uri(res):
381	                label = concept_labels.get(res)
382	                if label:
383	                    dc_type_labels.append(label)
384	                else:
385	                    concept_labels_pending.setdefault(res, None)
386	        elif isinstance(v, str) and v:
387	            dc_type_labels.append(v)
388	
389	    # -- dc_subject structs (three field variants) --
390	    dc_subj: list[dict] = []
391	    for key in ("dcSubject", "dcTermsSubject", "dcTermSubject"):
392	        dc_subj.extend(
393	            _resolve_subject_items(cho.get(key), concept_labels, concept_labels_pending)
394	        )
395	
396	    # -- agents unified --
397	    agents: list[dict] = (
398	        _agent_field(cho.get("creator"),     "creation",    agent_labels_pending)
399	        + _agent_field(cho.get("publisher"), "publication", agent_labels_pending)
400	        + _agent_field(cho.get("contributor"), "contribution", agent_labels_pending)
401	        + _lido_agents(cho, rdf, lido_labels, agent_name_map)
402	    )
403	
404	    # -- dates unified --
405	    dates: list[dict] = []
406	    dc_date_raw = _scalar_str(cho.get("date"))
407	    if dc_date_raw:
408	        normed = _parse_dc_date(dc_date_raw)
409	        if normed:
410	            dates.append({"value": normed, "begin": None, "end": None, "type": "unknown_event"})
411	    for val in _scalar_values(cho.get("created")):
412	        dates.append({"value": val, "begin": None, "end": None, "type": "creation"})
413	    for val in _scalar_values(cho.get("issued")):
414	        dates.append({"value": val, "begin": None, "end": None, "type": "publication"})
415	    dates.extend(_lido_dates(events, ts_map, LIDO_CREATED, "creation"))
416	    dates.extend(_lido_dates(events, ts_map, LIDO_ISSUED,  "publication"))
417	
418	    # -- mediatype / sector (int16) --
419	    mediatype: int | None = None
420	    sector:    int | None = None
421	    for c in coerce_list(rdf.get("Concept")):
422	        if not isinstance(c, dict):
423	            continue
424	        about = c.get("about") or ""
425	        if mediatype is None and "/medientyp/mt" in about:
426	            try:
427	                mediatype = int(about.rsplit("/mt", 1)[-1])
428	            except ValueError:
429	                pass
430	        if sector is None and "/sparte/sparte" in about:
431	            try:
432	                sector = int(about.rsplit("/sparte", 1)[-1])
433	            except ValueError:
434	                pass
435	
436	    # -- hierarchy_type (int16) --
437	    raw_ht = (_scalar_values(cho.get("hierarchyType")) or [None])[0]
438	    htype: int | None = None
439	    if raw_ht:
440	        try:
441	            htype = int(raw_ht.replace("htype_", ""))
442	        except ValueError:
443	            pass
444	
445	    row = {
446	        "obj_id":         props.get("item-id", ""),
447	        "title":          title_str,
448	        "lang_title":     lang_title,
449	        "lang_obj":       lang_obj,
450	        "provider_id":    prov_info.get("provider-ddb-id", ""),
451	        "dataset_id":     props.get("dataset-id", ""),
452	        "dc_type":        dc_type_labels,
453	        "agents":         agents,
454	        "dates":          dates,
455	        "dc_subject":     dc_subj,
456	        "hierarchy_type": htype,
457	        "mediatype":      mediatype,
458	        "sector":         sector,
459	        "is_part_of":     bool(cho.get("isPartOf")),
460	    }
461	    return row, concept_labels_pending, agent_labels_pending
462	
463	
464	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
465	# Locked DB writes
466	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
467	
468	def _write_prov_db(
469	    prov_db_path: Path,
470	    prov_out_path: Path,
471	    new_entities: dict[str, str],
472	    new_prov_lines: list[str],
473	) -> None:
474	    """Lock-protected write to prov.duckdb + idempotent append to prov-shared.nq."""
475	    import duckdb
476	    with open(str(prov_db_path) + ".lock", "a") as lf:
477	        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)
478	        try:
479	            conn = duckdb.connect(str(prov_db_path))
480	            conn.execute("""
481	                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS prov_entities (
482	                    uri         VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
483	                    entity_type VARCHAR NOT NULL
484	                )
485	            """)
486	            uris = list(new_entities.keys())
487	            if uris:
488	                ph = ",".join("?" * len(uris))
489	                existing = {r[0] for r in conn.execute(
490	                    f"SELECT uri FROM prov_entities WHERE uri IN ({ph})", uris
491	                ).fetchall()}
492	            else:
493	                existing = set()
494	            truly_new = {u: t for u, t in new_entities.items() if u not in existing}
495	            if truly_new:
496	                conn.executemany(
497	                    "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO prov_entities VALUES (?, ?)",
498	                    list(truly_new.items()),
499	                )
500	            conn.close()
501	            if truly_new:
502	                truly_new_nts = {f"<{u}>" for u in truly_new}
503	                with open(prov_out_path, "a", encoding="utf-8") as pf:
504	                    for line in new_prov_lines:
505	                        subj = line.split(" ", 1)[0]
506	                        if subj in truly_new_nts:
507	                            pf.write(line + "\n")
508	        finally:
509	            fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)
510	
511	
512	def _write_labels_db(
513	    db_path: Path,
514	    table: str,
515	    pending: dict[str, str | None],
516	) -> None:
517	    """Lock-protected INSERT OR IGNORE of (uri, label) pairs into a labels DuckDB."""
518	    if not pending:
519	        return
520	    import duckdb
521	    with open(str(db_path) + ".lock", "a") as lf:
522	        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)
523	        try:
524	            conn = duckdb.connect(str(db_path))
525	            conn.execute(f"""
526	                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS {table} (
527	                    uri   VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
528	                    label VARCHAR
529	                )
530	            """)
531	            conn.executemany(
532	                f"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO {table} VALUES (?, ?)",
533	                list(pending.items()),
534	            )
535	            conn.close()
536	        finally:
537	            fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)
538	
539	
540	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
541	# Main
542	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
543	
544	def main() -> None:
545	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
546	        description="Pass 1 prescan: PROV-O shared nodes + label DBs + Parquet"
547	    )
548	    parser.add_argument("--db",                type=Path, required=True,
549	                        help="Sector SQLite file (objs table, bufgz column)")
550	    parser.add_argument("--prov-db",           type=Path, required=True, dest="prov_db",
551	                        help="Shared prov.duckdb (prov_entities table); created if absent")
552	    parser.add_argument("--concept-labels-db", type=Path, required=True, dest="concept_labels_db",
553	                        help="Shared concept_labels.duckdb; created if absent")
554	    parser.add_argument("--agent-labels-db",   type=Path, required=True, dest="agent_labels_db",
555	                        help="Shared agent_labels.duckdb; created if absent")
556	    parser.add_argument("--lido",              type=Path, required=True,
557	                        help="lido_event_types.csv (resource → label)")
558	    parser.add_argument("--prov-out",          type=Path, required=True, dest="prov_out",
559	                        help="prov-shared.nq output (appended, deduped)")
560	    parser.add_argument("--parquet-out",       type=Path, required=True, dest="parquet_out",
561	                        help="Per-sector Parquet output")
562	    args = parser.parse_args()
563	
564	    logging.basicConfig(
565	        level=logging.INFO,
566	        format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)-8s %(message)s",
567	        datefmt="%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
568	    )
569	    log = logging.getLogger(__name__)
570	
571	    # Load LIDO event type labels
572	    lido_labels: dict[str, str] = {}
573	    with open(args.lido, encoding="utf-8", newline="") as f:
574	        for row in csv.DictReader(f):
575	            lido_labels[row["resource"]] = row["label"]
576	    log.info("Loaded %d LIDO labels from %s", len(lido_labels), args.lido.name)
577	
578	    # Scan accumulators
579	    local_emitted: dict[str, str]            = {}  # PROV-O shared nodes seen this run
580	    new_prov_lines: list[str]               = []  # shared-node NQ descriptor lines
581	    concept_labels_all: dict[str, str | None] = {}
582	    agent_labels_all:   dict[str, str | None] = {}
583	    parquet_rows: list[dict]                = []
584	
585	    db_conn = sqlite3.connect(str(args.db))
586	    cur = db_conn.cursor()
587	    cur.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM objs")
588	    total = cur.fetchone()[0]
589	    log.info("Scanning %s (%d records)", args.db.name, total)
590	
591	    cur.execute("SELECT uid, bufgz FROM objs WHERE bufgz IS NOT NULL ORDER BY rowid")
592	    processed = errors = 0
593	
594	    for uid, blob in cur:
595	        try:
596	            data = json.loads(gzip.decompress(blob))
597	        except Exception as exc:
598	            log.warning("uid=%s decompress/parse error: %s", uid, exc)
599	            errors += 1
600	            continue
601	
602	        props  = data.get("properties") or {}
603	        obj_id = props.get("item-id") or uid
604	        cho_uri = DDB_ITEM_BASE + obj_id
605	        cho_subj_nt = f"<{cho_uri}> "  # per-record CHO subject prefix
606	
607	        # PROV-O: emit shared-node descriptor lines (skip per-CHO lines)
608	        all_prov_lines = emit_prov_triples(data, cho_uri, GRAPH_PROV, local_emitted)
609	        for line in all_prov_lines:
610	            if not line.startswith(cho_subj_nt):
611	                new_prov_lines.append(line)
612	
613	        # Parquet row + pending label dicts
614	        try:
615	            row, cpending, apending = extract_record(data, lido_labels)
616	            parquet_rows.append(row)
617	            for k, v in cpending.items():
618	                concept_labels_all.setdefault(k, v)
619	            for k, v in apending.items():
620	                agent_labels_all.setdefault(k, v)
621	        except Exception as exc:
622	            log.warning("uid=%s extract error: %s", uid, exc)
623	            errors += 1
624	
625	        processed += 1
626	        if processed % 100_000 == 0:
627	            log.info("  %d / %d", processed, total)
628	
629	    db_conn.close()
630	    log.info("Scan complete: %d processed, %d errors", processed, errors)
631	
632	    # After scan: local_emitted contains all PROV-O shared node URIs found
633	    new_entities = dict(local_emitted)
634	
635	    # Lock-protected writes
636	    log.info("Writing prov.duckdb + prov-shared.nq (%d shared nodes)", len(new_entities))
637	    _write_prov_db(args.prov_db, args.prov_out, new_entities, new_prov_lines)
638	
639	    log.info("Writing concept_labels.duckdb (%d URIs)", len(concept_labels_all))
640	    _write_labels_db(args.concept_labels_db, "concept_labels", concept_labels_all)
641	
642	    log.info("Writing agent_labels.duckdb (%d URIs)", len(agent_labels_all))
643	    _write_labels_db(args.agent_labels_db, "agent_labels", agent_labels_all)
644	
645	    log.info("Writing Parquet: %d rows → %s", len(parquet_rows), args.parquet_out)
646	    arrays = {col: [r.get(col) for r in parquet_rows] for col in PARQUET_SCHEMA.names}
647	    table  = pa.table(arrays, schema=PARQUET_SCHEMA)
648	    pq.write_table(table, str(args.parquet_out))
649	
650	    log.info("Done.")
651	
652	
653	if __name__ == "__main__":
654	    main()
655	
```

97	def emit_prov_triples(record: dict, ddb_cho_uri: str, graph_iri: str, emitted: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> NQList:
98	    """Emit PROV-O Layer 1 (Without-Activity) for one record (§6.2, ddbedm-prov-o-plan.md §2)."""
99	    lines: NQList = []
100	    props = record.get("properties") or {}
101	    prov  = record.get("provider-info") or {}
102	
103	    item_id         = (props.get("item-id")         or "").strip()
104	    dataset_id      = (props.get("dataset-id")      or "").strip()
105	    dataset_label   = (props.get("dataset-label")   or "").strip()
106	    revision        = (props.get("revision-id")     or "").strip()
107	    ingest_dt       = (props.get("ingest-date")     or "").strip()
108	    map_ver         = (props.get("mapping-version") or "").strip()
109	    provider_ddb_id = (prov.get("provider-ddb-id")  or "").strip()
110	    provider_name   = (prov.get("provider-name")    or "").strip()
111	    provider_uri    = (prov.get("provider-uri")     or "").strip()
112	    provider_id     = (prov.get("provider-id")      or "").strip()
113	    provider_isil   = (prov.get("provider-isil")    or "").strip()
114	
115	    src_desc  = (record.get("source") or {}).get("description") or {}
116	    src_ref   = (src_desc.get("record") or {}) if isinstance(src_desc, dict) else {}
117	    ref_val   = (src_ref.get("ref",  "") or "").strip() if isinstance(src_ref, dict) else ""
118	    src_href  = (src_ref.get("href", "") or "").strip() if isinstance(src_ref, dict) else ""
119	    rec_type  = (src_ref.get("type", "") or "").strip() if isinstance(src_ref, dict) else ""
120	
121	    ds_uri   = f"urn:ddbedm:dataset:{dataset_id}"       if dataset_id      else ""
122	    xslt_uri = f"urn:ddbedm:xslt:{map_ver}"             if map_ver         else ""
123	    prov_uri = f"urn:ddbedm:provider:{provider_ddb_id}" if provider_ddb_id else ""
124	
125	    # ── CHO node ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
126	    cho_nt = f"<{ddb_cho_uri}>"
127	    lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{PROV_ENTITY}>", graph_iri))
128	    if ds_uri:

props: dict = {\"item-id\": item_id}\n    if xslt:\n        props[\"mapping-version\"] = xslt\n    if dataset_id:\n        props[\"dataset-id\"] = dataset_id\n    prov: dict = {}\n    if provider_ddb_id:\n        prov[\"provider-ddb-id\"] = provider_ddb_id\n    return {\n        \"properties\":    props,\n        \"provider-info\": prov,\n        \"edm\": {\"RDF\": {\"ProvidedCHO\": cho or {}}},\n    }\n\n\ndef _scan_prov(records):\n    \"\"\"Simulate prescan's PROV-O emission: collect shared-node lines only.\"\"\"\n    local_emitted: dict[str, str] = {}\n    new_prov_lines: emit_prov_triples(rec, cho_uri, GRAPH_PROV, local_emitted):\n            if not line.startswith(cho_sfx):\n                new_prov_lines.append(line)\n    return local_emitted, new_prov_lines\n\n\n# ── TestPrescanProv ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nclass TestPrescanProv:\n    \"\"\"3 records sharing one XSLT version and two providers.\"\"\"\n\n    _RECORDS = [\n        _rec(\"OBJ001\", xslt=\"v1.0\", provider_ddb_id=\"prov001\"),\n        _rec(\"OBJ002\", xslt=\"v1.0\", provider_ddb_id=\"prov001\"),\n        _rec(\"OBJ003\", xslt=\"v1.0\", provider_ddb_id=\"prov002\"),\n    ]\n\n    def test_prov_entities_count_and_types(self, tmp_path):\n        duckdb = pytest.importorskip(\"duckdb\")\n        emitted, lines = = duckdb.connect(str(prov_db))\n        rows = conn.execute(\"SELECT entity_type FROM prov_entities\").fetchall()\n        conn.close()\n\n        c = Counter(r[0] for r in rows)\n        assert len(rows) == 4\n        assert c[\"prov_xslt\"]     == 1\n        assert c[\"prov_provider\"] == 2\n        assert c[\"prov_ddb\"]      == 1\n\n    def test_prov_nq_line_count(self, tmp_path):\n        # xslt: 3 lines, ddb_agent: 3 lines, prov001: 2 lines, prov002: 2 lines = 10\n        pytest.importorskip(\"duckdb\")\n        emitted, lines = _scan_prov(self._RECORDS)\n        prov_db = tmp_path /   "old_string": "    # -- dc_type labels --\n    dc_type_labels: list[str] = []\n    for v in coerce_list(cho.get(\"dcType\")):\n        if isinstance(v, dict):\n            lit = v.get(\"$\") or \"\"\n            res = v.get(\"resource\") or \"\"\n            if lit:\n                dc_type_labels.append(lit)\n            elif res and not res.startswith(_DDB_VOCNET) and _is_ext_uri(res):\n                label = concept_labels.get(res)\n                if label:\n                    dc_type_labels.append(label)\n                else:\n                    concept_labels_pending.setdefault(res, None)\n        elif isinstance(v, str) and v:\n            dc_type_labels.append(v)",
  "new_string": "    # -- dc_type structs --\n    dc_type: list[dict] = []\n    for v in coerce_list(cho.get(\"dcType\")):\n        if isinstance(v, dict):\n            lit = v.get(\"$\") or \"\"\n            res = v.get(\"resource\") or \"\"\n            if lit:\n                dc_type.append({\"name\": lit, \"is_ext_uri\": False})\n            elif res and not res.startswith(_DDB_VOCNET) and _is_ext_uri(res):\n                label = concept_labels.get(res)\n                if label:\n                    dc_type.append({\"name\": label, \"is_ext_uri\": True})\n                else:\n                    concept_labels_pending.setdefault(res, None)\n        elif isinstance(v, str) and v:\n            dc_type.append({\"name\": v, \"is_ext_uri\": False})"
}

  "new_string": "    # -- dc_type structs --\n    dc_type: list[dict] = []\n    dc_type_raw = cho.get(\"dcType\") or []\n    if not isinstance(dc_type_raw, list):\n        dc_type_raw = [dc_type_raw]\n    for v in dc_type_raw:\n        if isinstance(v, dict):\n            lit = v.get(\"$\") or \"\"\n            res = v.get(\"resource\") or \"\"\n            if lit:\n                dc_type.append({\"name\": lit, \"is_ext_uri\": False})\n            elif res and not res.startswith(_DDB_VOCNET) and _is_ext_uri(res):\n                label = concept_labels.get(res)\n                if label:\n                    dc_type.append({\"name\": label, \"is_ext_uri\": True})\n        elif isinstance(v, str) and v:\n            dc_type.append({\"name\": v, \"is_ext_uri\": False})"
}

### User *(2026-05-19T07:54:31.931Z)*

**Tool result**
```
1	# scripts/transform/
2	
3	Reference transform for the mocho ingest pipeline, using the goethe-faust corpus
4	(115,432 DDB records). Converts DDB-EDM JSONL to mocho-aligned N-Quads and a
5	DuckDB werk_staging table for GND Werk linking.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Package structure
10	
11	| Module | Contents |
12	|---|---|
13	| `constants.py` | IRIs, prefix table, dispatch tables, path defaults, type aliases |
14	| `utils.py` | N-Quads formatting, URI minting, value normalisation |
15	| `loaders.py` | CSV/JSON config loaders |
16	| `emitters.py` | Triple emitters: ddbedm passthrough, mocho alignment, PROV-O, werk_staging |
17	| `transform.py` | `transform_record` — per-record orchestration |
18	| `__main__.py` | CLI entry point (`python -m transform`) |
19	| `merge.py` | `python -m transform merge` — concat .nq shards, merge DuckDB + stats |
20	| `sqlite_export.py` | `export()` — sequential SQLite → JSONL export for bulk runs |
21	
22	---
23	
24	## Pipeline
25	
26	### Development / goethe-faust corpus
27	
28	```
29	data/items-all-goethe-faust.json      JSONL input
30	output/config/*.csv / *.json          dispatch + alignment tables
31	          │
32	          ▼
33	  python -m transform   (from scripts/)
34	          │
35	          └── output/transform/YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS/
36	                  <stem>.nq                   N-Quads (all named graphs)
37	                  <stem>-werk-staging.duckdb  DuckDB werk_staging table
38	                  <stem>-stats.json           run statistics
39	                  <stem>-errors.jsonl         per-record errors
40	                  <stem>.log                  run log
41	```
42	
43	`<stem>` is the input filename without extension (e.g. `items-all-goethe-faust.json` → `items-all-goethe-faust`), or the value of `--stub` if provided. Each invocation creates a new timestamped run directory. Use `--outdir` to override.
44	
45	### Full GeMeA corpus (Option C parallel)
46	
47	One `sN.sqlite` per sector. Each sector runs in its own subshell — export then
48	transform back-to-back — with all sectors in parallel:
49	
50	```
51	s1.sqlite ─┐                          s1/ (.nq + .duckdb)  ─┐
52	s2.sqlite ─┤  sqlite_export + transform  s2/ (.nq + .duckdb)  ─┤  cat → merged.nq
53	  ...      ─┤  (pipelined per sector,    ...                  ─┤  duckdb → merged.duckdb
54	sN.sqlite ─┘   all in parallel)       sN/ (.nq + .duckdb)  ─┘
55	```
56	
57	```bash
58	GOETHE=goethe-faust
59	CFG=$GOETHE/output/config
60	GEMEA=gemea/data/sqlite
61	EXPORT=/tmp/gemea-export
62	
63	for n in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7; do
64	  (
65	    PYTHONPATH=$GOETHE/scripts python -m transform.sqlite_export \
66	      --db  $GEMEA/s${n}.sqlite \
67	      --out $EXPORT/s${n}.jsonl \
68	    && \
69	    PYTHONPATH=$GOETHE/scripts python -m transform \
70	      --jsonl  $EXPORT/s${n}.jsonl \
71	      --outdir $GOETHE/output/transform/gemea/s${n} \
72	      --stats dispatch \
73	      --alignment $CFG/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv \
74	      --lido      $CFG/lido_event_types.csv \
75	      --htype     $CFG/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv \
76	      --mediatype $CFG/lookup_mediatype_class.csv \
77	      --audio     $CFG/audio_type2class.json
78	  ) &
79	done
80	wait
81	
82	# merge
83	cat $GOETHE/output/transform/gemea/s*/*.nq > $GOETHE/output/transform/gemea/merged.nq
84	```
85	
86	See [`notes/transform-dryrun-plan.md`](../../notes/transform-dryrun-plan.md) for the full plan including DuckDB merge.
87	
88	## Named graphs
89	
90	| Graph IRI | Content |
91	|---|---|
92	| `https://gemea.ise.fiz-karlsruhe.de/graph/ddbedm` | Verbatim EDM passthrough |
93	| `https://gemea.ise.fiz-karlsruhe.de/graph/mocho` | mocho-aligned triples (class dispatch, property alignment, agents, places) |
94	| `https://gemea.ise.fiz-karlsruhe.de/graph/prov` | PROV-O Layer 1 provenance chain |
95	
96	`werk_staging` rows are written to DuckDB, not to the N-Quads file.
97	
98	## Inputs
99	
100	| File | Description |
101	|---|---|
102	| `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json` | DDB-EDM JSONL; one JSON object per line |
103	| `data/ids-all-goethe-faust.txt` | 32-char DDB object IDs; passed via `--ids` to filter JSONL |
104	| `output/config/lookup_mediatype_class.csv` | `(sector, mediatype)` → class dispatch |
105	| `output/config/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | `htype_code` → rdf:type IRIs (§1.1) |
106	| `output/config/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv` | `(target_class, edm_prop)` → mocho property |
107	| `output/config/lido_event_types.csv` | LIDO event URI → agent predicates per WEMI level |
108	| `output/config/audio_type2class.json` | `dc:type` → audio group (A/B/C) for mt001 dispatch |
109	
110	## CLI
111	
112	Run from the `scripts/` directory. Full argument list: `python -m transform --help`.
113	
114	| Group | Arguments |
115	|---|---|
116	| I/O | `--jsonl FILE`, `--ids FILE`, `--outdir DIR`, `--stem STR` |
117	| Config | `--alignment`, `--lido`, `--htype`, `--mediatype`, `--audio` |
118	| Stats | `--stats none\|basic\|dispatch\|full` (default: `basic`) |
119	| Logging | `--log-level DEBUG\|INFO\|WARNING\|ERROR`, `--debug` |
120	| Development | `--limit N` |
121	
122	**Stats recommendation**: `--stats dispatch` for full-corpus runs; `--stats full --limit 50000`
123	for vocabulary coverage data. Schema and performance trade-offs: [`notes/transform-stats-plan.md`](../../notes/transform-stats-plan.md).
124	
125	## Merging shards
126	
127	After a parallel run, merge all per-worker outputs into combined files:
128	
129	```bash
130	python -m transform merge /data/ddb/gemea/mocho-transform/20260508_1200xxxx
131	```
132	
133	Finds all `*.nq`, `*-werk-staging.duckdb`, and `*-stats.json` under the run directory
134	recursively, merges them, and deletes the shards only after all merges succeed.
135	
136	| Output | Default path |
137	|---|---|
138	| N-Quads | `<out_base>/combined.nq` |
139	| DuckDB werk_staging | `<out_base>/werk-staging-merged.duckdb` |
140	| Stats | `<out_base>/combined-stats.json` |
141	
142	Override with `--nq`, `--db`, `--stats`. DuckDB merge is skipped if `duckdb` is not
143	installed (shard files retained).
144	
145	---
146	
147	```bash
148	# full corpus, default stats
149	python -m transform
150	
151	# full corpus, dispatch stats (recommended for production)
152	python -m transform --stats dispatch
153	
154	# first 500 records, debug logging
155	python -m transform --limit 500 --debug
156	
157	# vocabulary coverage sample
158	python -m transform --limit 50000 --stats full
159	
160	# explicit output directory
161	python -m transform --outdir ../output/transform/dev
162	
163	# custom ID filter
164	python -m transform --ids ../data/ids-sample.txt
165	```
166	
167	## Tests
168	
169	```bash
170	# from project root
171	.venv/bin/python -m pytest scripts/transform/tests/ -q
172	```
173	
174	42 unit tests covering loaders, class dispatch (§1.1), N-Quad formatting, PROV node
175	construction, creator/contributor dispatch, and the D9 fallback. Uses actual config
176	CSVs — no mocking.
177	
178	---
179	
180	**Key references**:
181	- `notes/transform-adr.md` — transform decisions D1–D12
182	- `notes/transform-script-adr.md` — implementation decisions D13–D27
183	- `notes/transform-revised-plan.md` — §1.1 class dispatch table
184	- `notes/transform-stats-plan.md` — stats schema and resource paper guidance
185	
```

### User *(2026-05-19T07:54:43.900Z)*

**Tool result**
```
70	|---|---|---|
71	| `lang` | `pa.string()` | **dropped** — split into `lang_title` and `lang_obj` |
72	| `provider_id` | _(absent)_ | `pa.string()` — `provider-info.provider-ddb-id` |
73	| `dataset_id` | _(absent)_ | `pa.string()` — `properties.dataset-id` |
74	| `lang_title` | _(absent)_ | `pa.string()` — language tag of `ProvidedCHO.title` (e.g. `ger`, `eng`, `zxx`) |
75	| `lang_obj` | _(absent)_ | `pa.string()` — content language from `dc:language` / `dcterms:language` (e.g. `ger`, `eng`) |
76	| `dc_type` | `pa.string()` | `pa.list_(pa.string())` — labels only (see below) |
77	| `dc_date` | `pa.string()` | **dropped** — merged into `dates` |
78	| `dc_date_qualifier` | `pa.string()` | **dropped** — merged into `dates` |
79	| `dc_issued` | `pa.list_(pa.string())` | **dropped** — merged into `dates` |
80	| `dc_created` | `pa.list_(pa.string())` | **dropped** — merged into `dates` |
81	| `dates` | _(absent)_ | `pa.list_(struct(value: string, begin: string, end: string, type: string))` — new unified field |
82	| `dc_creator` | `pa.list_(struct(name: string, type: string))` | **dropped** — merged into `agents` |
83	| `dc_contributor` | `pa.list_(struct(name: string, type: string))` | **dropped** — merged into `agents` |
84	| `dc_publisher` | `pa.list_(struct(name: string, type: string))` | **dropped** — merged into `agents` |
85	| `agents` | `pa.list_(pa.string())` | `pa.list_(struct(name: string, type: string, is_ext_uri: bool))` — unified |
86	| `dc_subject` | `pa.list_(pa.string())` | `pa.list_(struct(name: string, is_ext_uri: bool))` — labels only (see below) |
87	| `dc_subject_uris` | `pa.list_(pa.string())` | **dropped** |
88	| `mediatype` | _(absent)_ | `pa.int16()` — new field |
89	| `sector` | _(absent)_ | `pa.int16()` — new field |
90	| `hierarchy_type` | `pa.string()` | `pa.int16()` |
91	
92	**`dc_type`**: corpus analysis shows values are a mix of plain literals (`"Fotoalbum"`,
93	`"Dokument"`) and authority URIs (Getty AAT, GND, Wikidata). The Parquet column stores
94	**literals only** — URIs are resolved to their `skos:prefLabel` via the same record's
95	`edm.RDF.Concept[]` block before storing.
96	
97	Extraction logic:
98	
99	1. Build a `concept_labels` dict from all `Concept[].about → prefLabel.$` in the record.
100	2. For each `dcType` entry:
101	   - Has `$` (plain literal) → use it directly.
102	   - Has `resource` (URI) and it resolves in `concept_labels` → use the label.
103	   - Has `resource` (URI) but **not** in `concept_labels` and is a **non-DDB URI**
104	     (i.e. does not start with `http://ddb.vocnet.org/`) → store `(uri, None)` in
105	     `concept_labels.duckdb` for later enrichment; omit from Parquet list for now.
106	3. DDB vocnet URIs (`http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/…`, `http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/…`)
107	   that appear in `dcType` are silently skipped — those are sector/mediatype codes
108	   captured in the `mediatype` column instead.
109	
110	Corpus sample results (1,000 records): 561 literal values, 231 URI values. Of the URIs,
111	Getty AAT and GND resolve via same-record Concept blocks; Wikidata URIs often do not
112	(no matching Concept entry in the record).
113	
114	```python
115	# build label index from Concept blocks
116	concepts = rdf.get("Concept") or []
117	if isinstance(concepts, dict): concepts = [concepts]
118	concept_labels: dict[str, str] = {}
119	for c in concepts:
120	    if not isinstance(c, dict): continue
121	    about = c.get("about") or ""
122	    pref = c.get("prefLabel")
123	    if isinstance(pref, list): pref = pref[0] if pref else None
124	    if isinstance(pref, dict): pref = pref.get("$", "")
125	    if about and pref:
126	        concept_labels[about] = pref
127	
128	# resolve dc_type values
129	dc_type_labels: list[str] = []
130	dc_type_raw = cho.get("dcType") or []
131	if not isinstance(dc_type_raw, list): dc_type_raw = [dc_type_raw]
132	for v in dc_type_raw:
133	    if isinstance(v, dict):
134	        res = v.get("resource")
135	        lit = v.get("$")
136	        if lit:
137	            dc_type_labels.append(lit)
138	        elif res and res not in (None, "None", ""):
139	            if res.startswith("http://ddb.vocnet.org/"):
140	                pass  # sector/mediatype code — skip
141	            elif res in concept_labels:
142	                dc_type_labels.append(concept_labels[res])
143	            else:
144	                concept_labels_pending[res] = None  # unresolvable → concept_labels.duckdb with NULL label
145	    elif isinstance(v, str) and v:
146	        dc_type_labels.append(v)
147	# same loop logic applies to dc_subject (dcSubject + dcTermsSubject + dcTermSubject),
148	# skipping bare 32-char DDB IDs (no http prefix) in addition to ddb.vocnet.org URIs
149	```
150	
151	**`mediatype`**: not in `properties.*`. It comes from `edm.RDF.Concept[].about` —
152	scan concept `about` values for the pattern `http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt*`
153	and extract the `mt*` code (last path segment). Take the first unique match; there
154	can be duplicates within a record (same URI repeated). Sample: `mt002`, `mt003`,
155	`mt001`, `mt005`, `mt007`.
156	
157	Extraction snippet:
158	```python
159	mediatype = ""
160	sector = ""
161	concepts = rdf.get("Concept") or []
162	if isinstance(concepts, dict):
163	    concepts = [concepts]
164	for c in concepts:
165	    if not isinstance(c, dict):
166	        continue
167	    about = c.get("about") or ""
168	    if not mediatype and "/medientyp/mt" in about:
169	        mediatype = int(about.rsplit("/mt", 1)[-1])      # "mt002" → 2
170	    if not sector and "/sparte/sparte" in about:
171	        sector = int(about.rsplit("/sparte", 1)[-1])     # "sparte005" → 5
172	
173	# hierarchy_type: strip "htype_" prefix, cast to int
174	raw_ht = (_scalar_values(cho.get("hierarchyType")) or [None])[0]
175	hierarchy_type = int(raw_ht.replace("htype_", "")) if raw_ht else None  # "htype_015" → 15
176	```
177	
178	**`concept_labels.duckdb`** — new DuckDB file written by prescan. Collects all
179	`(uri, label)` pairs seen in `dcType` and `dc_subject` source fields across the corpus,
180	for later lookup and enrichment.
181	
182	```sql
183	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS concept_labels (
184	    uri     VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
185	    label   VARCHAR          -- skos:prefLabel resolved from same-record Concept block;
186	                             -- NULL if not resolvable within the record
187	)
188	```
189	
190	**What goes in**: all non-DDB-internal URIs from `dcType`, `dcSubject`, `dcTermsSubject`,
191	and `dcTermSubject` (the last two are typo variants of the same field, both present in
192	the corpus). Excluded: DDB vocnet URIs (`http://ddb.vocnet.org/…`) and bare 32-character
193	DDB-internal IDs (e.g. `YUVZR2OFQCZ5HKUOCBWXTII5WN6DCLOK`).
194	
195	**`dcTermSubject` note**: a misspelling of `dcterms:subject` present in 590/1,000
196	sampled records (alongside `dcTermsSubject`). Both fields contain the same structure —
197	bare DDB IDs and GND URIs — and are scanned together. Bare IDs are excluded from
198	`concept_labels.duckdb` (entity resolution problem, not a label lookup problem).
199	
200	**Populated via** `INSERT OR IGNORE (uri, label)` for every qualifying URI encountered,
201	where `label` is the `skos:prefLabel` resolved from the same record's `Concept[]`
202	block (or `NULL` if no matching Concept exists — e.g. some Wikidata URIs lack a
203	Concept entry). `INSERT OR IGNORE` means the first non-null label seen wins; a later
204	enrichment pass can fill in nulls via authority endpoint queries (Getty AAT SPARQL,
205	Wikidata API, lobid-gnd).
206	
207	**Parquet impact**: `dc_type` and `dc_subject` lists in Parquet contain only resolved
208	labels. Values with NULL labels are omitted from Parquet but remain in
209	`concept_labels.duckdb` for enrichment. `dc_subject_uris` column is **dropped**
210	(superseded by `concept_labels.duckdb`).
211	
212	**`agent_labels.duckdb`** — new DuckDB file, parallel to `concept_labels.duckdb`,
213	for `dc_creator`, `dc_contributor`, `dc_publisher` agent URIs.
214	
215	```sql
216	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agent_labels (
217	    uri     VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
218	    label   VARCHAR          -- from $-field of the same dict; rarely NULL for agents
219	)
220	```
221	
222	**What goes in**: HTTP URIs from creator/contributor/publisher dicts (primarily GND:
223	`http://d-nb.info/gnd/…`). Bare 32-char DDB IDs are not observed in agent fields
224	(corpus sample: 0 bare IDs across 200 records); if encountered they are excluded.
225	
226	**Key difference from `concept_labels`**: no Concept block lookup needed — the label
227	is already inline in the same `{'resource': uri, '$': label}` dict. Extract both in
228	one pass:
229	
```

### Assistant *(2026-05-19T07:56:45.298Z)*

**Tool call:** `Edit`
```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/notes/transform-prescan-plan.md",
  "old_string": "**`dc_type`**: corpus analysis shows values are a mix of plain literals (`\"Fotoalbum\"`,\n`\"Dokument\"`) and authority URIs (Getty AAT, GND, Wikidata). The Parquet column stores\n**literals only** — URIs are resolved to their `skos:prefLabel` via the same record's\n`edm.RDF.Concept[]` block before storing.\n\nExtraction logic:\n\n1. Build a `concept_labels` dict from all `Concept[].about → prefLabel.$` in the record.\n2. For each `dcType` entry:\n   - Has `$` (plain literal) → use it directly.\n   - Has `resource` (URI) and it resolves in `concept_labels` → use the label.\n   - Has `resource` (URI) but **not** in `concept_labels` and is a **non-DDB URI**\n     (i.e. does not start with `http://ddb.vocnet.org/`) → store `(uri, None)` in\n     `concept_labels.duckdb` for later enrichment; omit from Parquet list for now.\n3. DDB vocnet URIs (`http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/…`, `http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/…`)\n   that appear in `dcType` are silently skipped — those are sector/mediatype codes\n   captured in the `mediatype` column instead.\n\nCorpus sample results (1,000 records): 561 literal values, 231 URI values. Of the URIs,\nGetty AAT and GND resolve via same-record Concept blocks; Wikidata URIs often do not\n(no matching Concept entry in the record).\n\n```python\n# build label index from Concept blocks\nconcepts = rdf.get(\"Concept\") or []\nif isinstance(concepts, dict): concepts = [concepts]\nconcept_labels: dict[str, str] = {}\nfor c in concepts:\n    if not isinstance(c, dict): continue\n    about = c.get(\"about\") or \"\"\n    pref = c.get(\"prefLabel\")\n    if isinstance(pref, list): pref = pref[0] if pref else None\n    if isinstance(pref, dict): pref = pref.get(\"$\", \"\")\n    if about and pref:\n        concept_labels[about] = pref\n\n# resolve dc_type values\ndc_type_labels: list[str] = []\ndc_type_raw = cho.get(\"dcType\") or []\nif not isinstance(dc_type_raw, list): dc_type_raw = [dc_type_raw]\nfor v in dc_type_raw:\n    if isinstance(v, dict):\n        res = v.get(\"resource\")\n        lit = v.get(\"$\")\n        if lit:\n            dc_type_labels.append(lit)\n        elif res and res not in (None, \"None\", \"\"):\n            if res.startswith(\"http://ddb.vocnet.org/\"):\n                pass  # sector/mediatype code — skip\n            elif res in concept_labels:\n                dc_type_labels.append(concept_labels[res])\n            else:\n                concept_labels_pending[res] = None  # unresolvable → concept_labels.duckdb with NULL label\n    elif isinstance(v, str) and v:\n        dc_type_labels.append(v)\n# same loop logic applies to dc_subject (dcSubject + dcTermsSubject + dcTermSubject),\n# skipping bare 32-char DDB IDs (no http prefix) in addition to ddb.vocnet.org URIs\n```",
  "new_string": "**`dc_type`** and **`dc_subject`** use the shared `CONCEPT_STRUCT = pa.struct([(\"name\", pa.string()), (\"is_ext_uri\", pa.bool_())])`.\nValues are a mix of plain literals (`\"Fotoalbum\"`, `\"Dokument\"`) and authority URIs (Getty AAT, GND, Wikidata).\nEach entry stores the resolved label in `name` and `True` in `is_ext_uri` when the value came from a URI.\n\nExtraction logic (identical for both fields):\n\n1. Build a `concept_labels` dict from all `Concept[].about → prefLabel.$` in the record.\n2. For each entry:\n   - Has `$` (plain literal) → `{name: lit, is_ext_uri: False}`.\n   - Has `resource` (URI) that resolves in `concept_labels` → `{name: label, is_ext_uri: True}`.\n   - Has `resource` (non-DDB URI) **not** in `concept_labels` → store `(uri, None)` in\n     `concept_labels.duckdb` for later enrichment; omit from Parquet list for now.\n3. DDB vocnet URIs (`http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/…`, `http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/…`)\n   are silently skipped — sector/mediatype codes are captured in the `mediatype`/`sector` columns instead.\n4. `dc_subject` additionally skips bare 32-char DDB IDs (no http prefix).\n\nCorpus sample results (1,000 records): 561 literal values, 231 URI values. Of the URIs,\nGetty AAT and GND resolve via same-record Concept blocks; Wikidata URIs often do not\n(no matching Concept entry in the record).\n\n```python\n# resolve dc_type values → list[{name, is_ext_uri}]\ndc_type: list[dict] = []\nfor v in coerce_list(cho.get(\"dcType\")):\n    if isinstance(v, dict):\n        lit, res = v.get(\"$\") or \"\", v.get(\"resource\") or \"\"\n        if lit:\n            dc_type.append({\"name\": lit, \"is_ext_uri\": False})\n        elif res and not res.startswith(\"http://ddb.vocnet.org/\") and _is_ext_uri(res):\n            label = concept_labels.get(res)\n            if label:\n                dc_type.append({\"name\": label, \"is_ext_uri\": True})\n            else:\n                concept_labels_pending.setdefault(res, None)\n    elif isinstance(v, str) and v:\n        dc_type.append({\"name\": v, \"is_ext_uri\": False})\n# dc_subject uses the same pattern + skips bare 32-char DDB IDs\n```"
}
```

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/infra/transform-workflow.md",
  "old_string": "#### Step 0. Pre-Scan",
  "new_string": "## Full corpus transform — `run_gemea_transform.sh`\n\nSingle command that runs the complete two-pass pipeline across all 7 sectors.\nRun from the `goethe-faust/` project root inside a `tmux` or `screen` session on teach03.\n\n### Steps\n\n1. **Activate the virtual environment**\n\n   ```bash\n   source .venv/bin/activate\n   ```\n\n2. **Start a fresh run** (wipes previous outputs for this version):\n\n   ```bash\n   bash scripts/run_gemea_transform.sh --new\n   ```\n\n   Or **resume** after interruption (skips sectors with non-empty `.nq` output):\n\n   ```bash\n   bash scripts/run_gemea_transform.sh --resume\n   ```\n\n   The script runs four phases automatically:\n\n   | Phase | What happens |\n   |---|---|\n   | 0 — Prescan | 7 sectors in parallel → `prov.duckdb`, `concept_labels.duckdb`, `agent_labels.duckdb`, `prov-shared.nq`, per-sector `*_meta.parquet` |\n   | 1+2 — Export + Transform | Each sector: SQLite → JSONL → N-Quads (128 workers total, all sectors in parallel) |\n   | 3 — DuckDB merge | All `*-werk-staging.duckdb` shards → `werk-staging-merged.duckdb` |\n   | 4 — NQ split + merge | `.nq` files split by graph into per-graph `.nt`; `prov-shared.nq` included |\n\n3. **Check outputs** under the auto-versioned directory:\n\n   ```\n   /data/ddb/gemea/mocho-transform/<YYYYMMDD-HH><hash>/\n     s{1..7}/                      per-sector .nq, .duckdb, -stats.json, -errors.jsonl, .log\n     nt/                           per-graph .nt files (ddbedm.nt, mocho.nt, prov.nt)\n     werk-staging-merged.duckdb\n     prov.duckdb\n     concept_labels.duckdb\n     agent_labels.duckdb\n     prov-shared.nq\n     parquet/s{1..7}_meta.parquet  per-sector metadata Parquet\n   ```\n\n### Arguments to change\n\n**Hardcoded paths** — edit directly in the script:\n\n| Variable | Default | Change when |\n|---|---|---|\n| `SQLITE_DIR` | `/data/ddb/data` | SQLite files are elsewhere |\n| `EXPORT_DIR` | `/data/ddb/gemea/json-export` | JSONL export should go elsewhere |\n| `OUT_BASE` | `/data/ddb/gemea/mocho-transform/<version>` | Auto-versioned; edit `OUT_BASE=` line to pin a path |\n| `WORKERS` | `s1:17 s2:86 s3:1 s4:6 s5:8 s6:9 s7:1` (128 total) | Tune to available cores; values are proportional to sector record counts |\n\n**Override env vars** — prepend to the command, no script edit needed:\n\n| Variable | Overrides | Notes |\n|---|---|---|\n| `PROV_DB_ARG` | `prov.duckdb` path | Share across runs to skip re-emitting known PROV-O nodes |\n| `CONCEPT_LABELS_DB_ARG` | `concept_labels.duckdb` path | |\n| `AGENT_LABELS_DB_ARG` | `agent_labels.duckdb` path | |\n| `PARQUET_DIR_ARG` | per-sector Parquet output dir | |\n| `PARQUET_MERGE_ARG` | if set, merges all per-sector Parquet into one file at this path | Leave unset to skip the merge step |\n\nExample with overrides:\n\n```bash\nPROV_DB_ARG=/data/shared/prov.duckdb \\\nPARQUET_MERGE_ARG=/data/gemea/all_meta.parquet \\\nbash scripts/run_gemea_transform.sh --new\n```\n\n---\n\n#### Step 0. Pre-Scan"
}
```

### User *(2026-05-19T08:21:57.252Z)*

In Step 2.0 Pre-Scan - "7 sectors in parallel". is there handling of concurrent duckdb writes

---

### Assistant *(2026-05-19T08:22:08.848Z)*

Yes — each of the three shared DuckDB writes in `prescan.py` is protected by `fcntl.flock`. The pattern in `_write_prov_db` and `_write_labels_db` ([prescan.py:476](goethe-faust/scripts/transform/prescan.py#L476)):

```python
with open(str(db_path) + ".lock", "a") as lf:
    fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)   # blocks until acquired
    try:
        conn = duckdb.connect(str(db_path))
        conn.execute("CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ...")
        conn.executemany("INSERT OR IGNORE ...")
        conn.close()
    finally:
        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)
```

Each shared file (`prov.duckdb`, `concept_labels.duckdb`, `agent_labels.duckdb`) gets its own `.lock` sidecar file. Sectors queue up and write sequentially when they finish scanning — no sector holds the lock during the scan itself, only during the brief write flush at the end. `INSERT OR IGNORE` makes the writes idempotent so a retry or overlapping write is safe.

Two caveats worth knowing: `fcntl` is POSIX-only (fine on teach03, would break on Windows), and it only works across processes on the same host — not across NFS mounts.

### User *(2026-05-19T12:22:22.311Z)*

i want to run the prescan for s2.sqlite. what's the command?

---

### Assistant *(2026-05-19T12:22:38.610Z)*

Run from the `goethe-faust/scripts/` directory so the `transform` package is importable:

```bash
cd /path/to/goethe-faust/scripts

OUTDIR=/data/gemea/www/downloads/gemea/$(date +%Y%m%d)
mkdir -p "$OUTDIR/parquet"

python3 -m transform.prescan \
  --db                /data/ddb/data/s2.sqlite \
  --prov-db           "$OUTDIR/prov.duckdb" \
  --concept-labels-db "$OUTDIR/concept_labels.duckdb" \
  --agent-labels-db   "$OUTDIR/agent_labels.duckdb" \
  --lido              ../output/config/lido_event_types.csv \
  --prov-out          "$OUTDIR/prov-shared.nq" \
  --parquet-out       "$OUTDIR/parquet/s2_meta.parquet"
```

If you want to reuse shared DuckDB files from a previous run (to skip re-emitting already-known PROV-O nodes), point `--prov-db`, `--concept-labels-db`, and `--agent-labels-db` at the existing files instead — the writes are idempotent (`INSERT OR IGNORE`).

---

### User *(2026-05-19T12:25:01.836Z)*

ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'langcodes'

---

### Assistant *(2026-05-19T12:25:14.785Z)*

`langcodes` is a dependency of the transform package but isn't installed in the Python you're using. Use the project venv instead:

```bash
cd /path/to/goethe-faust/scripts

OUTDIR=/data/gemea/www/downloads/gemea/$(date +%Y%m%d)
mkdir -p "$OUTDIR/parquet"

../.venv/bin/python3 -m transform.prescan \
  --db                /data/ddb/data/s2.sqlite \
  --prov-db           "$OUTDIR/prov.duckdb" \
  --concept-labels-db "$OUTDIR/concept_labels.duckdb" \
  --agent-labels-db   "$OUTDIR/agent_labels.duckdb" \
  --lido              ../output/config/lido_event_types.csv \
  --prov-out          "$OUTDIR/prov-shared.nq" \
  --parquet-out       "$OUTDIR/parquet/s2_meta.parquet"
```

Or activate first and then use bare `python3`:

```bash
source ../.venv/bin/activate
```

If the venv doesn't exist yet, create it from the project root:

```bash
cd /path/to/goethe-faust
python3 -m venv .venv && .venv/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt
```

### Assistant *(2026-05-19T14:05:27.322Z)*

The parent directory doesn't exist yet. Add `mkdir -p` for the output dir before running:

```bash
mkdir -p ~/gemea/output/20260519/parquet
```

The lock file is created by `open(..., "a")` — it only needs the parent directory to exist, not the file itself. Same applies to `prov.duckdb`, `prov-shared.nq`, and the Parquet output.

468	
469	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
470	# Locked DB writes
471	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
472	
473	def _write_prov_db(
474	    prov_db_path: Path,
475	    prov_out_path: Path,
476	    new_entities: dict[str, str],
477	    new_prov_lines: list[str],
478	) -> None:
479	    """Lock-protected write to prov.duckdb + idempotent append to prov-shared.nq."""
480	    import duckdb
481	    with open(str(prov_db_path) + ".lock", "a") as lf:
482	        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)
483	        try:
484	            conn = duckdb.connect(str(prov_db_path))
485	            conn.execute("""
486	                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS prov_entities (
487	                    uri         VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
488	                    entity_type VARCHAR NOT NULL
489	                )
490	            """)
491	            uris = list(new_entities.keys())
492	            if uris:
493	                ph = ",".join("?" * len(uris))
494	                existing = {r[0] for r in conn.execute(
495	                    f"SELECT uri FROM prov_entities WHERE uri IN ({ph})", uris
496	                ).fetchall()}
497	            else:
498	                existing = set()
499	            truly_new = {u: t for u, t in new_entities.items() if u not in existing}
500	            if truly_new:
501	                conn.executemany(
502	                    "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO prov_entities VALUES (?, ?)",
503	                    list(truly_new.items()),
504	                )
505	            conn.close()
506	            if truly_new:
507	                truly_new_nts = {f"<{u}>" for u in truly_new}
508	                with open(prov_out_path, "a", encoding="utf-8") as pf:
509	                    for line in new_prov_lines:
510	                        subj = line.split(" ", 1)[0]
511	                        if subj in truly_new_nts:
512	                            pf.write(line + "\n")
513	        finally:
514	            fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)
515	
516	
517	def _write_labels_db(
518	    db_path: Path,
519	    table: str,
520	    pending: dict[str, str | None],
521	) -> None:
522	    """Lock-protected INSERT OR IGNORE of (uri, label) pairs into a labels DuckDB."""
523	    if not pending:
524	        return
525	    import duckdb
526	    with open(str(db_path) + ".lock", "a") as lf:
527	        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)
528	        try:
529	            conn = duckdb.connect(str(db_path))
530	            conn.execute(f"""
531	                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS {table} (
532	                    uri   VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
533	                    label VARCHAR
534	                )
535	            """)
536	            conn.executemany(
537	                f"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO {table} VALUES (?, ?)",
538	                list(pending.items()),
539	            )
540	            conn.close()
541	        finally:
542	            fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)
```

### Assistant *(2026-05-20T06:14:51.682Z)*

`touch()` creates the file (or updates its mtime if it exists) immediately on startup — if the path is unwritable for any reason, it raises `PermissionError` before the scan begins.

---

### User *(2026-05-20T08:02:12.407Z)*

what happens to the rest of parquet rows at the end, will it be written also?

---

### Assistant *(2026-05-20T08:02:27.735Z)*

Yes — prescan accumulates all rows in a plain list in memory and writes them all in a single `pq.write_table()` call at the end ([prescan.py:649](goethe-faust/scripts/transform/prescan.py#L649)):

```python
arrays = {col: [r.get(col) for r in parquet_rows] for col in PARQUET_SCHEMA.names}
table  = pa.table(arrays, schema=PARQUET_SCHEMA)
pq.write_table(table, str(args.parquet_out))
```

There's no batching at all — everything is in `parquet_rows` by the time the write happens, so nothing is left behind. The trade-off is memory: all rows for one sector sit in RAM until the scan is done. For s2 (the largest sector, ~86 workers' worth of records) that's the main concern, but since prescan runs sequentially per-sector it's bounded to one sector at a time.

### User *(2026-05-20T08:35:14.840Z)*

what happens to the .duckdb files when I run for the next sector?

---

### Assistant *(2026-05-20T08:35:25.214Z)*

They accumulate. Each sector run appends to the same three files using `INSERT OR IGNORE`, so:

- URIs already in the DB from a previous sector are silently skipped
- New URIs from the current sector are added
- The tables grow across all 7 sector runs

This is the intended behavior — by the end of all 7 sectors, the DuckDB files contain the union of everything seen across the full corpus. The `fcntl.flock` lock ensures no two sectors corrupt each other if they finish at the same time.

---

### User *(2026-05-20T08:36:23.813Z)*

what happens to prov.nq

---

### Assistant *(2026-05-20T08:36:28.428Z)*

Same pattern — `_write_prov_db` opens `prov-shared.nq` in append mode (`"a"`), and only writes lines for URIs that are `truly_new` (not already in `prov.duckdb`). So each sector appends only the shared nodes it saw for the first time. By the end of all 7 sectors the file contains the deduplicated union of all PROV-O shared-node triples.

---

### User *(2026-05-20T08:41:51.938Z)*

why does it take so long to finish at the end

---

### Assistant *(2026-05-20T08:42:14.182Z)*

The three DuckDB writes all happen after the scan finishes. For s2 (18.5M records), `concept_labels_all` and `agent_labels_all` can accumulate millions of unique URIs in memory, and they're all written in one `executemany` call under an exclusive lock at the end.

The fix is the same as for Parquet — flush the label dicts to DuckDB periodically during the scan. Want me to add that?

---

- Update `README.md` and `transform-prescan-plan.md` to reflect all schema changes

User's most recent request: "I have prov duckdb now. I want to rerun transform on all the sectors again, what's the workflow now"

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- Two-pass pipeline: Pass 1 (prescan, sequential per sector) → shared DuckDBs; Pass 2 (transform workers) reads prov.duckdb read-only
- `CONCEPT_STRUCT = pa.struct([("name", pa.string()), ("is_ext_uri", pa.bool_())])` — shared struct for dc_type and dc_subject
- `AGENT_STRUCT`, `DATE_STRUCT`, `CONCEPT_STRUCT` — three Parquet struct types
- `PARQUET_SCHEMA` fields: obj_id, title, lang_title, lang_obj, description (list_(string)), provider_id, dataset_id, dc_type (list_(CONCEPT_STRUCT)), agents (list_(AGENT_STRUCT)), dates (list_(DATE_STRUCT)), dc_subject (list_(CONCEPT_STRUCT)), hierarchy_type (int16), mediatype (int16), sector (int16), is_part_of (bool)
- `fcntl.flock` for concurrent DuckDB writes across parallel sector processes
- `INSERT OR IGNORE` for idempotent DuckDB accumulation across sectors
- `pq.ParquetWriter` with 500k-row periodic flushes (instead of bulk end-of-scan write)
- `args.parquet_out.touch()` — early writability check before scan starts

    parquet_writer: pq.ParquetWriter | None = None
    parquet_total = 0

    def _flush_parquet() -> None:

        if not parquet_buf:

        arrays = {col: [r.get(col) for r in parquet_buf] for col in PARQUET_SCHEMA.names}

        parquet_writer.write_table(table)

- **`ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'langcodes'`**: User running with system python3 instead of venv. Fix: use `../.venv/bin/python3` or activate venv first.

- **`FileNotFoundError: prov.duckdb.lock` — parent directory missing**: Crashed after scan completed (wasted processing time). Fix: added `mkdir -p` for all output parent dirs + `args.parquet_out.touch()` at start of main() before scanning.

- **`FileNotFoundError: parquet file` — parent directory missing after 18.5M rows**: Same root cause, user said "you are wasting my time". Fix was already in the code (mkdir for parquet parent), but user was running old code. Immediate fix: `mkdir -p ~/gemea/output/20260519/parquet`.

- **dc_type list assumption without corpus analysis**: User rejected `list_(string)` assumption, asked "did you do a corpus analysis?". Ran analysis on items-excerpt-1000.json: 41% multi-valued, up to 9 values → confirmed `list_(string)` is correct.

- **SUBJECT_STRUCT naming**: User asked to rename to CONCEPT_STRUCT since it's used for both dc_type and dc_subject. Applied replace_all across prescan.py and export_ddb.py.

- **Stale "redefined below" header comment in export_ddb_parquet.py**: Removed after extract_meta was moved to match export_ddb.py.

5. Problem Solving:

- **Concurrent DuckDB writes**: Solved via `fcntl.flock` with `.lock` sidecar files. Each sector acquires exclusive lock only during the brief end-of-scan write flush, not during scanning. POSIX-only limitation noted.
- **Slow finish at end**: Identified as bulk `executemany` of all accumulated concept/agent label URIs under exclusive lock. Partial fix: periodic Parquet flush implemented. Label dict periodic flush proposed but not yet implemented.
- **Prescan run sequence on teach03**: User ran s2, s5, s1, s3, s4 sequentially (not in parallel), accumulating into shared DuckDB files. Now has prov.duckdb and wants to run transform.

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Purpose:   Transform a single sector SQLite database with N parallel workers, then merge.
3	#            --test mode uses a JSONL file instead of SQLite (splits file into worker chunks).
4	# Usage:     bash scripts/run-transform-sector.sh [OPTIONS]
5	#              --sector SECTOR    SQLite stem, e.g. s2          (default: s2)
6	#              --sqlite-dir DIR   Directory with <sector>.sqlite (default: /data/gemea/sqlite)
7	#              --scripts-dir DIR  Path to goethe-faust/scripts/  (default: /home/ann/goethe-faust/scripts)
8	#              --version VER      Output version tag              (default: YYYYMMDD today)
9	#              --workers N        Parallel worker count           (default: 50; 5 in --test mode)
10	#              --merge            Merge stats + werk_staging after workers finish (default: off)
11	#              --merge-all        Merge stats + werk_staging + .nq after workers finish
12	#              --output-dir DIR   Override output directory (default: derived from mode)
13	#              --test             JSONL mode: input items-all-goethe-faust.json,
14	#                                 output /data/gemea/www/downloads/gemea/<version>/, 5 workers
15	# Inputs:    <sqlite-dir>/<sector>.sqlite  (normal mode)
16	#            <scripts-dir>/../data/items-all-goethe-faust.json  (--test mode)
17	# Outputs:   /data/gemea/www/downloads/gemea/<version>/nq/<sector>.nq       (--merge-all)
18	#            /data/gemea/www/downloads/gemea/<version>/<sector>-stats.json
19	#            /data/gemea/www/downloads/gemea/<version>/<sector>-werk-staging.duckdb
20	#            /data/gemea/www/downloads/gemea/<version>/<sector>-errors.jsonl
21	#            /data/gemea/www/downloads/gemea/<version>/<sector>.log
22	# Deps:      python3; duckdb (pip install duckdb) for .duckdb merge; split (coreutils)
23	# Assumes:   Config files at <scripts-dir>/../output/config/
24	
25	set -euo pipefail
26	
27	# ── Defaults ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
28	SECTOR=s2
29	SQLITE_DIR=/data/gemea/sqlite
30	SCRIPTS_DIR=/home/ann/goethe-faust/scripts
31	VERSION=$(date '+%Y%m%d')
32	WORKERS=50
33	MERGE=true
34	MERGE_ALL=false
35	TEST=false
36	OUTPUT_DIR=""
37	
38	# ── Arg parsing ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
39	while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
40	  case "$1" in
41	    --sector)     SECTOR="$2";      shift 2 ;;
42	    --sqlite-dir) SQLITE_DIR="$2";  shift 2 ;;
43	    --scripts-dir)SCRIPTS_DIR="$2"; shift 2 ;;
44	    --version)    VERSION="$2";     shift 2 ;;
45	    --workers)    WORKERS="$2";     shift 2 ;;
46	    --merge)       MERGE=true;        shift 1 ;;
47	    --merge-all)   MERGE_ALL=true;   shift 1 ;;
48	    --test)        TEST=true;        shift 1 ;;
49	    --output-dir)  OUTPUT_DIR="$2";  shift 2 ;;
50	    *) echo "Unknown argument: $1" >&2; exit 1 ;;
51	  esac
52	done
53	
54	# ── Derived paths ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
55	GOETHE="$(cd "$SCRIPTS_DIR/.." && pwd)"
56	CFG="$GOETHE/output/config"
57	PYTHON=$(cd "$SCRIPTS_DIR" && { [[ -x "../.venv/bin/python3" ]] && echo "../.venv/bin/python3" || echo python3; })
58	
59	if [[ "$TEST" == "true" ]]; then
60	  [[ "$WORKERS" -eq 50 ]] && WORKERS=5   # apply test default only if not explicitly overridden
61	  JSONL="$GOETHE/data/items-all-goethe-faust.json"
62	  OUT="${OUTPUT_DIR:-/data/gemea/www/downloads/gemea/$VERSION}"
63	else
64	  DB="$SQLITE_DIR/${SECTOR}.sqlite"
65	  OUT="${OUTPUT_DIR:-/data/gemea/www/downloads/gemea/$VERSION}"
66	fi
67	
68	# ── Validate ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
69	[[ -d "$SCRIPTS_DIR" ]] || { echo "Scripts dir not found: $SCRIPTS_DIR" >&2; exit 1; }
70	[[ -d "$CFG" ]]         || { echo "Config dir not found: $CFG" >&2; exit 1; }
71	if [[ "$TEST" == "true" ]]; then
72	  [[ -f "$JSONL" ]] || { echo "Test JSONL not found: $JSONL" >&2; exit 1; }
73	else
74	  [[ -f "$DB" ]]    || { echo "SQLite not found: $DB" >&2; exit 1; }
75	fi
76	
77	mkdir -p "$OUT" "$OUT/nq"
78	
79	# ── Launch workers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
80	if [[ "$TEST" == "true" ]]; then
81	  TOTAL=$(wc -l < "$JSONL")
82	  CHUNK_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
83	  trap 'rm -rf "$CHUNK_DIR"' EXIT
84	  LINES_PER_CHUNK=$(( (TOTAL + WORKERS - 1) / WORKERS ))
85	  split -l "$LINES_PER_CHUNK" "$JSONL" "$CHUNK_DIR/chunk_"
86	
87	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] TEST mode  total=$TOTAL  workers=$WORKERS"
88	  echo "  JSONL   = $JSONL"
89	  echo "  OUT     = $OUT"
90	
91	  for chunk in "$CHUNK_DIR"/chunk_*; do
92	    CHUNK_TOTAL=$(wc -l < "$chunk")
93	    (
94	      cd "$SCRIPTS_DIR"
95	      $PYTHON -m transform \
96	        --jsonl     "$chunk" \
97	        --outdir    "$OUT/nq" \
98	        --stats     dispatch \
99	        --total     "$CHUNK_TOTAL" \
100	        --alignment "$CFG/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv" \
101	        --lido      "$CFG/lido_event_types.csv" \
102	        --htype     "$CFG/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv" \
103	        --mediatype "$CFG/lookup_mediatype_class.csv" \
104	        --audio     "$CFG/audio_type2class.json"
105	    ) &
106	  done
107	else
108	  TOTAL=$(sqlite3 "$DB" "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM objs")
109	  CHUNK=$(( (TOTAL + WORKERS - 1) / WORKERS ))
110	
111	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] sector=$SECTOR  total=$TOTAL  workers=$WORKERS  chunk=$CHUNK"
112	  echo "  DB      = $DB"
113	  echo "  OUT     = $OUT"
114	  echo "  VERSION = $VERSION"
115	
116	  for i in $(seq 0 $((WORKERS - 1))); do
117	    OFFSET=$(( i * CHUNK ))
118	    (
119	      cd "$SCRIPTS_DIR"
120	      $PYTHON -m transform \
121	        --db        "$DB" \
122	        --offset    "$OFFSET" \
123	        --limit     "$CHUNK" \
124	        --outdir    "$OUT/nq" \
125	        --stats     dispatch \
126	        --total     "$CHUNK" \
127	        --alignment "$CFG/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv" \
128	        --lido      "$CFG/lido_event_types.csv" \
129	        --htype     "$CFG/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv" \
130	        --mediatype "$CFG/lookup_mediatype_class.csv" \
131	        --audio     "$CFG/audio_type2class.json"
132	    ) &
133	  done
134	fi
135	
136	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] All $WORKERS workers launched — waiting..."
137	wait
138	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Workers done"
139	
140	# ── Merge ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
141	if [[ "$MERGE_ALL" == "true" ]]; then
142	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Merging shards (stats + werk_staging + .nq)"
143	  cd "$SCRIPTS_DIR"
144	  $PYTHON -m transform merge "$OUT/nq" --outdir "$OUT" --stem "$SECTOR"
145	elif [[ "$MERGE" == "true" ]]; then
146	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Merging shards (stats + werk_staging only)"
147	  cd "$SCRIPTS_DIR"
148	  $PYTHON -m transform merge "$OUT/nq" --outdir "$OUT" --stem "$SECTOR" --skip-nq
149	else
150	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Skipping merge (pass --merge or --merge-all)"
151	fi
152	
153	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Done. Output: $OUT"
154	
```
