```
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Purpose:   Full GeMeA corpus transform — 128 workers across 7 sectors in parallel (Option C, teach03)
3	# Usage:     bash scripts/run_gemea_transform.sh --new      # wipe previous output and restart
4	#            bash scripts/run_gemea_transform.sh --resume   # skip sectors already done
5	#            Prepend PROV_DB_ARG=, CONCEPT_LABELS_DB_ARG=, AGENT_LABELS_DB_ARG=,
6	#            PARQUET_DIR_ARG=, PARQUET_MERGE_ARG= to override default paths.
7	#            Run from the goethe-faust project root, inside a tmux/screen session.
8	# Inputs:    /data/ddb/data/s{1..7}.sqlite
9	# Outputs:   $OUT_BASE/s{1..7}/   (per-sector .nq, .duckdb, -stats.json, -errors.jsonl, .log)
10	#            $OUT_BASE/nq/ddbedm.nt, mocho.nt, prov.nt
11	#            $OUT_BASE/werk-staging-merged.duckdb
12	# Deps:      .venv/ created by scripts/setup_venv.sh (optional), duckdb via pip3 --user,
13	#            scripts/split_nq.py (stdlib only)
14	# Notes:     Sectors with >1 worker: export → split JSONL → N parallel transforms.
15	#            Sectors with 1 worker:  export → transform (no split).
16	#            cd into scripts/ before python3 -m transform so duckdb (user site-packages)
17	#            is visible without PYTHONPATH.
18	
19	set -euo pipefail
20	
21	# ── Args ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
22	MODE=""
23	for arg in "$@"; do
24	  case "$arg" in
25	    --new)    MODE=new ;;
26	    --resume) MODE=resume ;;
27	    *) echo "Usage: $0 [--new|--resume]" >&2; exit 1 ;;
28	  esac
29	done
30	if [[ -z "$MODE" ]]; then
31	  echo "Error: specify --new (wipe and restart) or --resume (skip completed sectors)" >&2
32	  exit 1
33	fi
34	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
35	
36	# ── Paths ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
37	GOETHE="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
38	SQLITE_DIR=/data/ddb/data
39	EXPORT_DIR=/data/ddb/gemea/json-export
40	SCRIPTS=$GOETHE/scripts
41	OUT_BASE=/data/gemea/www/downloads/gemea/$(date '+%Y%m%d')
42	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
43	
44	CFG=$GOETHE/output/config
45	PYTHON=$( [[ -x "$GOETHE/.venv/bin/python3" ]] && echo "$GOETHE/.venv/bin/python3" || echo python3 )
46	
47	# Workers per sector — 128 total (50% of 256 cores on teach03)
48	# Distributed proportionally to record count so all sectors finish simultaneously.
49	declare -A WORKERS=([1]=17 [2]=86 [3]=1 [4]=6 [5]=8 [6]=9 [7]=1)
50	
51	mkdir -p "$EXPORT_DIR" "$OUT_BASE"
52	
53	PROV_DB=${PROV_DB_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/prov.duckdb}
54	CONCEPT_LABELS_DB=${CONCEPT_LABELS_DB_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/concept_labels.duckdb}
55	AGENT_LABELS_DB=${AGENT_LABELS_DB_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/agent_labels.duckdb}
56	PARQUET_DIR=${PARQUET_DIR_ARG:-$OUT_BASE/parquet}
57	PROV_SHARED=$OUT_BASE/prov-shared.nq
58	
59	if [[ "$MODE" == "new" ]]; then
60	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] --new: wiping previous output"
61	  rm -rf "$OUT_BASE"/s{1..7} "$OUT_BASE/nq" \
62	         "$OUT_BASE/werk-staging-merged.duckdb" \
63	         "$OUT_BASE/prov.duckdb" \
64	         "$OUT_BASE/prov-shared.nq"
65	fi
66	
67	mkdir -p "$PARQUET_DIR"
68	
69	# ── Phase 0: prescan (parallel, one process per sector) ───────────────────────
70	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Starting prescan phase (7 sectors in parallel)"
71	for n in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7; do
72	  (
73	    echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] prescan starting"
74	    cd "$SCRIPTS"
75	    "$PYTHON" -m transform.prescan \
76	      --db                "$SQLITE_DIR/s${n}.sqlite" \
77	      --prov-db           "$PROV_DB" \
78	      --concept-labels-db "$CONCEPT_LABELS_DB" \
79	      --agent-labels-db   "$AGENT_LABELS_DB" \
80	      --lido              "$CFG/lido_event_types.csv" \
81	      --prov-out          "$PROV_SHARED" \
82	      --parquet-out       "$PARQUET_DIR/s${n}_meta.parquet"
83	    echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] prescan done"
84	  ) &
85	done
86	wait
87	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Prescan phase complete"
88	
89	# optional: merge per-sector Parquet into one combined file
90	if [[ -n "${PARQUET_MERGE_ARG:-}" ]]; then
91	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Merging per-sector Parquet → $PARQUET_MERGE_ARG"
92	  cd "$SCRIPTS"
93	  "$PYTHON" -m transform merge_parquet \
94	    --indir "$PARQUET_DIR" \
95	    --out   "$PARQUET_MERGE_ARG"
96	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Parquet merge done"
97	fi
98	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
99	
100	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Starting GeMeA transform (${MODE}) — 128 workers across 7 sectors"
101	echo "  GOETHE     = $GOETHE"
102	echo "  SQLITE_DIR = $SQLITE_DIR"
103	echo "  EXPORT_DIR = $EXPORT_DIR"
104	echo "  OUT_BASE   = $OUT_BASE"
105	echo "  Workers    = s1:${WORKERS[1]} s2:${WORKERS[2]} s3:${WORKERS[3]} s4:${WORKERS[4]}" \
106	     "s5:${WORKERS[5]} s6:${WORKERS[6]} s7:${WORKERS[7]}"
107	
108	# ── Phase 1+2: export then transform (chunked where W>1) ──────────────────────
109	for n in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7; do
110	  W=${WORKERS[$n]}
111	  (
112	    mkdir -p "$OUT_BASE/s${n}"
113	
114	    # --resume: skip if any .nq already exists and is non-empty
115	    if [[ "$MODE" == "resume" ]]; then
116	      existing=$(find "$OUT_BASE/s${n}" -name "*.nq" -size +0 2>/dev/null | head -1)
117	      if [[ -n "$existing" ]]; then
118	        echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] skipping — output exists"
119	        exit 0
120	      fi
121	    fi
122	
123	    # Export full sector JSONL
124	    echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] export starting"
125	    cd "$SCRIPTS"
126	    "$PYTHON" -m transform.sqlite_export \
127	      --db  "$SQLITE_DIR/s${n}.sqlite" \
128	      --out "$EXPORT_DIR/s${n}.jsonl" \
129	      2>> "$OUT_BASE/s${n}/export.log"
130	    echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] export done"
131	
132	    if [[ $W -eq 1 ]]; then
133	      # Single worker — transform directly
134	      TOTAL=$(wc -l < "$EXPORT_DIR/s${n}.jsonl")
135	      echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] transform starting (1 worker, ${TOTAL} records)"
136	      cd "$SCRIPTS"
137	      "$PYTHON" -m transform \
138	        --jsonl      "$EXPORT_DIR/s${n}.jsonl" \
139	        --outdir     "$OUT_BASE/s${n}" \
140	        --stats      dispatch \
141	        --total      "$TOTAL" \
142	        --alignment  "$CFG/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv" \
143	        --lido       "$CFG/lido_event_types.csv" \
144	        --htype      "$CFG/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv" \
145	        --mediatype  "$CFG/lookup_mediatype_class.csv" \
146	        --audio      "$CFG/audio_type2class.json" \
147	        --prov-db    "$PROV_DB"
148	    else
149	      # Multiple workers — split JSONL into W chunks then transform in parallel
150	      CHUNK_DIR="$EXPORT_DIR/s${n}_chunks"
151	      mkdir -p "$CHUNK_DIR"
152	      echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] splitting into ${W} chunks"
153	      split -n l/$W "$EXPORT_DIR/s${n}.jsonl" "$CHUNK_DIR/chunk_"
154	
155	      echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] launching ${W} transform workers"
156	      for chunk in "$CHUNK_DIR"/chunk_*; do
157	        stem=$(basename "$chunk")
158	        TOTAL=$(wc -l < "$chunk")
159	        mkdir -p "$OUT_BASE/s${n}/$stem"
160	        (
161	          cd "$SCRIPTS"
162	          "$PYTHON" -m transform \
163	            --jsonl      "$chunk" \
164	            --outdir     "$OUT_BASE/s${n}/$stem" \
165	            --stats      dispatch \
166	            --total      "$TOTAL" \
167	            --alignment  "$CFG/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv" \
168	            --lido       "$CFG/lido_event_types.csv" \
169	            --htype      "$CFG/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv" \
170	            --mediatype  "$CFG/lookup_mediatype_class.csv" \
171	            --audio      "$CFG/audio_type2class.json" \
172	            --prov-db    "$PROV_DB"
173	        ) &
174	      done
175	      wait  # wait for all chunk workers of this sector
176	    fi
177	
178	    echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] [s${n}] done"
179	  ) &
180	done
181	
182	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] All sector workers launched — waiting for completion..."
183	wait
184	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] All sectors complete — merging"
185	
186	# ── Phase 3: merge DuckDB werk_staging ───────────────────────────────────────
187	OUT_BASE="$OUT_BASE" "$PYTHON" <<'PYEOF'
188	import glob, os, sys
189	try:
190	    import duckdb
191	except ImportError:
192	    print("duckdb not available — skipping werk_staging merge")
193	    sys.exit(0)
194	out_base = os.environ["OUT_BASE"]
195	shards = sorted(glob.glob(f"{out_base}/**/*-werk-staging.duckdb", recursive=True))
196	if not shards:
197	    print("No werk_staging shards found — skipping DuckDB merge")
198	    sys.exit(0)
199	out = f"{out_base}/werk-staging-merged.duckdb"
200	conn = duckdb.connect(out)
201	conn.execute(f"CREATE TABLE werk_staging AS SELECT * FROM '{shards[0]}'")
202	for p in shards[1:]:
203	    conn.execute(f"INSERT INTO werk_staging SELECT * FROM '{p}'")
204	rows = conn.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM werk_staging").fetchone()[0]
205	conn.close()
206	print(f"werk_staging merged ({len(shards)} shards, {rows} rows) → {out}")
207	PYEOF
208	
209	# ── Phase 4: split each .nq → per-chunk .nt, then merge by graph ─────────────
210	NT_DIR=$OUT_BASE/nq
211	mkdir -p "$NT_DIR"
212	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Splitting N-Quads into per-chunk .nt files"
213	while IFS= read -r nq; do
214	  "$PYTHON" "$SCRIPTS/split_nq.py" "$nq" &
215	done < <(find "$OUT_BASE" -name "*.nq" | sort)
216	# include shared PROV-O node triples from prescan
217	[[ -f "$PROV_SHARED" ]] && "$PYTHON" "$SCRIPTS/split_nq.py" "$PROV_SHARED" &
218	wait
219	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] All splits done — merging by graph → $NT_DIR"
220	
221	declare -A seen
222	while IFS= read -r f; do
223	  s="${f##*-}"; s="${s%.nt}"
224	  seen["$s"]=1
225	done < <(find "$OUT_BASE" -name "*-*.nt" ! -path "$NT_DIR/*")
226	for slug in "${!seen[@]}"; do
227	  find "$OUT_BASE" -name "*-${slug}.nt" ! -path "$NT_DIR/*" | sort | xargs cat > "$NT_DIR/${slug}.nt"
228	  echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] → $NT_DIR/${slug}.nt"
229	done
230	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Split+merge complete"
231	
232	echo "[$(date '+%F %T')] Done. Output: $OUT_BASE"
233	
```

### Assistant *(2026-05-20T13:23:01.339Z)*

The script already passes `--prov-db "$PROV_DB"` to workers and supports `PROV_DB_ARG` to override the default path. But Phase 0 (prescan) runs unconditionally — there's no way to skip it when you already have a completed prov.duckdb. I'll add `--skip-prescan` to the script, then give you the full workflow.

### Assistant *(2026-05-20T13:28:05.109Z)*

Now the workflow. Adjust the `PROV_DIR` path to wherever your 20260519 prescan output actually lives on teach03:

---

**Situation**: prescan done for s1–s5, prov.duckdb exists. s6 and s7 still need prescan.

**Step 1 — finish prescan for s6 and s7**

```bash
PROV_DIR=~/gemea/output/20260519   # adjust to actual path on teach03
CFG=~/goethe-faust/output/config
SCRIPTS=~/goethe-faust/scripts
PYTHON=~/goethe-faust/.venv/bin/python3

for n in 6 7; do
  (
    cd "$SCRIPTS"
    "$PYTHON" -m transform.prescan \
      --db                /data/gemea/sqlite/s${n}.sqlite \
      --prov-db           $PROV_DIR/prov.duckdb \
      --concept-labels-db $PROV_DIR/concept_labels.duckdb \
      --agent-labels-db   $PROV_DIR/agent_labels.duckdb \
      --lido              $CFG/lido_event_types.csv \
      --prov-out          $PROV_DIR/prov-shared.nq \
      --parquet-out       $PROV_DIR/parquet/s${n}_meta.parquet
  ) &
done
wait
```

**Step 2 — run transform (skip prescan, use existing prov.duckdb)**

```bash
PROV_DIR=~/gemea/output/20260519

PROV_DB_ARG=$PROV_DIR/prov.duckdb \
CONCEPT_LABELS_DB_ARG=$PROV_DIR/concept_labels.duckdb \
AGENT_LABELS_DB_ARG=$PROV_DIR/agent_labels.duckdb \
PARQUET_DIR_ARG=$PROV_DIR/parquet \
bash scripts/run_gemea_transform.sh --new --skip-prescan
```

`--new` wipes `$OUT_BASE/s{1..7}` and the default `$OUT_BASE/prov.duckdb` — it will **not** touch `$PROV_DB_ARG` since that points to a different path. The transform workers will read prov.duckdb read-only.

Output will land in `/data/gemea/www/downloads/gemea/20260520/`.

### Assistant *(2026-05-20T13:35:14.469Z)*

They're only used in Phase 0 (prescan). With `--skip-prescan`, those variables are never referenced — the transform workers don't take `--concept-labels-db` or `--agent-labels-db`. You don't need them.

```bash
PROV_DB_ARG=$PROV_DIR/prov.duckdb \
bash scripts/run_gemea_transform.sh --new --skip-prescan
```

That's the minimal command. `PARQUET_DIR_ARG` is also prescan-only, so you can drop that too.

```json
  "new_string": "# Purpose:   Transform a single sector SQLite database with N parallel workers, then merge.\n#            --test mode uses a JSONL file instead of SQLite (splits file into worker chunks).\n# Usage:     bash scripts/run-transform-sector.sh [OPTIONS]\n#              --sector SECTOR    SQLite stem, e.g. s2          (default: s2)\n#              --sqlite-dir DIR   Directory with <sector>.sqlite (default: /data/gemea/sqlite)\n#              --scripts-dir DIR  Path to goethe-faust/scripts/  (default: /home/ann/goethe-faust/scripts)\n#              --version VER      Output version tag              (default: YYYYMMDD today)\n#              --workers N        Parallel worker count           (default: 50; 5 in --test mode)\n#              --prov-db FILE     Path to prov.duckdb from prescan (optional; passed to each worker)\n#              --merge            Merge stats + werk_staging after workers finish (default: off)\n#              --merge-all        Merge stats + werk_staging + .nq after workers finish\n#              --output-dir DIR   Override output directory (default: derived from mode)\n#              --test             JSONL mode: input items-all-goethe-faust.json,\n#                                 output /data/gemea/www/downloads/gemea/<version>/, 5 workers"
```

```json
  "old_string": "  TOTAL=$(sqlite3 \"$DB\" \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM objs\")\n  CHUNK=$(( (TOTAL + WORKERS - 1) / WORKERS ))\n\n  echo \"[$(date '+%F %T')] sector=$SECTOR  total=$TOTAL  workers=$WORKERS  chunk=$CHUNK\"\n  echo \"  DB      = $DB\"\n  echo \"  OUT     = $OUT\"\n  echo \"  VERSION = $VERSION\"\n\n  for i in $(seq 0 $((WORKERS - 1))); do\n    OFFSET=$(( i * CHUNK ))\n    (\n      cd \"$SCRIPTS_DIR\"\n      $PYTHON -m transform \\\n        --db        \"$DB\" \\\n        --offset    \"$OFFSET\" \\\n        --limit     \"$CHUNK\" \\\n        --outdir    \"$OUT/nq\" \\\n        --stats     dispatch \\\n        --total     \"$CHUNK\" \\\n        --alignment \"$CFG/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv\" \\\n        --lido      \"$CFG/lido_event_types.csv\" \\\n        --htype     \"$CFG/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv\" \\\n        --mediatype \"$CFG/lookup_mediatype_class.csv\" \\\n        --audio     \"$CFG/audio_type2class.json\"\n    ) &\n  done",
  "new_string": "  TOTAL=$(sqlite3 \"$DB\" \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM objs\")\n  CHUNK=$(( (TOTAL + WORKERS - 1) / WORKERS ))\n\n  echo \"[$(date '+%F %T')] sector=$SECTOR  total=$TOTAL  workers=$WORKERS  chunk=$CHUNK\"\n  echo \"  DB      = $DB\"\n  echo \"  OUT     = $OUT\"\n  echo \"  VERSION = $VERSION\"\n  [[ -n \"$PROV_DB\" ]] && echo \"  PROV_DB = $PROV_DB\"\n\n  PROV_ARG=(); [[ -n \"$PROV_DB\" ]] && PROV_ARG=(--prov-db \"$PROV_DB\")\n  for i in $(seq 0 $((WORKERS - 1))); do\n    OFFSET=$(( i * CHUNK ))\n    (\n      cd \"$SCRIPTS_DIR\"\n      $PYTHON -m transform \\\n        --db        \"$DB\" \\\n        --offset    \"$OFFSET\" \\\n        --limit     \"$CHUNK\" \\\n        --outdir    \"$OUT/nq\" \\\n        --stats     dispatch \\\n        --total     \"$CHUNK\" \\\n        --alignment \"$CFG/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv\" \\\n        --lido      \"$CFG/lido_event_types.csv\" \\\n        --htype     \"$CFG/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv\" \\\n        --mediatype \"$CFG/lookup_mediatype_class.csv\" \\\n        --audio     \"$CFG/audio_type2class.json\" \\\n        \"${PROV_ARG[@]}\"\n    ) &\n  done"
}
```

### Assistant *(2026-05-20T13:38:26.566Z)*

Now for s7 with 1 worker:

```bash
bash scripts/run-transform-sector.sh \
  --sector s7 \
  --workers 1 \
  --prov-db ~/gemea/output/20260519/prov.duckdb \
  --merge-all
```

`--workers` controls the split. `--merge-all` produces the final `.nq` in `$OUT/nq/s7.nq`. Output lands in `/data/gemea/www/downloads/gemea/20260520/` by default; override with `--output-dir` or `--version`.

### Assistant *(2026-05-20T14:04:41.497Z)*

Let me ask a targeted question before checking anything further.

When a transform worker sees an xslt version or provider entity that's already in prov.duckdb, what should it do?

**a)** Emit the shared entity definition triples to the `.nq` as normal — prov.duckdb only ensures the same URI is reused (no new entity minted), but the triples still appear in each sector's output (duplicates across sectors are acceptable / deduped at load time)

**b)** Suppress the definition from the per-sector `.nq` entirely — the definitions live only in `prov-shared.nq` written during prescan, and the final load concatenates both files

Currently the code does **(b)** — shared node definitions are absent from `s7.nq`. From your description ("these should still be in the .nq"), it sounds like you want **(a)**. Is that right?

```
97	def emit_prov_triples(record: dict, ddb_cho_uri: str, graph_iri: str, emitted: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> NQList:
98	    """Emit PROV-O Layer 1 (Without-Activity) for one record (§6.2, ddbedm-prov-o-plan.md §2)."""
99	    lines: NQList = []
100	    props = record.get("properties") or {}
101	    prov  = record.get("provider-info") or {}
102	
103	    item_id         = (props.get("item-id")         or "").strip()
104	    dataset_id      = (props.get("dataset-id")      or "").strip()
105	    dataset_label   = (props.get("dataset-label")   or "").strip()
106	    revision        = (props.get("revision-id")     or "").strip()
107	    ingest_dt       = (props.get("ingest-date")     or "").strip()
108	    map_ver         = (props.get("mapping-version") or "").strip()
109	    provider_ddb_id = (prov.get("provider-ddb-id")  or "").strip()
110	    provider_name   = (prov.get("provider-name")    or "").strip()
111	    provider_uri    = (prov.get("provider-uri")     or "").strip()
112	    provider_id     = (prov.get("provider-id")      or "").strip()
113	    provider_isil   = (prov.get("provider-isil")    or "").strip()
114	
115	    src_desc  = (record.get("source") or {}).get("description") or {}
116	    src_ref   = (src_desc.get("record") or {}) if isinstance(src_desc, dict) else {}
117	    ref_val   = (src_ref.get("ref",  "") or "").strip() if isinstance(src_ref, dict) else ""
118	    src_href  = (src_ref.get("href", "") or "").strip() if isinstance(src_ref, dict) else ""
119	    rec_type  = (src_ref.get("type", "") or "").strip() if isinstance(src_ref, dict) else ""
120	
121	    ds_uri   = f"urn:ddbedm:dataset:{dataset_id}"       if dataset_id      else ""
122	    xslt_uri = f"urn:ddbedm:xslt:{map_ver}"             if map_ver         else ""
123	    prov_uri = f"urn:ddbedm:provider:{provider_ddb_id}" if provider_ddb_id else ""
124	
125	    # ── CHO node ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
126	    cho_nt = f"<{ddb_cho_uri}>"
127	    lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{PROV_ENTITY}>", graph_iri))
128	    if ds_uri:
129	        lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{PROV_DERIVED}>",   f"<{ds_uri}>",   graph_iri))
130	    if xslt_uri:
131	        lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{PROV_ATTRIBUTED}>", f"<{xslt_uri}>", graph_iri))
132	    if ingest_dt:
133	        lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{PROV_GEN_TIME}>",
134	                             f'"{_escape_literal(ingest_dt)}"^^<{XSD_DATETIME}>', graph_iri))
135	    if revision:
136	        lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{DCTERMS_HAS_VER}>",
137	                             f'"{_escape_literal(revision)}"', graph_iri))
138	    if ref_val:
139	        lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{DCTERMS_REF}>",
140	                             f'"ddb:{_escape_literal(ref_val)}"', graph_iri))
141	
142	    # ── Dataset node ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
143	    if ds_uri and (emitted is None or ds_uri not in emitted):
144	        if emitted is not None:
145	            emitted[ds_uri] = "prov_dataset"
146	        ds_nt = f"<{ds_uri}>"
147	        lines.append(make_nq(ds_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{DCAT_DATASET}>", graph_iri))
148	        lines.append(make_nq(ds_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{PROV_ENTITY}>",  graph_iri))
149	        lines.append(make_nq(ds_nt, f"<{DCTERMS_ID}>",
150	                             f'"{_escape_literal(dataset_id)}"', graph_iri))
151	        if dataset_label:
152	            lines.append(make_nq(ds_nt, f"<{RDFS_LABEL}>",
153	                                 f'"{_escape_literal(dataset_label)}"@de', graph_iri))
154	        if rec_type:
155	            lines.append(make_nq(ds_nt, f"<{DCTERMS_TYPE}>", f"<{rec_type}>", graph_iri))
156	        if prov_uri:
157	            lines.append(make_nq(ds_nt, f"<{PROV_ATTRIBUTED}>",
158	                                 f"<{prov_uri}>", graph_iri))
159	
160	    # ── XSLT SoftwareAgent node ───────────────────────────────────────────────
161	    if xslt_uri and (emitted is None or xslt_uri not in emitted):
162	        if emitted is not None:
163	            emitted[xslt_uri] = "prov_xslt"
164	        xslt_nt = f"<{xslt_uri}>"
165	        lines.append(make_nq(xslt_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{PROV_SW_AGENT}>", graph_iri))
166	        lines.append(make_nq(xslt_nt, f"<{DCTERMS_HAS_VER}>",
167	                             f'"{_escape_literal(map_ver)}"', graph_iri))
168	        lines.append(make_nq(xslt_nt, f"<{PROV_ON_BEHALF}>", f"<{DDB_BASE}>", graph_iri))
169	
170	    # ── DDB Agent node (fixed URI) ────────────────────────────────────────────
171	    if emitted is None or DDB_BASE not in emitted:
172	        if emitted is not None:
173	            emitted[DDB_BASE] = "prov_ddb"
174	        ddb_nt = f"<{DDB_BASE}>"
175	        lines.append(make_nq(ddb_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{PROV_AGENT}>", graph_iri))
176	        lines.append(make_nq(ddb_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{FOAF_ORG}>",   graph_iri))
177	        lines.append(make_nq(ddb_nt, f"<{FOAF_NAME}>",
178	                             '"Deutsche Digitale Bibliothek"', graph_iri))
179	
180	    # ── Provider Agent node ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
181	    if prov_uri and (emitted is None or prov_uri not in emitted):
182	        if emitted is not None:
183	            emitted[prov_uri] = "prov_provider"
184	        prov_nt = f"<{prov_uri}>"
185	        lines.append(make_nq(prov_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{PROV_AGENT}>", graph_iri))
186	        lines.append(make_nq(prov_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{FOAF_ORG}>",   graph_iri))
```

### Assistant *(2026-05-20T14:08:36.967Z)*

The problem is clear now. prov.duckdb stores URIs only — not the triple content (provider names, ISIL codes, dataset labels). So prov-shared.nq can't be reconstructed from the DB alone; it had to be generated during prescan on teach03 and never got synced.

For the s7 run to be self-contained we need prov-shared.nq. Two options:

**a)** Sync `prov-shared.nq` from teach03 (has s1–s6 shared nodes), then run prescan on s7 with the existing prov.duckdb to append any new s7-only entities

**b)** Run prescan on s7 with a fresh empty prov.duckdb — generates a prov-shared.nq containing all shared nodes encountered in s7 (some will overlap with s1–s6, but that's fine; triplestore deduplicates on load)

Since s7.sqlite is available locally, I'll do **(b)** — gives a complete, self-contained s7 output without needing teach03.

### Assistant *(2026-05-20T14:10:32.763Z)*

Zero entity definition triples in s7.nq — shared nodes are correctly suppressed. All 319 lines (61 entities) are in `prov-shared.nq` only.

---

**To answer your earlier question: `prov-shared.nq` is written by the prescan** (`transform.prescan`), not by the transform workers. The transform workers only suppress shared node definitions (using prov.duckdb as a seen-set) and write per-record triples. The prescan is what accumulates the shared entity triples and writes them to prov-shared.nq.

The issue with your teach03 run was that prov-shared.nq wasn't synced along with prov.duckdb. For the full corpus load, you need both files:

```
prov-shared.nq   ← shared entity definitions (from prescan)
s1.nq … s7.nq   ← per-record triples (from transform workers)
```

### User *(2026-05-20T14:11:29.896Z)*

**Tool result**
```
1	"""
2	Purpose:    Pass 1 of the two-pass GeMeA transform.
3	            Scans one sector SQLite file to:
4	              1. Emit unique PROV-O shared-node descriptive triples → prov-shared.nq
5	              2. Write PROV-O node URIs              → prov.duckdb (prov_entities table)
6	              3. Collect authority URIs from dcType / dc_subject → concept_labels.duckdb
7	              4. Collect agent URIs from creator/contributor/publisher → agent_labels.duckdb
8	              5. Write a per-sector Parquet metadata file
9	            All shared-DB writes are protected by fcntl.flock so sectors can run in
10	            parallel without concurrent writers.
11	Usage:      python -m transform.prescan
12	                --db <sector>.sqlite
13	                --prov-db prov.duckdb
14	                --concept-labels-db concept_labels.duckdb
15	                --agent-labels-db agent_labels.duckdb
16	                --lido lido_event_types.csv
17	                --prov-out prov-shared.nq
18	                --parquet-out <stem>_meta.parquet
19	Inputs:     <sector>.sqlite          — table objs, column bufgz (gzip-compressed cortex JSON)
20	            prov.duckdb              — shared; created if absent
21	            concept_labels.duckdb    — shared; created if absent
22	            agent_labels.duckdb      — shared; created if absent
23	            lido_event_types.csv     — resource → label mapping
24	Outputs:    prov-shared.nq           — N-Quads for PROV-O shared nodes (appended, deduped)
25	            <stem>_meta.parquet      — per-sector ProvidedCHO metadata
26	            prov.duckdb              — updated prov_entities table
27	            concept_labels.duckdb    — updated concept_labels table
28	            agent_labels.duckdb      — updated agent_labels table
29	Deps:       stdlib + duckdb + pyarrow
30	Assumes:    SQLite has objs(uid VARCHAR, bufgz BLOB); JSON follows DDB EDM cortex format.
31	"""
32	
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	import argparse
36	import csv
37	import fcntl
38	import gzip
39	import json
40	import logging
41	import re
42	import sqlite3
43	from pathlib import Path
44	
45	import pyarrow as pa
46	import pyarrow.parquet as pq
47	
48	from .constants import GRAPH_PROV, DDB_ITEM_BASE
49	from .emitters import emit_prov_triples
50	from .utils import coerce_list, normalize_date
51	
52	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
53	# Parquet schema
54	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
55	
56	AGENT_STRUCT = pa.struct([
57	    pa.field("name",       pa.string()),
58	    pa.field("type",       pa.string()),
59	    pa.field("is_ext_uri", pa.bool_()),
60	])
61	
62	DATE_STRUCT = pa.struct([
63	    pa.field("value", pa.string()),
64	    pa.field("begin", pa.string()),
65	    pa.field("end",   pa.string()),
66	    pa.field("type",  pa.string()),
67	])
68	
69	CONCEPT_STRUCT = pa.struct([
70	    pa.field("name",       pa.string()),
71	    pa.field("is_ext_uri", pa.bool_()),
72	])
73	
74	PARQUET_SCHEMA = pa.schema([
75	    ("obj_id",         pa.string()),
76	    ("title",          pa.string()),
77	    ("lang_title",     pa.string()),
78	    ("lang_obj",       pa.string()),
79	    ("description",    pa.list_(pa.string())),
80	    ("provider_id",    pa.string()),
81	    ("dataset_id",     pa.string()),
82	    ("dc_type",        pa.list_(CONCEPT_STRUCT)),
83	    ("agents",         pa.list_(AGENT_STRUCT)),
84	    ("dates",          pa.list_(DATE_STRUCT)),
85	    ("dc_subject",     pa.list_(CONCEPT_STRUCT)),
86	    ("hierarchy_type", pa.int16()),
87	    ("mediatype",      pa.int16()),
88	    ("sector",         pa.int16()),
89	    ("is_part_of",     pa.bool_()),
90	])
91	
92	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
93	# Constants
94	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
95	
96	_DDB_VOCNET = "http://ddb.vocnet.org/"
97	_BARE_ID_RE = re.compile(r'^[A-Z0-9]{32}$')
98	_QUALIFIER_RE = re.compile(r'\([^)]*\)')
99	
100	LIDO_CREATED: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
101	    "http://terminology.lido-schema.org/lido00012",
102	    "http://terminology.lido-schema.org/eventType/creation",
103	    "http://terminology.lido-schema.org/lido00007",
104	})
105	LIDO_ISSUED: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
106	    "http://terminology.lido-schema.org/lido00228",
107	    "http://terminology.lido-schema.org/eventType/publication",
108	})
109	
110	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
111	# Helpers
112	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
113	
114	def _is_ext_uri(resource: str) -> bool:
115	    """True if resource is an HTTP URI that is not a bare 32-char DDB ID."""
116	    return bool(resource) and resource.startswith("http") and not _BARE_ID_RE.match(resource)
117	
118	
119	def _scalar_str(val) -> str | None:
120	    """Return the first non-empty string value from a cortex field."""
121	    if val is None:
122	        return None
123	    if isinstance(val, str):
124	        return val or None
125	    if isinstance(val, dict):
126	        return val.get("$") or None
127	    if isinstance(val, list):
128	        for item in val:
129	            result = _scalar_str(item)
130	            if result:
131	                return result
132	    return None
133	
134	
135	def _scalar_values(val) -> list[str]:
136	    """Extract all string values from a cortex field (scalar / dict / list)."""
137	    if val is None:
138	        return []
139	    if isinstance(val, str):
140	        return [val] if val else []
141	    if isinstance(val, dict):
142	        v = val.get("$") or ""
143	        return [v] if v else []
144	    if isinstance(val, list):
145	        out: list[str] = []
146	        for item in val:
147	            out.extend(_scalar_values(item))
148	        return out
149	    return []
150	
151	
152	def _parse_dc_date(raw: str) -> str | None:
153	    """Strip parenthesized qualifiers and brackets, return first normalized date value."""
154	    if not raw:
155	        return None
156	    clean = _QUALIFIER_RE.sub("", raw).strip().strip("[]").strip()
157	    if not clean:
158	        return None
159	    vals = normalize_date(clean)
160	    return vals[0] if vals else None
161	
162	
163	def _build_concept_labels(rdf: dict) -> dict[str, str]:
164	    """Build URI → prefLabel map from all Concept blocks in the record."""
165	    result: dict[str, str] = {}
166	    for c in coerce_list(rdf.get("Concept")):
167	        if not isinstance(c, dict):
168	            continue
169	        about = (c.get("about") or "").strip()
170	        if not about:
171	            continue
172	        for item in coerce_list(c.get("prefLabel")):
173	            if isinstance(item, dict):
174	                label = item.get("$") or ""
175	            elif isinstance(item, str):
176	                label = item
177	            else:
178	                continue
179	            if label:
180	                result[about] = label
181	                break
182	    return result
183	
184	
185	def _build_agent_name_map(rdf: dict) -> dict[str, str]:
186	    """Build URI → name map from Agent and Organization entities in the record."""
187	    result: dict[str, str] = {}
188	    for entity_type in ("Agent", "Organization"):
189	        for ent in coerce_list(rdf.get(entity_type)):
190	            if not isinstance(ent, dict):
191	                continue
192	            about = (ent.get("about") or "").strip()
193	            if not about:
194	                continue
195	            for key in ("prefLabel", "name", "altLabel"):
196	                for item in coerce_list(ent.get(key)):
197	                    if isinstance(item, dict):
198	                        label = item.get("$") or ""
199	                    elif isinstance(item, str):
200	                        label = item
201	                    else:
202	                        continue
203	                    if label:
204	                        result[about] = label
205	                        break
206	                if about in result:
207	                    break
208	    return result
209	
210	
211	def _agent_field(
212	    field_val,
213	    agent_type: str,
214	    agent_labels_pending: dict[str, str | None],
215	) -> list[dict]:
216	    """Convert creator/contributor/publisher field to {name, type, is_ext_uri} structs."""
217	    out: list[dict] = []
218	    for v in coerce_list(field_val):
219	        if isinstance(v, dict):
220	            name = v.get("$") or ""
221	            res  = v.get("resource") or ""
222	            ext  = _is_ext_uri(res)
223	            if ext:
224	                agent_labels_pending.setdefault(res, name or None)
225	            if name or res:
226	                out.append({"name": name, "type": agent_type, "is_ext_uri": ext})
227	        elif isinstance(v, str) and v:
228	            out.append({"name": v, "type": agent_type, "is_ext_uri": False})
229	    return out
230	
231	
232	def _lido_agents(
233	    cho: dict,
234	    rdf: dict,
235	    lido_labels: dict[str, str],
236	    agent_name_map: dict[str, str],
237	) -> list[dict]:
238	    """Extract LIDO participants from hasMet → Event.P11_had_participant."""
239	    out: list[dict] = []
240	    event_map = {
241	        e["about"]: e for e in coerce_list(rdf.get("Event"))
242	        if isinstance(e, dict) and e.get("about")
243	    }
244	    for hm in coerce_list(cho.get("hasMet")):
245	        if not isinstance(hm, dict):
246	            continue
247	        ev_ref = hm.get("resource")
248	        if not ev_ref:
249	            continue
250	        ev = event_map.get(ev_ref, {})
251	        ht = ev.get("hasType") or {}
252	        ht_uri = ht.get("resource") if isinstance(ht, dict) else None
253	        event_label = lido_labels.get(ht_uri or "", "unknown_event")
254	        for p in coerce_list(ev.get("P11_had_participant")):
255	            if not isinstance(p, dict):
256	                continue
257	            res  = p.get("resource") or ""
258	            name = p.get("$") or agent_name_map.get(res, "")
259	            ext  = _is_ext_uri(res)
260	            out.append({"name": name, "type": event_label, "is_ext_uri": ext})
261	    return out
262	
263	
264	def _timespan_to_date(ts: dict, date_type: str) -> dict | None:
265	    """Convert a TimeSpan dict to a {value, begin, end, type} struct."""
266	    ts_begin = (_scalar_values(ts.get("begin")) or [None])[0]
267	    ts_end   = (_scalar_values(ts.get("end"))   or [None])[0]
268	    if ts_begin is None and ts_end is None:
269	        pref = (_scalar_values(ts.get("prefLabel")) or [None])[0]
270	        return {"value": pref, "begin": None, "end": None, "type": date_type} if pref else None
271	    if ts_begin and ts_end and ts_begin == ts_end:
272	        return {"value": ts_begin, "begin": None, "end": None, "type": date_type}
273	    return {"value": None, "begin": ts_begin, "end": ts_end, "type": date_type}
274	
275	
276	def _lido_dates(
277	    events: list,
278	    ts_map: dict[str, dict],
279	    lido_uris: frozenset[str],
280	    date_type: str,
281	) -> list[dict]:
282	    """Extract date structs from LIDO events matching lido_uris via TimeSpan lookup."""
283	    out: list[dict] = []
284	    for ev in events:
285	        if not isinstance(ev, dict):
286	            continue
287	        ht = ev.get("hasType") or {}
288	        ht_uri = ht.get("resource") if isinstance(ht, dict) else None
289	        if ht_uri not in lido_uris:
290	            continue
291	        oc = ev.get("occuredAt") or ev.get("occurredAt")
292	        if not oc:
293	            continue
294	        ts_ref = oc.get("resource") if isinstance(oc, dict) else oc
295	        ts = ts_map.get(ts_ref, {}) if isinstance(ts_ref, str) else {}
296	        d = _timespan_to_date(ts, date_type)
297	        if d:
298	            out.append(d)
299	    return out
300	
301	
302	def _resolve_subject_items(
303	    field_val,
304	    concept_labels: dict[str, str],
305	    concept_labels_pending: dict[str, str | None],
306	) -> list[dict]:
307	    """Convert subject field values to {name, is_ext_uri} structs."""
308	    out: list[dict] = []
309	    for v in coerce_list(field_val):
310	        if isinstance(v, dict):
311	            lit = v.get("$") or ""
312	            res = v.get("resource") or ""
313	            if lit:
314	                out.append({"name": lit, "is_ext_uri": False})
315	            elif res and _is_ext_uri(res) and not res.startswith(_DDB_VOCNET):
316	                label = concept_labels.get(res)
317	                if label:
318	                    out.append({"name": label, "is_ext_uri": True})
319	                else:
320	                    concept_labels_pending.setdefault(res, None)
321	        elif isinstance(v, str) and v and not _BARE_ID_RE.match(v):
322	            out.append({"name": v, "is_ext_uri": False})
323	    return out
324	
325	
326	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
327	# Record extraction
328	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
329	
330	def extract_record(
331	    data: dict,
332	    lido_labels: dict[str, str],
333	) -> tuple[dict, dict[str, str | None], dict[str, str | None]]:
334	    """Extract a Parquet row + pending label DB inserts from one record.
335	
336	    Returns:
337	        (parquet_row, concept_labels_pending, agent_labels_pending)
338	    """
339	    props     = data.get("properties") or {}
340	    prov_info = data.get("provider-info") or {}
341	    rdf       = (data.get("edm") or {}).get("RDF") or {}
342	    cho       = rdf.get("ProvidedCHO") or {}
343	    if isinstance(cho, list):
344	        cho = cho[0] if cho else {}
345	
346	    events    = coerce_list(rdf.get("Event"))
347	    timespans = coerce_list(rdf.get("TimeSpan"))
348	    ts_map    = {
349	        t["about"]: t for t in timespans
350	        if isinstance(t, dict) and t.get("about")
351	    }
352	
353	    concept_labels        = _build_concept_labels(rdf)
354	    agent_name_map        = _build_agent_name_map(rdf)
355	    concept_labels_pending: dict[str, str | None] = {}
356	    agent_labels_pending:   dict[str, str | None] = {}
357	
358	    # -- title + lang_title --
359	    title_raw  = cho.get("title")
360	    title_str  = ""
361	    lang_title = ""
362	    if isinstance(title_raw, list):
363	        title_raw = title_raw[0] if title_raw else None
364	    if isinstance(title_raw, dict):
365	        title_str  = title_raw.get("$") or ""
366	        lang_title = title_raw.get("lang") or ""
367	    elif isinstance(title_raw, str):
368	        title_str = title_raw
369	
370	    # -- lang_obj --
371	    lang_obj = _scalar_str(cho.get("language") or cho.get("dcTermsLanguage")) or ""
372	
373	    # -- description --
374	    description = _scalar_values(cho.get("description"))
375	
376	    # -- dc_type structs --
377	    dc_type: list[dict] = []
378	    for v in coerce_list(cho.get("dcType")):
379	        if isinstance(v, dict):
380	            lit = v.get("$") or ""
381	            res = v.get("resource") or ""
382	            if lit:
383	                dc_type.append({"name": lit, "is_ext_uri": False})
384	            elif res and not res.startswith(_DDB_VOCNET) and _is_ext_uri(res):
385	                label = concept_labels.get(res)
386	                if label:
387	                    dc_type.append({"name": label, "is_ext_uri": True})
388	                else:
389	                    concept_labels_pending.setdefault(res, None)
390	        elif isinstance(v, str) and v:
391	            dc_type.append({"name": v, "is_ext_uri": False})
392	
393	    # -- dc_subject structs (three field variants) --
394	    dc_subj: list[dict] = []
395	    for key in ("dcSubject", "dcTermsSubject", "dcTermSubject"):
396	        dc_subj.extend(
397	            _resolve_subject_items(cho.get(key), concept_labels, concept_labels_pending)
398	        )
399	
400	    # -- agents unified --
401	    agents: list[dict] = (
402	        _agent_field(cho.get("creator"),     "creation",    agent_labels_pending)
403	        + _agent_field(cho.get("publisher"), "publication", agent_labels_pending)
404	        + _agent_field(cho.get("contributor"), "contribution", agent_labels_pending)
405	        + _lido_agents(cho, rdf, lido_labels, agent_name_map)
406	    )
407	
408	    # -- dates unified --
409	    dates: list[dict] = []
410	    dc_date_raw = _scalar_str(cho.get("date"))
411	    if dc_date_raw:
412	        normed = _parse_dc_date(dc_date_raw)
413	        if normed:
414	            dates.append({"value": normed, "begin": None, "end": None, "type": "unknown_event"})
415	    for val in _scalar_values(cho.get("created")):
416	        dates.append({"value": val, "begin": None, "end": None, "type": "creation"})
417	    for val in _scalar_values(cho.get("issued")):
418	        dates.append({"value": val, "begin": None, "end": None, "type": "publication"})
419	    dates.extend(_lido_dates(events, ts_map, LIDO_CREATED, "creation"))
420	    dates.extend(_lido_dates(events, ts_map, LIDO_ISSUED,  "publication"))
421	
422	    # -- mediatype / sector (int16) --
423	    mediatype: int | None = None
424	    sector:    int | None = None
425	    for c in coerce_list(rdf.get("Concept")):
426	        if not isinstance(c, dict):
427	            continue
428	        about = c.get("about") or ""
429	        if mediatype is None and "/medientyp/mt" in about:
430	            try:
431	                mediatype = int(about.rsplit("/mt", 1)[-1])
432	            except ValueError:
433	                pass
434	        if sector is None and "/sparte/sparte" in about:
435	            try:
436	                sector = int(about.rsplit("/sparte", 1)[-1])
437	            except ValueError:
438	                pass
439	
440	    # -- hierarchy_type (int16) --
441	    raw_ht = (_scalar_values(cho.get("hierarchyType")) or [None])[0]
442	    htype: int | None = None
443	    if raw_ht:
444	        try:
445	            htype = int(raw_ht.replace("htype_", ""))
446	        except ValueError:
447	            pass
448	
449	    row = {
450	        "obj_id":         props.get("item-id", ""),
451	        "title":          title_str,
452	        "lang_title":     lang_title,
453	        "lang_obj":       lang_obj,
454	        "description":    description,
455	        "provider_id":    prov_info.get("provider-ddb-id", ""),
456	        "dataset_id":     props.get("dataset-id", ""),
457	        "dc_type":        dc_type,
458	        "agents":         agents,
459	        "dates":          dates,
460	        "dc_subject":     dc_subj,
461	        "hierarchy_type": htype,
462	        "mediatype":      mediatype,
463	        "sector":         sector,
464	        "is_part_of":     bool(cho.get("isPartOf")),
465	    }
466	    return row, concept_labels_pending, agent_labels_pending
467	
468	
469	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
470	# Locked DB writes
471	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
472	
473	def _write_prov_db(
474	    prov_db_path: Path,
475	    prov_out_path: Path,
476	    new_entities: dict[str, str],
477	    new_prov_lines: list[str],
478	) -> None:
479	    """Lock-protected write to prov.duckdb + idempotent append to prov-shared.nq."""
480	    import duckdb
481	    with open(str(prov_db_path) + ".lock", "a") as lf:
482	        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)
483	        try:
484	            conn = duckdb.connect(str(prov_db_path))
485	            conn.execute("""
486	                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS prov_entities (
487	                    uri         VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
488	                    entity_type VARCHAR NOT NULL
489	                )
490	            """)
491	            uris = list(new_entities.keys())
492	            if uris:
493	                ph = ",".join("?" * len(uris))
494	                existing = {r[0] for r in conn.execute(
495	                    f"SELECT uri FROM prov_entities WHERE uri IN ({ph})", uris
496	                ).fetchall()}
497	            else:
498	                existing = set()
499	            truly_new = {u: t for u, t in new_entities.items() if u not in existing}
500	            if truly_new:
501	                conn.executemany(
502	                    "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO prov_entities VALUES (?, ?)",
503	                    list(truly_new.items()),
504	                )
505	            conn.close()
506	            if truly_new:
507	                truly_new_nts = {f"<{u}>" for u in truly_new}
508	                with open(prov_out_path, "a", encoding="utf-8") as pf:
509	                    for line in new_prov_lines:
510	                        subj = line.split(" ", 1)[0]
511	                        if subj in truly_new_nts:
512	                            pf.write(line + "\n")
513	        finally:
514	            fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)
515	
516	
517	def _write_labels_db(
518	    db_path: Path,
519	    table: str,
520	    pending: dict[str, str | None],
521	) -> None:
522	    """Lock-protected INSERT OR IGNORE of (uri, label) pairs into a labels DuckDB."""
523	    if not pending:
524	        return
525	    import duckdb
526	    with open(str(db_path) + ".lock", "a") as lf:
527	        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)
528	        try:
529	            conn = duckdb.connect(str(db_path))
530	            conn.execute(f"""
531	                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS {table} (
532	                    uri   VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
533	                    label VARCHAR
534	                )
535	            """)
536	            conn.executemany(
537	                f"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO {table} VALUES (?, ?)",
538	                list(pending.items()),
539	            )
540	            conn.close()
541	        finally:
542	            fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)
543	
544	
545	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
546	# Main
547	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
548	
549	def main() -> None:
550	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
551	        description="Pass 1 prescan: PROV-O shared nodes + label DBs + Parquet"
552	    )
553	    parser.add_argument("--db",                type=Path, required=True,
554	                        help="Sector SQLite file (objs table, bufgz column)")
555	    parser.add_argument("--prov-db",           type=Path, required=True, dest="prov_db",
556	                        help="Shared prov.duckdb (prov_entities table); created if absent")
557	    parser.add_argument("--concept-labels-db", type=Path, required=True, dest="concept_labels_db",
558	                        help="Shared concept_labels.duckdb; created if absent")
559	    parser.add_argument("--agent-labels-db",   type=Path, required=True, dest="agent_labels_db",
560	                        help="Shared agent_labels.duckdb; created if absent")
561	    parser.add_argument("--lido",              type=Path, required=True,
562	                        help="lido_event_types.csv (resource → label)")
563	    parser.add_argument("--prov-out",          type=Path, required=True, dest="prov_out",
564	                        help="prov-shared.nq output (appended, deduped)")
565	    parser.add_argument("--parquet-out",       type=Path, required=True, dest="parquet_out",
566	                        help="Per-sector Parquet output")
567	    args = parser.parse_args()
568	
569	    logging.basicConfig(
570	        level=logging.INFO,
571	        format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)-8s %(message)s",
572	        datefmt="%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
573	    )
574	    log = logging.getLogger(__name__)
575	
576	    # Ensure all output parent directories exist and parquet path is writable
577	    for p in (args.prov_db, args.concept_labels_db, args.agent_labels_db,
578	              args.prov_out, args.parquet_out):
579	        p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
580	    args.parquet_out.touch()
581	
582	    # Load LIDO event type labels
583	    lido_labels: dict[str, str] = {}
584	    with open(args.lido, encoding="utf-8", newline="") as f:
585	        for row in csv.DictReader(f):
586	            lido_labels[row["resource"]] = row["label"]
587	    log.info("Loaded %d LIDO labels from %s", len(lido_labels), args.lido.name)
588	
589	    PARQUET_FLUSH = 500_000  # rows per flush
590	
591	    # Scan accumulators
592	    local_emitted: dict[str, str]             = {}
593	    new_prov_lines: list[str]                = []
594	    concept_labels_all: dict[str, str | None] = {}
595	    agent_labels_all:   dict[str, str | None] = {}
596	    parquet_buf: list[dict]                  = []
597	    parquet_writer: pq.ParquetWriter | None  = None
598	    parquet_total = 0
599	
600	    def _flush_parquet() -> None:
601	        nonlocal parquet_writer, parquet_total
602	        if not parquet_buf:
603	            return
604	        arrays = {col: [r.get(col) for r in parquet_buf] for col in PARQUET_SCHEMA.names}
605	        table  = pa.table(arrays, schema=PARQUET_SCHEMA)
606	        if parquet_writer is None:
607	            parquet_writer = pq.ParquetWriter(str(args.parquet_out), PARQUET_SCHEMA)
608	        parquet_writer.write_table(table)
609	        parquet_total += len(parquet_buf)
610	        parquet_buf.clear()
611	
612	    db_conn = sqlite3.connect(str(args.db))
613	    cur = db_conn.cursor()
614	    cur.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM objs")
615	    total = cur.fetchone()[0]
616	    log.info("Scanning %s (%d records)", args.db.name, total)
617	
618	    cur.execute("SELECT uid, bufgz FROM objs WHERE bufgz IS NOT NULL ORDER BY rowid")
619	    processed = errors = 0
620	
621	    for uid, blob in cur:
622	        try:
623	            data = json.loads(gzip.decompress(blob))
624	        except Exception as exc:
625	            log.warning("uid=%s decompress/parse error: %s", uid, exc)
626	            errors += 1
627	            continue
628	
629	        props  = data.get("properties") or {}
630	        obj_id = props.get("item-id") or uid
631	        cho_uri = DDB_ITEM_BASE + obj_id
632	        cho_subj_nt = f"<{cho_uri}> "
633	
634	        # PROV-O: emit shared-node descriptor lines (skip per-CHO lines)
635	        all_prov_lines = emit_prov_triples(data, cho_uri, GRAPH_PROV, local_emitted)
636	        for line in all_prov_lines:
637	            if not line.startswith(cho_subj_nt):
638	                new_prov_lines.append(line)
639	
640	        # Parquet row + pending label dicts
641	        try:
642	            row, cpending, apending = extract_record(data, lido_labels)
643	            parquet_buf.append(row)
644	            for k, v in cpending.items():
645	                concept_labels_all.setdefault(k, v)
646	            for k, v in apending.items():
647	                agent_labels_all.setdefault(k, v)
648	        except Exception as exc:
649	            log.warning("uid=%s extract error: %s", uid, exc)
650	            errors += 1
651	
652	        processed += 1
653	        if processed % 100_000 == 0:
654	            log.info("  %d / %d", processed, total)
655	        if len(parquet_buf) >= PARQUET_FLUSH:
656	            _flush_parquet()
657	            log.info("  Parquet flush: %d rows written so far", parquet_total)
658	
659	    db_conn.close()
660	    _flush_parquet()
661	    if parquet_writer:
662	        parquet_writer.close()
663	    log.info("Scan complete: %d processed, %d errors, %d Parquet rows",
664	             processed, errors, parquet_total)
665	
666	    # After scan: local_emitted contains all PROV-O shared node URIs found
667	    new_entities = dict(local_emitted)
668	
669	    # Lock-protected writes
670	    log.info("Writing prov.duckdb + prov-shared.nq (%d shared nodes)", len(new_entities))
671	    _write_prov_db(args.prov_db, args.prov_out, new_entities, new_prov_lines)
672	
673	    log.info("Writing concept_labels.duckdb (%d URIs)", len(concept_labels_all))
674	    _write_labels_db(args.concept_labels_db, "concept_labels", concept_labels_all)
675	
676	    log.info("Writing agent_labels.duckdb (%d URIs)", len(agent_labels_all))
677	    _write_labels_db(args.agent_labels_db, "agent_labels", agent_labels_all)
678	
679	    log.info("Done.")
680	
681	
682	if __name__ == "__main__":
683	    main()
684	
```

### Assistant *(2026-05-20T14:12:30.879Z)*

Found the design flaw. In `_write_prov_db` (line 499–512): it only writes to `prov-shared.nq` for entities that are `truly_new` — not already in prov.duckdb. If prescan was re-run after prov.duckdb was already populated, `truly_new` is empty for every sector and `prov-shared.nq` is never opened, let alone created.

The root cause: the N-Quads content is held in memory during the scan and discarded after writing; prov.duckdb only stores URIs + entity types. There's no way to regenerate the NQ from the DB after the fact.

The fix: **store the N-Quads lines in prov.duckdb alongside the URI**. Then `prov-shared.nq` can be (re)generated from the DB at any time. I'll add a `nq_lines` column:

---

### Assistant *(2026-05-20T14:12:50.562Z)*

**Tool call:** `Edit`
```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust/scripts/transform/prescan.py",
  "old_string": "def _write_prov_db(\n    prov_db_path: Path,\n    prov_out_path: Path,\n    new_entities: dict[str, str],\n    new_prov_lines: list[str],\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Lock-protected write to prov.duckdb + idempotent append to prov-shared.nq.\"\"\"\n    import duckdb\n    with open(str(prov_db_path) + \".lock\", \"a\") as lf:\n        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)\n        try:\n            conn = duckdb.connect(str(prov_db_path))\n            conn.execute(\"\"\"\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS prov_entities (\n                    uri         VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,\n                    entity_type VARCHAR NOT NULL\n                )\n            \"\"\")\n            uris = list(new_entities.keys())\n            if uris:\n                ph = \",\".join(\"?\" * len(uris))\n                existing = {r[0] for r in conn.execute(\n                    f\"SELECT uri FROM prov_entities WHERE uri IN ({ph})\", uris\n                ).fetchall()}\n            else:\n                existing = set()\n            truly_new = {u: t for u, t in new_entities.items() if u not in existing}\n            if truly_new:\n                conn.executemany(\n                    \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO prov_entities VALUES (?, ?)\",\n                    list(truly_new.items()),\n                )\n            conn.close()\n            if truly_new:\n                truly_new_nts = {f\"<{u}>\" for u in truly_new}\n                with open(prov_out_path, \"a\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as pf:\n                    for line in new_prov_lines:\n                        subj = line.split(\" \", 1)[0]\n                        if subj in truly_new_nts:\n                            pf.write(line + \"\\n\")\n        finally:\n            fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)",
  "new_string": "def _write_prov_db(\n    prov_db_path: Path,\n    prov_out_path: Path,\n    new_entities: dict[str, str],\n    new_prov_lines: list[str],\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Lock-protected write to prov.duckdb + idempotent append to prov-shared.nq.\n\n    prov.duckdb stores (uri, entity_type, nq_lines) so prov-shared.nq can always\n    be regenerated via: SELECT nq_lines FROM prov_entities (see regenerate_prov_nq).\n    \"\"\"\n    import duckdb\n\n    # Build per-URI NQ lines map from new_prov_lines\n    uri_to_lines: dict[str, list[str]] = {}\n    for uri in new_entities:\n        prefix = f\"<{uri}> \"\n        uri_to_lines[uri] = [l for l in new_prov_lines if l.startswith(prefix)]\n\n    with open(str(prov_db_path) + \".lock\", \"a\") as lf:\n        fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_EX)\n        try:\n            conn = duckdb.connect(str(prov_db_path))\n            conn.execute(\"\"\"\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS prov_entities (\n                    uri         VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,\n                    entity_type VARCHAR NOT NULL,\n                    nq_lines    VARCHAR NOT NULL DEFAULT ''\n                )\n            \"\"\")\n            # Migrate existing tables that lack nq_lines\n            cols = {r[0] for r in conn.execute(\n                \"SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns \"\n                \"WHERE table_name='prov_entities'\"\n            ).fetchall()}\n            if \"nq_lines\" not in cols:\n                conn.execute(\"ALTER TABLE prov_entities ADD COLUMN nq_lines VARCHAR NOT NULL DEFAULT ''\")\n\n            uris = list(new_entities.keys())\n            if uris:\n                ph = \",\".join(\"?\" * len(uris))\n                existing = {r[0] for r in conn.execute(\n                    f\"SELECT uri FROM prov_entities WHERE uri IN ({ph})\", uris\n                ).fetchall()}\n            else:\n                existing = set()\n            truly_new = {u: t for u, t in new_entities.items() if u not in existing}\n            if truly_new:\n                rows = [\n                    (u, t, \"\\n\".join(uri_to_lines.get(u, [])))\n                    for u, t in truly_new.items()\n                ]\n                conn.executemany(\n                    \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO prov_entities VALUES (?, ?, ?)\",\n                    rows,\n                )\n            conn.close()\n            # Write NQ lines for all newly inserted entities\n            if truly_new:\n                truly_new_nts = {f\"<{u}>\" for u in truly_new}\n                with open(prov_out_path, \"a\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as pf:\n                    for line in new_prov_lines:\n                        subj = line.split(\" \", 1)[0]\n                        if subj in truly_new_nts:\n                            pf.write(line + \"\\n\")\n        finally:\n            fcntl.flock(lf, fcntl.LOCK_UN)\n\n\ndef regenerate_prov_nq(prov_db_path: Path, prov_out_path: Path) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Regenerate prov-shared.nq from nq_lines stored in prov.duckdb.\n\n    Overwrites prov_out_path. Returns number of lines written.\n    Safe to call after prescan when prov-shared.nq is missing or incomplete.\n    \"\"\"\n    import duckdb\n    conn = duckdb.connect(str(prov_db_path), read_only=True)\n    rows = conn.execute(\"SELECT nq_lines FROM prov_entities WHERE nq_lines != ''\").fetchall()\n    conn.close()\n    prov_out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    count = 0\n    with open(prov_out_path, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        for (lines_str,) in rows:\n            for line in lines_str.splitlines():\n                if line.strip():\n                    f.write(line + \"\\n\")\n                    count += 1\n    return count"
}
```

### Assistant *(2026-05-20T14:20:01.728Z)*

As expected — the teach03 prov.duckdb predates this change and has no `nq_lines`. So `regen` won't work on it; it'll return 0 lines.

The only path forward for teach03 is to **re-run prescan on all sectors with the new code and a fresh prov.duckdb**. The new code stores `nq_lines` in the DB as it scans, so prov-shared.nq gets written during the scan and can be regenerated any time afterwards via `regen`.

After syncing the updated prescan.py to teach03:

```bash
# wipe old shared DBs (they lack nq_lines)
rm -f ~/gemea/output/20260519/prov.duckdb
rm -f ~/gemea/output/20260519/prov.duckdb.lock

# re-run prescan for all 7 sectors in parallel
SCRIPTS=~/goethe-faust/scripts
CFG=~/goethe-faust/output/config
OUT=~/gemea/output/20260519

for n in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7; do
  (
    cd "$SCRIPTS"
    ../.venv/bin/python3 -m transform.prescan \
      --db                /data/gemea/sqlite/s${n}.sqlite \
      --prov-db           $OUT/prov.duckdb \
      --concept-labels-db $OUT/concept_labels.duckdb \
      --agent-labels-db   $OUT/agent_labels.duckdb \
      --lido              $CFG/lido_event_types.csv \
      --prov-out          $OUT/prov-shared.nq \
      --parquet-out       $OUT/parquet/s${n}_meta.parquet
  ) &
done
wait
```

After that, if prov-shared.nq is ever lost, it can be regenerated without re-scanning:

```bash
cd ~/goethe-faust/scripts
../.venv/bin/python3 -m transform.prescan regen \
  --prov-db  ~/gemea/output/20260519/prov.duckdb \
  --prov-out ~/gemea/output/20260519/prov-shared.nq
```
